tips.description: Mario made his debut in the arcade game Donkey Kong. He wore a blue shirt and red overalls—the opposite of his current outfit.
When you think of Mario, you might think of his signature phrases like "Mamma Mia!" and "It's-a me, Mario!" These classic Mario phrases were first uttered by Mario in Super Mario 64.
The Super Mario series has released 35 titles, appeared on at least 15 systems, and it's sold more than 340 million copies worldwide!
The title Super Mario Bros. 2 refers to different games in the West and in Japan, but both games introduced Luigi's ability to jump higher than Mario!
When Mario first appeared in the arcade version of Donkey Kong, he was known as Jumpman. The kidnapped woman was called "Lady," but she was later given the name Pauline.
In Punch-Out!!, released on the NES in 1987, the referee was none other than Mario! His outfit was a bit different, but he still wore his trademark cap.
A device in Super Mario Sunshine made by Gadd Science, Inc. It's a handy partner that can defeat enemies with water or change abilities by changing the nozzle.

tips.name: Builder Mario
tips.description: Select the seventh color variation to play as Builder Mario from Super Mario Maker.

tips.name: Wedding
tips.description: Select the eighth color variation to play as Wedding Mario from Super Mario Odyssey.

tips.name: Cape's Effect
tips.description: This move has the effect of turning your opponents around. This can really mess them up if they're trying to get back onto the stage!

extra_info.name: Dodging with Cape
extra_info.description: Use this in midair to slow down Mario's falling speed for a moment. In particular, the first time you use it, he'll float up a bit—great for dodging!

extra_info.name: Reversing Controls with Cape
extra_info.description: Mario's Cape doesn't just flip an opponent's body around. It also reverses an opponent's controls for a short period of time.

tips.name: Super Jump Punch
tips.description: Sometimes the coins that appear will become the regional coins from Super Mario Odyssey. When that happens, Mario's cap will turn into Cappy.
F.L.U.D.D.'s water is great for stopping fighters like Ness and Ike from recovering with their up specials. Try to keep it charged and ready!

tips.name: F.L.U.D.D.
tips.description: A blast of water is an effective way to disrupt your opponents. It doesn't hurt, but it can slide them around.

extra_info.name: Water from F.L.U.D.D.
extra_info.description: Tilt the control stick up or down to change the angle of the water spray. The more water you charge up, the more forcefully it will spray out.

tips.description: F.L.U.D.D. isn't just for pushing fighters—it can also push some items, like capsules and crates, and even deflect incoming Bob-ombs.

tips.name: Mario Finale
tips.description: Unleash a wave of fire that damages and pushes opponents. Hit opponents when they are close to deal big damage. It's perfect for stopping opponents from returning to the stage.

extra_info.name: Mario Finale Warning
extra_info.description: You'll slide backward little by little when releasing Mario Finale. Trigger it too close to an edge, or while on a narrow platform, and you might find yourself in midair!

tips.name: Lead Headbutt
tips.description: A powerful upward headbutt. Mario's head is invulnerable while performing it. Useful against opponents moving in to attack from above.

tips.name: Meteor Knuckle
tips.description: It's a slow attack, but Mario swings his fist down hard enough for a meteor smash as it begins.

tips.name: Airplane Swing
tips.description: When grabbing opponents and swinging them around, you can hit other fighters with the move as well. Use this to deal damage to an entire group of fighters.
Donkey Kong first wore his signature red necktie with the initials DK in Donkey Kong Country. In that game, he sets off on an adventure to recover his banana hoard that was stolen by King K. Rool.
Donkey Kong, the arcade classic, was released in 1981. In that game, the heroine known as Lady was kidnapped and Donkey Kong tried to stop Mario from rescuing her.

tips.name: Giant Punch
tips.description: You can tell this move is fully charged from the steam and the look on Donkey Kong's face. It's a powerful blow, but it leaves you wide open.

tips.name: Headbutt Effect
tips.description: Use this move to bury your opponents if they're on the ground or meteor smash them in midair.

extra_info.name: Headbutt's Traits
extra_info.description: Headbutt grants super armor at the beginning of the move, allowing you to fight through incoming attacks. It's also useful when returning to the stage, landing, and against opponents waiting for you to land.

tips.name: Spinning Kong Movement
tips.description: This move doesn't offer a lot of lift, but it can carry Donkey Kong a fair distance left or right.

extra_info.name: No Pushing during Spinning Kong
extra_info.description: When the move is activated, things that push fighters (like water) will have no effect on Donkey Kong. When used on the ground, you'll get super armor at the beginning of the move.

tips.name: Hand Slap
tips.description: When used on the ground, DK's Hand Slap affects a large area around him, but it won't hit airborne fighters. When used in midair, it has a meteor effect on opponents.

tips.name: Jungle Rush
tips.description: Repeatedly punch the opponent and finish off by launching them with an uppercut. The move won't activate if the first punch misses, but it's super powerful.

tips.name: Slouch Slap
tips.description: Hit an opponent that doesn't have much damage to make them fall over. Follow up with a dash attack or dash grab!

tips.name: Comboing with Kong Karry
tips.description: You'll toss opponents diagonally upward when throwing forward, toss them high when throwing backward, and launch them low when tossed down. Throwing them up is a great start to a combo.
Donkey Kong can pick up opponents and carry them around! Try to throw them before they shake free, though.
Donkey Kong can use his side special Headbutt and his down special Hand Slap to damage or even shatter his foes' shields.
Four of Donkey Kong's moves have a meteor effect—his forward air attack, down air attack, midair side special, and the second hit of his midair down special.
Link first appeared in The Legend of Zelda on the NES. Even back then, he had a wide inventory of items, including the Bow, Boomerang, and Bombs.

Link is the main protagonist of the series The Legend of Zelda. He has pointy ears and wields a sword and shield. In the Zelda games, Link uses items like bombs and arrows to defeat enemies and solve puzzles.
Despite being the hero, Link's name only appears in the title of five games. Can you name them? One might be trickier to remember than the others!

tips.name: Clothing "of the Wild"
tips.description: Link's second color variation is his Hero of the Wild armor, which dresses him in his traditional green garb. You can obtain this armor in Breath of the Wild by completing all of the shrines.

tips.name: Bow and Arrows
tips.description: The power and distance of the arrow increases as it's charged. Arrows can stick in the ground or walls, and if you pick one up and use this move again, you can fire off two arrows at once.

tips.name: Boomerang
tips.description: The Boomerang is the strongest the moment it's thrown. Flick the control stick quickly when you press the button to increase distance and power. If you don't catch the returning Boomerang, it will fly off.

tips.name: Remote Bomb Recovery
tips.description: If you are launched off the stage, you can use the Remote Bomb to blast yourself back toward the stage. It's very hard to pull off, but it might work as a last resort!

extra_info.name: Remote Bomb's Traits
extra_info.description: You can detonate whenever you want, but keep in mind that Link will be damaged by the explosion too. The bomb will explode after a certain period or when hit with a fire attack.

tips.name: Ancient Bow and Arrow
tips.description: Link shoots a powerful, blue glowing arrow that explodes on contact and launches opponents. The arrow will still explode if it hits a wall or the ground, and it can hit multiple opponents.

tips.name: Sword Slice
tips.description: If Link's damage is at 0% or he has full stamina in a stamina battle, he can shoot a sword beam by using his side smash attack.

extra_info.name: Double Sword Slice
extra_info.description: Press the button again to attack a second time. The second attack has more launching power.

tips.description: Link's down and up air attacks, Downward Thrust and Jump Thrust, are from Zelda II - The Adventure of Link. In that game, they're learned from town swordsmen.

tips.name: Jump Slash
tips.description: Link's dash attack strikes with almost as much force as a smash attack. The point of his sword is especially painful.
Link's Hylian Shield is capable of stopping even the most powerful of projectiles. This means you won't take any damage at all.
Link's Hylian Shield can block projectiles, but it won't block anything while you're in the middle of attacking.
Samus Aran made her debut in the NES title Metroid, released in North America in 1987. It is a science-fiction action-adventure game with a focus on exploration.

Equipped with many weapons, such as beams and missiles, Samus the galactic bounty hunter wears a Power Suit designed by the Chozo. She makes full use of the suit and techniques learned from the Chozo.
In the original Metroid, the ending would change slightly depending on your clear time. The better endings revealed the secret of Samus's identity!
The baby Metroid that appeared in Super Metroid imprinted on Samus before being left in the care of the Galactic Research Station at Ceres.

tips.name: Charge Shot
tips.description: This is devastating when fully charged. It can be unleashed instantly, and fired repeatedly when not charging. You can also charge it in midair.

tips.name: Missile
tips.description: Holding a direction will fire a homing missile, while quickly inputting a direction will fire a Super Missile!

extra_info.name: Super Missile
extra_info.description: Flick the control stick quickly as you press the button to fire a Super Missile. When fired, they pause briefly before rocketing ahead. Mix things up with homing missiles to make your foe's life tough.

tips.name: Screw Attack
tips.description: When recovering with the Screw Attack, you can't grab edges behind you until you've finished rising. Make sure to face the right way when you use it!

tips.name: Bomb
tips.description: Samus morphs into a ball and drops a bomb. You can use the explosion to gain a little height.

tips.name: Zero Laser
tips.description: Before the laser fires, it'll pull in nearby fighters a little bit. While the laser is firing, you can change the firing angle by tilting the control stick up or down.

tips.name: Straight and Cannon Hammer
tips.description: Foes can often put up their shields in between the first and second hits of this combo. It's safer to just hit with the first attack and then flee.

tips.name: Midair Grapple Beam
tips.description: Hop backward with a short hop, and then fire the Grapple Beam by using a grab in midair. Retreat while keeping your foes at bay!
Dark Samus made her debut in 2002 in Metroid Prime. She was born from Metroid Prime cells merged with Samus Aran's DNA. She is highly intelligent.

Dark Samus is a living Phazon entity with the radioactive substance Phazon as her energy supply. She has a wide range of abilities, like being able to teleport, cloak herself, and attack with an arm cannon.

tips.name: Charge Shot
tips.description: Devastating when fully charged, it can be unleashed instantly and fired repeatedly. You can also charge it in midair.

tips.name: Missile
tips.description: Holding a direction when you press the special-move button will fire a homing missile, and quickly inputting a direction as you press the special-move button will fire a Super Missile!

tips.name: Screw Attack
tips.description: When recovering with the Screw Attack, you can't grab edges behind you until you've finished rising. Make sure to face the right way when you use it!

tips.name: Bomb
tips.description: Dark Samus morphs into a ball and drops a bomb. You can use the explosion to gain a little height.

tips.name: Phazon Laser
tips.description: Unleash an enormous laser beam that goes through the stage. You can change the angle of the beam with the control stick. The beam will repeatedly hit opponents caught in its blast.

Yoshi has kept busy appearing in many titles since debuting in Super Mario World. Yoshi can flutter jump and has a long tongue that can gobble up apples and enemies. Yoshis can be a variety of colors.
Yoshi made his debut in Super Mario World, where Yoshi was trapped inside an egg by Bowser and freed by Mario.
Yoshi is the namesake of the NES title Yoshi. Yoshi later appeared in Yoshi's Cookie and Tetris Attack, both for the SNES.

tips.name: Egg Lay
tips.description: This move grabs opponents with Yoshi's tongue and traps them in an egg. They'll take less damage while trapped, but you can hit them all you like!

extra_info.name: Egg Lay off the Edge
extra_info.description: Opponents that have taken a lot of damage have more trouble escaping from eggs. This means you may be able to swallow them and lay them in an egg off the edge of the stage.

extra_info.name: Egg Form
extra_info.description: The more damage Yoshi's foes have accumulated, the longer they'll be stuck as an egg. They can escape faster by wiggling around.

tips.name: Egg Roll
tips.description: This move turns Yoshi into an egg that rolls along the ground. The faster it goes, the more powerful it'll be.

extra_info.name: Egg Roll in the Air
extra_info.description: You can start this move in the air, but you won't deal any damage until you hit the ground.

tips.name: Egg Throw's Power
tips.description: How hard Yoshi throws the egg depends on how long you hold the button down. Tap for a light throw or hold for a powerful one.

extra_info.name: Upward Eggs
extra_info.description: You can throw the egg straight up by inputting the direction Yoshi's facing away from just before throwing. Fake out your foes with this technique.

extra_info.name: A Little Eggstra Boost
extra_info.description: Unlike other fighters, Yoshi doesn't have a vertical move used mainly for recovering. However, Yoshi will flutter a little bit if this move is used in midair.

tips.name: Yoshi Bomb's Power
tips.description: The damage and launching power of the Yoshi Bomb are both a little higher when used on the ground.

extra_info.name: Break Shields with Yoshi Bomb
extra_info.description: Yoshi Bomb is great for breaking shields. Start off with multiple neutral attacks, and then use Yoshi Bomb to finish that shield off.

extra_info.name: Platforms and Yoshi Bomb
extra_info.description: At the start of the move, Yoshi will automatically pass through platforms. During the move, keep the stick tilted down to pass through all platforms until Yoshi lands.

tips.name: Yoshi Stampede
tips.description: A mighty Yoshi army stampedes through the stage. Fighters hit by the stampede will be launched. You can hit up to three fighters.

tips.name: Noggin Dunk
tips.description: Yoshi charges up for a moment and then delivers a powerful headbutt. If you hit opponents from above, they'll fly down with a meteor effect.

tips.name: Flutter Kick
tips.description: This attack is very powerful if the foe is directly beneath Yoshi. It can hit an opponent multiple times, so try your best to catch them right at the start of the attack for maximum damage!
Unlike other fighters, Yoshi's shield is an all-protecting egg. Even if the shield blocks attacks, it won't get smaller and Yoshi's body won't poke out from the shield.
Yoshi's midair jump won't stop if a foe attacks Yoshi in the middle of it. It's more difficult to withstand attacks with high launching power, though, so be careful if your damage is high!
Kirby made his debut in 1992 in Kirby's Dream Land for the Game Boy. The goal of his journey was to recover stolen food from King Dedede.
Kirby is a big eater who inhales any and everything. If he inhales an enemy, he can copy and use one of their abilities. He's also good at hovering by flapping his arms and puffing up like a balloon.
Kirby hasn't always been able to copy the abilities of the enemies he inhaled. That power debuted in his second title, Kirby's Adventure.
The original Kirby game, Kirby's Dream Land, was designed to be easy for beginners to play, but finishing it unlocks a more challenging mode.
Kirby Super Star on the SNES was the first Kirby game where two players could team up.

tips.name: Inhale
tips.description: If you press the attack button after sucking enemies in, you'll spit them back out. Use enemies to attack other enemies!

extra_info.name: Inhale Items
extra_info.description: In addition to enemies, Kirby can inhale some items. You'll recover a little damage this way, but sucking in explosives will hurt!

extra_info.name: Inhale's Projectiles
extra_info.description: After Kirby inhales a fighter, he'll spit them out as a star-shaped projectile. The heavier the fighter, the more attack power the projectile will have.

tips.name: Hammer Flip
tips.description: If fully charged, this move becomes very powerful, but Kirby will begin to take damage at full charge.

extra_info.name: Charged Up Hammer Flip
extra_info.description: When Hammer Flip is fully charged and used on the ground, Kirby is invincible for a brief moment at the start of his swing.

tips.name: Final Cutter
tips.description: Kirby slices up into the air and then slices straight down again. The shock wave that appears when you land can also damage your opponents.

tips.name: Canceling Stone
tips.description: While falling, you can cancel the attack by pressing the special-move button again. It's a perfect way to trick other players!

extra_info.name: Stone Transformation
extra_info.description: Kirby's form is different each time he uses this, but the move's effect is the same.

tips.name: Ultra Sword
tips.description: This Final Smash uses Ultra Sword to lay into opponents with a flurry of slashes. It cuts through terrain and covers a huge area, making it easy to catch nearby opponents.

tips.name: Squish Kick
tips.description: This attack can sometimes cause an opponent to take a tumble. Watch your opponent carefully. If they start to fall over, follow up with a grab or smash attack.

tips.name: Burning
tips.description: Kirby twists through the air like a flaming screw as he charges into an opponent. He's not a reckless fellow, so he'll automatically stop at edges instead of going over them.
Kirby gains the ability to use Aura Sphere from Lucario. No matter how high his damage gets, though, the attack won't get any stronger.
Kirby gets a Luma of his very own after inhaling Rosalina! It doesn't stay long enough to team up with him, though—it flies forward and then vanishes.
When Kirby inhales and copies a Mii Fighter, he'll wear a mask that looks like the Mii Fighter's face. Only the face itself is taken, though—headgear not included.
Kirby can steal a Copy Ability from another Kirby who's already copied someone else's ability. Confused yet?

If Kirby inhales another Kirby that doesn't have a Copy Ability, nothing will happen. However, if Kirby inhales another Kirby that does have a Copy Ability, Kirby can steal the inhaled Kirby's ability.
Taunt to discard your current Copy Ability. Find the most opportune moment to change your ability!
Kirby's light body lets him jump six times—more than most other fighters. If Kirby gets launched but not KO'd, you can almost bet he's coming back!
#1 is a tie between Jigglypuff, Meta Knight, and Kirby, who can all manage six. #4 is King Dedede with five, and Pit and Dark Pit are tied at #5 with four.
Fox made his debut in 1993, in the SNES game Star Fox. In his fighter craft, the Arwing, he fought to take down the evil scientist Andross.

Fox is the leader of the mercenary group Star Fox. He cares about his team and has a strong sense of justice. He heads to Venom to defeat Andross, the scientist responsible for the disappearance of his father.

tips.name: Blaster
tips.description: The Blaster fires quickly, and its shots go a long way, but enemies won't flinch when they're hit, so use this at a safe distance.

tips.name: Fox Illusion
tips.description: When you use this move on the ground, Fox will always stop if he reaches an edge. When used in the air, you need to be a little more careful.

extra_info.name: Using Fox Illusion Effectively
extra_info.description: A shielded opponent will stop Fox in his tracks, leaving him open to counterattack. Try to use this move when your opponent can't anticipate it.

tips.name: Reflector
tips.description: This move can reflect projectiles, and it also damages nearby opponents when activated.

extra_info.name: Reflector in the Air
extra_info.description: Using Reflector in midair slows down Fox's fall back to the ground. Using this when an enemy is about to attack from below can throw off their timing.

extra_info.name: Launching with Reflector
extra_info.description: A direct hit from Reflector will launch opponents at a low angle. You can use this to disrupt an opponent trying to make their way back to the stage.

tips.name: Team Star Fox
tips.description: The full force of Team Star Fox engages the targeted opponents. If Wolf is one of the opponents, Fox will deliver a special line.

tips.name: Tornado Kick
tips.description: Time your attack so that you land before hitting your opponent for a fifth time. They'll bounce off the ground, and you can make follow-up attacks.

tips.name: McCloud Flip
tips.description: A two-part attack where Fox kicks and swats opponents with his tail. It's great for doing damage and launching opponents.
Pikachu debuted in Pokémon Red and Pokémon Blue for Game Boy. Consider yourself a very lucky Trainer if you catch one in Viridian Forest.

Pikachu is an Electric-type Mouse Pokémon with a low appearance rate in Pokémon Red & Pokémon Blue. In Pokémon Yellow Version: Special Pikachu Edition, you could get one from Professor Oak at the start.
Pikachu Libre fights with a mask on. You can tell from the tail shape that this Pikachu is female.

tips.name: Thunder Jolt
tips.description: Pikachu's Thunder Jolt bounces along the surface of the stage. It can even attack foes who are hanging from edges.

tips.name: Skull Bash
tips.description: Execute this move like a smash attack, and it'll start off slightly charged already. This way, fully charging it doesn't take as long.

tips.name: Quick Attack
tips.description: Pikachu is vulnerable before landing after using Quick Attack in midair. However, if you direct the attack at the ground, the recovery time shortens a little.

extra_info.name: Controlling Quick Attack
extra_info.description: This move allows you to quickly attack while moving in the direction of your choice. You can move twice, but you can't continue moving in the same direction.

tips.name: Thunder
tips.description: The bolt of lightning has a meteor effect. Use it after throwing a foe upward for a powerful combo.

extra_info.name: Electric Discharge with Thunder
extra_info.description: Hit yourself with Thunder to cause a discharge that damages nearby foes. Using Thunder quickly after falling will pass through terrain, so it can be used below platforms in this way, too!

tips.name: Volt Tackle
tips.description: Pikachu wreathes itself in lightning and dashes all over the place. It's possible to escape Pikachu's wrath by moving outside of the bounds of the screen.

tips.name: Headbutt
tips.description: Press and hold the button to attack repeatedly. Be careful, though, because Pikachu shifts slightly as it attacks. You'll eventually end up out of attack range!

tips.name: Electric Flower
tips.description: Pikachu's spinning down smash draws in nearby opponents, launching them in the direction Pikachu is facing. This makes it a powerful move to use at the edge of a stage.
Pikachu's neutral air attack has short reach, but it hits in all directions. It's a handy move since it attacks quickly and easily allows for follow-up hits after you make contact.

tips.name: Glider
tips.description: This move can be triggered quickly and it hits multiple times, but it doesn't have a long reach, so you won't hit an opponent unless you're very close to them.

tips.name: Electric Screw
tips.description: This move has a meteor effect at the beginning. Pikachu has great recovery skills, so using it to dunk someone off the edge of the stage is a viable option.
Luigi made his debut in the 1983 arcade classic Mario Bros. At the time, he wore a white shirt and cap with green overalls.
In Mario games, Luigi usually appears as the character for player 2. He's Mario's younger twin brother. A little cowardly at times, but he'll also fight hard to help Mario.

Luigi's first big break as a main protagonist was in the 2001 release Luigi's Mansion. This is also the first appearance of the Poltergust. While Luigi can normally jump, he couldn't jump in that game.

tips.name: Fireball
tips.description: Luigi's green fireballs don't travel as far as his brother's, but they're slightly more powerful and they can bounce off of walls.

tips.name: Green Missile's Vulnerability
tips.description: This move can end with Luigi's head stuck in a wall. That leaves him vulnerable, so be careful!

extra_info.name: Overzealous Green Missile
extra_info.description: This move has a 1-in-10 chance to hit REALLY hard and fly REALLY far. Too far, in some cases.

extra_info.name: Exhausting Green Missile
extra_info.description: If you charge this for too long, he'll get worn out and give up. Let him catch his breath, and then try again.

tips.name: Super Jump Punch
tips.description: Get close to your enemy before using this. Hit them right at the start of the uppercut for maximum damage and launching power.

tips.name: Using Luigi Cyclone
tips.description: Press the button repeatedly while using this attack to move upward. It has an invincibility window while winding up, and it pulls in nearby opponents, too!

extra_info.name: Landing Luigi Cyclone
extra_info.description: If you land during Luigi Cyclone after using it in midair, you can slide left or right. This is a great way to suddenly avoid an opponent's follow-up attacks.

tips.name: Poltergust G-00
tips.description: With the Poltergust G-00 on his back, Luigi sucks in nearby fighters and items. The move wraps up with just the fighters being launched out of the vacuum cleaner.

tips.name: Lead Headbutt
tips.description: Hit an enemy in front to launch them backward. Hit an enemy behind you to launch them forward. The green guy's kooky like that!

tips.name: Luigi Kick
tips.description: This attack launches opponents almost straight upward. Since you know which way they're going, it's easy to move in and attack again.
If your foe's clinging to an edge for dear life, try using Luigi's down taunt. That meteor smash will be VERY satisfying!
The title Super Mario Bros. 2 refers to different games in the West and in Japan, but both games introduced Luigi's ability to jump higher than Mario!
Ness first appeared in the SNES title EarthBound, released in North America in 1995. He might look like your average kid, but he can use a variety of PSI powers. He's also got a younger sister.
Ness is the main character of EarthBound. In that game, a messenger from the future entrusts him with the fate of the world, and Ness heads out to stop the evil plans of an invader named Giygas.
In EarthBound, Ness occasionally gets homesick, which weakens him in battle. This can be cured by his going home or by just phoning his mom!
Ness has a little sister named Tracy. She works part-time at Escargo Express, answering Ness's calls when he needs to have items delivered.
Ness always had a variety of PSI powers, but he couldn't actually use PK Fire or PK Thunder in EarthBound. His friend Paula could, though.

tips.name: Enlarging PK Flash
tips.description: The power and radius of the charge grow as you hold down the button. You can also move it by tilting the stick left and right.

tips.name: PK Fire
tips.description: If this attack connects, a big pillar of fire appears. "I feel the burn!" your foes will yell.

extra_info.name: Aerial PK Fire
extra_info.description: There's less of a gap between PK Fire and your next move when used in the air instead of the ground. In midair, the attack flies down diagonally, so use it right before landing to keep opponents at bay.

tips.name: PK Thunder's Bolt
tips.description: You can guide the bolt of lightning around and hit yourself for propulsion—but this leaves you defenseless, so be careful!

extra_info.name: PK Thunder's Characteristics
extra_info.description: The lightning won't disappear if an enemy hits its tail. It's also invulnerable for a very brief moment after Ness initially fires it.

extra_info.name: Sneaky PK Thunder Play
extra_info.description: Since you can move the attack yourself, you can stop enemies from recovering by moving it near an edge and waiting for them to climb up.

extra_info.name: PK Thunder Tackle
extra_info.description: Ness can hit himself with the ball of electricity to launch himself like a missile. If you're good with the angle, it can be used for recovery.

extra_info.name: PK Thunder Usage
extra_info.description: If you hit yourself with PK Thunder and run into an obstacle like a wall or the ground, you can immediately use your up special again to trigger PK Thunder without continuing to fall.

tips.name: PSI Magnet
tips.description: This move absorbs energy-based projectiles and heals Ness as a result.

tips.name: PK Starstorm
tips.description: With help from Paula and Poo, countless meteors fall from the sky. You can make slight adjustments to where the meteors fall by tilting left or right.

tips.name: Batter Up!
tips.description: Ness's bat leaves him open to attack, but it has epic launching power. He can even hit projectiles with it!

tips.name: Around the World
tips.description: Ness's yo-yo flies forward and then swings up and around to the back for a wide-ranging, heavy-hitting attack.

tips.name: Reverse PK Throw
tips.description: Enemies with a higher damage percentage are much easier to launch with this. You can knock them out completely if they're damaged enough!
While charging up the up smash or down smash attacks, it's still possible to do damage with the yo-yo.
When an enemy is trying to get back on the stage, you can ruin their day with your side or down air attack. But try not to self-destruct!
When hitting an opponent with a bat, the tip will launch them the farthest. This is true of both the Home-Run Bat item and Ness's side smash.
Captain Falcon's first appearance was in the SNES launch title F-Zero, released in North America in 1991. You couldn't see him in the game itself, but he was in the manual.
He's the main character of the F-Zero series. Known for being a skilled bounty hunter, he's also an exceptional pilot and racer of high-speed F-Zero machines.
The Blue Falcon racing machine first appeared in the original F-Zero. Captain Falcon uses it in Smash Bros. for his Final Smash!

tips.name: Turn Around and Attack with Falcon Punch
tips.description: After pressing the attack button, quickly turn around to make Captain Falcon unleash an even more powerful attack as he turns.

tips.name: Raptor Boost
tips.description: Used in midair, his hand has a meteor effect on the downswing. The trick is to hit the foe when they're below Captain Falcon.

extra_info.name: Raptor Boost Techniques
extra_info.description: Grants super armor at the beginning of the move if performed on the ground. Drive into opponents with Raptor Boost, and then follow up when they're floating in the air.

tips.name: Continuous Falcon Dives
tips.description: A leaping grab that sends the opponent flying with explosive force. If it connects, you're free to use the same attack again without landing.

extra_info.name: Falcon Dive's Direction
extra_info.description: Press left or right the moment Captain Falcon crouches to adjust the angle of the attack. By holding forward, you can greatly increase the range at which you can grab enemies.

tips.name: Falcon Kick
tips.description: The attack is at its most powerful right after pressing the button and loses power as it goes. However, it loses less power when it is used in the air.

tips.name: Blue Falcon
tips.description: The summoned Blue Falcon hits an opponent and sends them flying to an F-Zero course. There, Captain Falcon rams his beloved vehicle into an opponent at high speed, launching them into the air.

extra_info.name: Blue Falcon Animation
extra_info.description: Depending on if he used it in the air or on the ground, Falcon's pose after striking will change. Of course, he won't be happy if he didn't hit anyone...

tips.name: Wheel Kick
tips.description: If Falcon's heel hits an airborne enemy during this attack, it will dunk them. Try standing on the edge of the stage and using it on an opponent trying to grab the edge.

tips.name: Knee Smash
tips.description: There's a violent knee jab at the start of this move. Connect with that for some serious launching power.

tips.name: Overhead Kick
tips.description: It's easy to use this move with a combo or as a diversion. Depending on the hit, you may be able to connect it with a forward air or down air attack to secure a KO.
Jigglypuff has been singing its opponents to sleep in North America since its debut in the 1998 Game Boy games Pokémon Red and Pokémon Blue.
Jigglypuff, a Balloon Pokémon, first appeared in Pokémon Red and Pokémon Blue. Starting with Pokémon X and Pokémon Y, Jigglypuff gained the new Fairy-type classification as well.

tips.name: Rollout's Vulnerability
tips.description: When you hit a foe with this, you'll bounce up and then only be able to move sideways until you land. Try to avoid hitting foes with it if you're trying to recover!

extra_info.name: Turning with Rollout
extra_info.description: While rolling on the ground, press in the opposite direction to make a quick turn. It won't work if you're airborne, but it'll slow you down.

tips.name: Pound
tips.description: A simple punch that travels horizontally. You can use it repeatedly in midair—combine it with jumps to stay aloft for a very long time.

extra_info.name: Using Pound
extra_info.description: This move can lay waste to opponents' shields. You can adjust the direction slightly by inputting up or down in the air right before attacking.

tips.name: Sing
tips.description: Enemies on the ground who hear this song will fall asleep. The more damage they have, the longer they'll nap.

extra_info.name: Pursue with Sing
extra_info.description: If Jigglypuff uses Sing in midair, it can move left or right slightly. You can use this to chase after an opponent who evaded your attack or use it to get back onstage when the edge is just out of reach.

tips.name: Rest
tips.description: This move is hard to hit with but can really send opponents flying! You'll need to get very close or use it right when your opponent tries to attack.

extra_info.name: Rest Techniques
extra_info.description: Jigglypuff can move again just a little sooner when it hits something compared to when it misses. This means that landing a hit instead of whiffing makes it harder for your opponent to counterattack.

extra_info.name: Farewell Gift
extra_info.description: Opponents launched by Rest will get a flower on their heads. Talk about insult to injury.

tips.name: Puff Up
tips.description: Jigglypuff grows huge before launching nearby opponents. It's easy to escape on some stages, but it's a force to be reckoned with on stages with no place to escape, such as Battlefield.

tips.name: Jiggly Split
tips.description: This move attacks in front of you and behind you at the same time, and your legs are invincible as they kick. It launches foes horizontally.

tips.name: Jiggly Ram
tips.description: When done on an opponent who hasn't taken much damage, you might find yourself open to counterattack, but when done on an opponent who's taken a lot of damage, you might get a KO.
If its shield is shattered, Jigglypuff will get launched and KO'd no matter its damage.

Once in the air, Jigglypuff can jump five times, allowing it to move much quicker than running on the ground. Try to keep opponents in the air and build an advantageous scenario for yourself during battles.
Peach first appeared in Super Mario Bros., where she was known as Princess Toadstool. This was also the first of her many run-ins with Bowser...
Peach was the star of Super Princess Peach, released in North America in 2006. Mario and the others get kidnapped by Bowser and it's up to Peach to save them with the help of her friend Perry.
When Mario took up a new career as Dr. Mario, Peach joined him as a nurse. But she didn't get to appear in the game itself—only in the manual!

tips.name: Peach Parasol Descent
tips.description: Press up and down to open and close your parasol and adjust your fall speed.

extra_info.name: Open and Shut Peach Parasol
extra_info.description: Peach can't grab the ledge if she's facing away from it and her parasol is open. Tap down to close it, though, and she'll grab on safely.

tips.name: Vegetable
tips.description: Of the eight types, the wrinkle-faced vegetable is the strongest. If you throw it while tapping a direction, it can do over 30% damage!

extra_info.name: Rare "Vegetables"
extra_info.description: Rarely, Peach will pull up a Bob-omb or Mr. Saturn. How rarely? Well, you have a 1/250 chance of a Bob-omb and a 1/166 chance of a Mr. Saturn!

tips.name: Peach Blossom
tips.description: Peach's charming dance puts nearby opponents into a deep sleep and causes three healing peaches to appear. It also does damage. The closer Peach is to her opponents, the greater the effect.

tips.name: Diverse Weapons
tips.description: While preparing a side smash attack, moving the control stick up or down will produce a different weapon. Up for a frying pan, down for a tennis racket, or no direction for a golf club.

extra_info.name: A Tool for Every Job
extra_info.description: The frying pan does the most damage, the tennis racket launches opponents at a low angle, and the golf club has the best reach.

tips.name: Ribbon Dance
tips.description: Peach unleashes a powerful attack directed straight above her. In addition to opponents above her, it can also hit nearby opponents on the ground.

tips.name: Attacking with Toad
tips.description: If your grab is successful, Peach's protector, Toad, will appear. He'll perform a combination attack with Peach during grab attacks and throws.
Holding the jump button will let Peach hover in the air for a short time. She can even attack as she floats!
Hold jump while crouching to float just above the ground. You can use air attacks quickly that way!
When floating in the air and holding an item, it's possible to attack without throwing the item. Your battle options can increase if you think about how to use this tip in combination with Vegetable.
Daisy debuted in Super Mario Land released in 1989. Mario saved her from a space alien named Tatanga, and the two escaped riding the Sky Pop.
Sarasaland's Princess. She's more down-to-earth and energetic than Peach. She gives it her all at sporting events and parties, but tries to keep the mood friendly.

tips.name: Toad
tips.description: A Toad will risk its life to protect Daisy. If an attack hits the Toad, it will unleash a counter and launch opponents in front of Daisy.

tips.name: Daisy Bomber
tips.description: Attack an opponent with a powerful hip bash. You can use it as many times as you want in the air, so it's helpful for returning to the stage.

tips.name: Daisy Parasol
tips.description: Jump up, open the parasol, and slowly float down. It's great to use for returning to the stage, but the parasol can hit opponents too, so you can use it for attacks.

tips.name: Vegetable
tips.description: Of the eight types, the vegetable with the wrinkly face is the strongest. If you throw it while tapping the control stick in a direction, it can do over 30% damage!

tips.name: Daisy Blossom
tips.description: Daisy's Final Smash puts opponents to sleep with a hypnotic dance. Pick up the daisies that appear to recover some health.
Mario's eternal rival, Bowser, debuted in Super Mario Bros. He appeared as the boss of every world, but they were all fake except for in World 8.
As the king of the Koopas, Bowser lives to disturb the peace. He's the eternal archenemy of Mario. Bowser attempts to fulfill his own ambitions by leading his minions to attack the Mushroom Kingdom.
In Super Mario Bros.: The Lost Levels, Mario fights a blue fake Bowser before the real thing. That blue fake is his eighth color in this game!

tips.name: Aim Fire Breath
tips.description: You can change the angle of Bowser's flame. Try aiming slightly above the enemy to make it harder for them to escape! Bowser's not above dirty tricks.

extra_info.name: The Power of Fire Breath
extra_info.description: Once Bowser's used up his fire once, he needs time to recharge. Give him 12 seconds, and he'll be ready to roast!

tips.name: Flying Slam
tips.description: Bowser's target has a bit of control over their direction in the air, too. The lower their damage, the more influence they have (but still not much).

extra_info.name: Flying Slam Entanglement
extra_info.description: The grabbed opponent is smashed on the ground under Bowser. While falling, Bowser can also catch other nearby opponents. But if you fall off the edge, you'll be KO'd before the grabbed opponent is!

tips.name: Whirling Tip for Whirling Fortress
tips.description: Repeatedly pressing the button while using this in midair will give you a small boost in how high you fly.

extra_info.name: Counter with Whirling Fortress
extra_info.description: Try using this right after shielding an enemy attack. Cancel the shield and whirl toward the enemy to counterattack!

tips.name: Bowser Bomb
tips.description: Bowser will leap into the air hornsfirst. Hitting opponents on the way up makes it much easier to clobber them again on the way down.

extra_info.name: Bowser Bomb the Ledge
extra_info.description: You can grab on to an edge while bombing, but only if you're really close to it. It's a quick albeit risky way to descend quickly.

extra_info.name: Bowser Bomb—the Shield Breaker
extra_info.description: If you land this on a shielding enemy, you can break their shield in one hit. Just make sure you capitalize afterward with a dropkick!

tips.name: Giga Bowser Punch
tips.description: Giga Bowser's massive punch will launch opponents depending on where it lands. If an opponent has taken a lot of damage, they'll be launched for a guaranteed KO.
Bowser's a real tough guy. When he's only lightly damaged, weaker attacks won't make him flinch.
When you're in the air prior to landing and it looks like an opponent is going to try to hit you from below, you can alter your landing timing by using a down air attack or a down special.
Weak attacks won't cause him to flinch while his damage is low. He also gets super armor at the start of his tilt attacks and smash attacks.
#1 is Bowser, #2 is King K. Rool, and #3 is Donkey Kong and King Dedede. The heavier a fighter, the stronger their attacks and the harder they are to launch.
Ice Climbers first appeared in the 1985 release Ice Climber. The boy is Popo and the girl is Nana. They jump with hammers in hand as they aim for the summit.
In Ice Climber, Popo and Nana are identical outside of their clothing. Two players can play at the same time, and the game can be cooperative or competitive depending on how you play.

tips.name: Ice from Ice Shot
tips.description: Popo and Nana both shoot ice from their hammers. If both climbers are present, there are two hunks of ice, but only one hunk if only one of them is present.

extra_info.name: Ice Shot Reflected
extra_info.description: Ice Shot will be sent back at you if hit by an opponent or blocked by their shield. If an opponent's attack sends it back your way, it can damage you, so stay alert.

tips.name: Squall Hammer
tips.description: Move left and right with the control stick and rise into the air by repeatedly pressing the button. When both Popo and Nana are present, you can launch opponents with the final hit.

tips.name: Belay
tips.description: Popo tosses Nana upward, and Nana then pulls Popo up with a rope. The rope is a lifeline that connects the two climbers.

tips.name: Blizzard
tips.description: The Ice Climbers unleash a snowstorm from their hands that freezes opponents. If this move is used on opponents that are already frozen, they will stay frozen longer.

tips.name: Iceberg
tips.description: A Final Smash where the Ice Climbers summon an iceberg from the bottom of the screen. The iceberg can be spun left and right, but you will move as well, so be smart.

extra_info.name: Iceberg's Condor
extra_info.description: A condor will appear along with the iceberg. Only the Ice Climbers can grab on to its legs.

extra_info.name: Polar Bear in Iceberg
extra_info.description: The Polar Bear will be in the middle area of the iceberg trying to maintain its balance. If you touch the Polar Bear, it will launch you farther than the iceberg. Quite dangerous.

tips.name: Hammer Sweep
tips.description: Popo attacks in front of himself and Nana attacks behind. When Popo is alone, his attack range will become smaller.

tips.name: Hammer Slam
tips.description: Roll forward in the air and slam down the hammer. Popo's attack can launch opponents far, and Nana's attack has a meteor effect if it hits just right.
Popo will be the only one that grabs. When throwing an opponent, Nana will raise her arms and cheer!
If Popo is grabbed, Nana will panic and be unable to move. Once Popo is freed, Nana will begin moving again.
Nana is slightly easier to launch than Popo. When Nana is gone, the Special moves and normal attacks will become weaker and Belay won't be as effective for recoveries.
Sheik first appeared in the 1998 N64 title The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time. Her true identity is Ze— I won't spoil it for you.
After Link pulls the Master Sword and ages over time, this mysterious youth appears before him. Sheik appears before Link a number of times after that, teaching him various songs using a harp.
If you use the Sheik amiibo in The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild, you can sometimes obtain Sheik's Mask. You can re-create Sheik's look by wearing that with pieces from the Stealth series.

tips.name: Needle Storm
tips.description: You can start charging this attack in the air. The more charged it is, the more needles are fired, but they lose power the farther they get from Sheik.

tips.name: Burst Grenade
tips.description: Hold the special-move button to throw the grenade farther. It doesn't move much once it lands, but it can travel three times as far in the air.

extra_info.name: Burst Grenade as an Item
extra_info.description: If Burst Grenade is hit before the pin is removed, it'll fly off and explode. If Sheik is hit before the pin is removed, Burst Grenade will become an item and fall to the ground.

tips.name: Bouncing Fish's Potential
tips.description: Press the button again to make Sheik use the axe kick faster. Use it again right after the first kick hits, and you won't freeze up when you land.

extra_info.name: Bouncing Fish's Reach
extra_info.description: Hold left or right to greatly affect the range of the attack. In this way, you can use the attack as a feint or really surprise an opponent!

tips.name: Sheikah Dance
tips.description: Sheik advances forth and slices through the opponent she hits. She can catch only one opponent with this move, but catching an opponent is pretty easy and the move is very powerful.

tips.name: Cycle Kick
tips.description: This attack is fast and can lift the enemy up a short distance, making it easier to strike the enemy multiple times in a combo.

tips.name: Razor Wing
tips.description: Landing this attack while Sheik's hand is coming up will launch opponents upward. Landing it when her hand comes down will launch them sideways.

tips.name: Hatchet
tips.description: This move doesn't have much attack power, but you can trigger it quickly and it doesn't leave Sheik vulnerable for long. It's also great for combos on opponents that haven't taken much damage yet.
Zelda first appeared in the NES title The Legend of Zelda, released in North America in 1987. In that title, the evil Ganon kidnaps Zelda and steals the Triforce of Power.

Zelda is the princess from the game series The Legend of Zelda. With a few exceptions, the Zelda in each game is a unique individual who shares the name Zelda, and the setting is different in each title.
The names of Zelda's special moves come from the goddesses who created Hyrule. They are Din, the Goddess of Power; Nayru, the Goddess of Wisdom; and Farore, the Goddess of Courage.

tips.name: Nayru's Love
tips.description: This move summons a magical barrier that reflects enemy projectiles. It's not just for defense, either—it also deals direct damage!

extra_info.name: Nayru's Love's Traits
extra_info.description: Zelda is invincible at the start of this move, and the move can also reflect projectiles. It comes out very quickly, making it useful in a wide variety of situations.

tips.name: Din's Fire
tips.description: This explodes when you release the button. Hold it longer to get more range and a stronger explosion.

extra_info.name: Din's Fire's Traits
extra_info.description: You can adjust the flame's path while the button is pressed. The flame will explode if it hits the stage, too much time passes, or you let go of the button.

tips.name: Farore's Wind
tips.description: Zelda will deal damage to those nearby when she disappears and reappears. Hold a direction to control where she teleports to.

extra_info.name: Aiming Farore's Wind
extra_info.description: Hold a direction before disappearing to warp that way. Zelda will follow the direction of any platforms she encounters during the warp, as well.

extra_info.name: Feint with Farore's Wind
extra_info.description: If you direct the move downward while standing on the ground, you'll reappear in the same place. It can really throw your opponent for a loop!

tips.name: Charged Phantom Slash
tips.description: Charge this attack to make the Phantom travel farther. It'll attack differently based on how long the attack is charged.

extra_info.name: Phantom Slash's Traits
extra_info.description: It's possible to attack by pressing the special-move button even before the Phantom is complete. The attack power and range increase depending on how complete the Phantom is.

extra_info.name: Destroying the Phantom
extra_info.description: If Zelda takes damage or the Phantom takes serious damage before it is complete, it will collapse before your eyes.

tips.name: Triforce of Wisdom
tips.description: A gigantic triangle swallows up one opponent and seals them away. The suction becomes progressively stronger. If an opponent has over 100% damage, it's an immediate KO.

tips.name: Lightning Kick
tips.description: The tip of Zelda's foot is packed with launch power at the beginning of the move. It has good KO potential but leaves you vulnerable, and only the tip has good launch power.

tips.name: Meteor Heel
tips.description: A move with a meteor effect. The beginning of the move has the most power, but you can use it to disrupt somebody from recovering even if you don't get a clean hit.
Dr. Mario made his debut in 1990 in the puzzle game of the same name. He was developing vitamins to combat some nasty viruses.
The game Dr. Mario was released for the NES and the Game Boy in the same year. It's a puzzle game where Mario tries to eradicate viruses using vitamin capsules.

tips.name: Capsules
tips.description: The capsules bounce higher than Mario's fireballs. Also, since they're physical projectiles, energy-absorbing specials won't work on them.

extra_info.name: Capsule Types
extra_info.description: The capsules Dr. Mario throws are selected at random from nine different combinations of red, blue, and yellow. The difference is only cosmetic.

tips.name: Super Sheet
tips.description: This move doesn't have as much horizontal range as Mario's Cape, but it has more range vertically, so lure your foes in and then strike!

tips.name: Super Jump Punch
tips.description: It won't hit multiple times, but it does do a lot of damage. The beginning has good launching power too. It's good for mix-ups since it triggers quickly and you can initiate it while shielding.

tips.name: Dr. Tornado
tips.description: Press the button repeatedly midattack to rise into the air. However, after it ends you are vulnerable, so be careful using it to recover.

tips.name: Doctor Finale
tips.description: Two giant vitamin capsules catch opponents and send them flying. Dr. Mario is invincible while using the move, and he'll float in the air for a short while if you use the move midair.

tips.name: Ear, Nose, and Throat
tips.description: Launches opponents a great distance backward at an upward diagonal. If it lands while you're on a tall platform, you may be able to KO opponents through the top of the screen.

tips.name: Dr. Kick
tips.description: A rare move in that the end of the move is stronger than the beginning. Time your movement well so you hit opponents near the end of the move to launch them.

tips.name: Dr. Punch
tips.description: Unlike Mario's similar move, this will never have a meteor effect regardless of your timing, but it is strongest when his fist is directly in front of him.

tips.name: CLEAR!
tips.description: Unlike Mario's down air attack, Dr. Mario's down air has a meteor effect. It's powerful, but Dr. Mario's recovery is limited, so be careful with your aim.

tips.name: Hospital Bed
tips.description: Unlike Mario's downward throw, Dr. Mario's always sends the foe bouncing upward, so you can follow it up more easily with an air attack.
Pichu debuted in Pokémon Gold and Silver, released in North America in 2000. If you leave two Pikachu or two Raichu of the opposite sex at the Daycare, Pichu can hatch from the Egg that is found.
Pichu isn't a professional at charging electricity like Pikachu. The slightest touch can make Pichu discharge its electricity and shock itself.

tips.name: Thunder Jolt
tips.description: Pichu's Thunder Jolt is smaller, faster, and stronger than Pikachu's, but it also does damage to Pichu.

tips.name: Skull Bash
tips.description: It takes a while to charge up to the max, but the power unleashed at max is incredibly powerful. This comes at a risk of self-destructing from the force.

tips.name: Agility
tips.description: Pichu's up special travels farther than Pikachu's, but it causes Pichu to take damage, and it doesn't deal damage to opponents on contact.

tips.name: Thunder
tips.description: A lightning strike from above that unleashes a bolt of electricity. If the bolt hits Pichu, Pichu will take damage.

tips.name: Volt Tackle
tips.description: Become an electric ball and bounce around the screen. It does more damage and has more launch power than Pikachu's Final Smash, but it also does a lot of damage to Pichu.
Any move where Pichu is engulfed in electricity will deal damage to Pichu. However, Pichu's moves also have significant power and are great for launching opponents.
#1 is Pichu, #2 is Jigglypuff, and #3 is Mr. Game & Watch and Squirtle. Light fighters are easy to launch but can easily escape from combos.
Falco debuted alongside Fox in the 1993 SNES title Star Fox. He may be hotheaded and a little cocky, but his skills are the real deal.
Falco—full name Falco Lombardi—has appeared in Star Fox games starting with the first one, Star Fox. He's as skilled an Arwing pilot as Fox. His design is based on a pheasant.

tips.name: Blaster
tips.description: Unlike Fox's Blaster, Falco's Blaster can make opponents flinch. It fires faster when airborne.

extra_info.name: Additional Attacks with Blaster
extra_info.description: After throwing an opponent up, down, or backward, you can follow up with a shot from Blaster. If the opponent has already taken a lot of damage, the shot might not hit them, though.

tips.name: Falco Phantasm
tips.description: Falco Phantasm can send an airborne enemy straight down—use it to dunk them into the abyss!

extra_info.name: Low Air Phantasm
extra_info.description: You can use Falco Phantasm at low altitude by jumping while running and immediately pressing the special-move button. I hear he prefers the air, anyway.

tips.name: Fire Bird
tips.description: Inertia affects this move more than it affects Fox's Fire Fox. If you use it while moving horizontally in midair, you'll slide along in midair for a moment.

tips.name: Reflector
tips.description: The energy shield can be used to slow an opponent down in addition to reflecting projectiles.

tips.name: Team Star Fox
tips.description: Falco jumps into a fighter craft called an Arwing and joins Team Star Fox for a full-on assault. Falco's line changes depending on if he's attacking one opponent or a group of opponents.

tips.name: Tail Cutter
tips.description: This move can launch an enemy off the stage with one hit if their damage is high enough. You're not left vulnerable, so it's a low-risk way to KO foes.

tips.name: Spinning Falco Chop
tips.description: If you land just before the final attack in Falco's midair combo, you can move straight into a neutral combo on the ground.

tips.name: Corkscrew Meteor
tips.description: Compared to Fox, the move is slow to attack and doesn't hit multiple times, but the first kick has a meteor effect.
#1 is Falco, #2 is Greninja, and #3 is Zero Suit Samus. The difference was very slight, but it was enough to place Luigi as #4.
Marth debuted in the first Fire Emblem game in 1990, but that game wasn't released in North America until the Nintendo DS remake in 2009.
Marth is the protagonist of the first Fire Emblem series game, Fire Emblem: Shadow Dragon and the Blade of Light. He's the prince of Altea and was driven from his homeland.

tips.name: Shield Breaker
tips.description: This move leaves you wide open, but if you charge it to maximum, it can break an enemy's shield in a single hit.

extra_info.name: Midair Shield Breaker
extra_info.description: Marth will move forward slightly if the move is used midair, so it can be used to recover when trying to get back to the stage. He'll move farther when the move is fully charged.

extra_info.name: Shield Breaker Techniques
extra_info.description: By entering up or down the moment the move starts, you can change the direction the blade strikes. You can do just a little bit more damage by hitting an opponent's head.

tips.name: Dancing Blade Combo
tips.description: Keep pressing the button to turn this into a four-hit combo. Holding different directions will change the angle of the strike!

extra_info.name: Dancing Blade's Color
extra_info.description: Marth's sword leaves a trail as he swings it, and the color reflects the attack's height. Blue is high, red is middle, and green is low.

extra_info.name: Dancing Blade's Sideways Swing
extra_info.description: Hold toward the foe you're attacking on the fourth swing for an attack that launches foes much farther than the up or down versions.

extra_info.name: Dancing Blade Up
extra_info.description: Press up on the fourth swing for a slice that will launch your foes. This swing is excellent for netting some KOs!

extra_info.name: Dancing Blade Down
extra_info.description: During Marth's Dancing Blade combo, tilt the control stick down on the fourth swing to finish the combo with a powerful series of blows to your opponent's feet!

extra_info.name: Aerial Dancing Blade
extra_info.description: Use Dancing Blade midjump to make Marth rise slightly in the air. Even during short hops, you'll be able to use the attack multiple times with this method.

tips.name: Dolphin Slash
tips.description: Just before the attack comes out, Marth will be invincible, but it works a little faster in the air than when on land. Once you get used to using it, you can even use it to break up an opponent's combo.

tips.name: Counter
tips.description: The counter depends on the attack it is countering. A countered smash attack may KO an opponent!

tips.name: Critical Hit
tips.description: In this Final Smash, Marth rushes forth with blazing speed. If you go too far, you can press the button again to stop on the spot.

tips.name: Dragon Killer
tips.description: The tip of the blade has astounding launching power. It's a bit hard to aim for a hit with it, but that just means the rush you get from landing a clean hit feels even better.

tips.name: About Face
tips.description: This is a unique move in that Marth can change his direction while attacking. After the beginning part of the move, he'll change direction in midair.
For moves that use Marth's sword, the base is weak and the tip is strong. Try to maintain a good distance when fighting so you hit your opponents with the tip.
Marth and Lucina are tied for #1, with Fox at #3. Walking doesn't leave you as open as running; patience can be a virtue in battle!

variants.name: Lucina
variants.boxing_ring_title: The Warrior From a Doomed Future

tips.description: Lucina made her debut with the 2013 release of Fire Emblem Awakening. At first, she went by Marth, but she later revealed her true identity.

Chrom's daughter who comes from the future wielding Parallel Falchion. The Brand of the Exalt is located in her left eye. When she first appears, she wears a mask and calls herself Marth, after the legendary Hero-King.

tips.name: Critical Hit
tips.description: Lucina dashes forward and launches any foes in her path. It goes quite far if nothing gets in its way, so swing your sword before you go off-screen!

tips.name: Anti-air Slash
tips.description: Lucina cuts an arc through the air with her sword. This does the most damage at the back, so try using it on foes standing close behind you!
Marth's sword deals more damage at the tip, but Lucina's is the same all the way along. That aside, their moves are fundamentally pretty similar.

Young Link debuted in The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time released in 1998. He lived in the Kokiri Forest, until the Great Deku Tree, guardian of the forest, informs him of danger approaching Hyrule.
To stop Ganondorf, the Great King of Evil, Young Link travels with his fairy companion Navi. He pulls out the Master Sword, and after a seven-year slumber, emerges as the Hero of Time.

tips.name: Fire Arrow
tips.description: Young Link's arrows will burn anything they hit. They can be used to detonate explosives from far away.

tips.name: Boomerang
tips.description: The Boomerang will also hit when returning, making it useful to keep opponents at bay. The Boomerang Young Link uses is the one from The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time.

extra_info.name: Boomerang's Flight Distance
extra_info.description: The distance the Boomerang flies depends on how far you tilt the control stick, but it can't fly as far as the other Links' boomerangs. However, it deals heavy damage the moment it's thrown.

tips.name: Spin Attack
tips.description: Spins around and hits opponents repeatedly. Hold down the button while on the ground to increase the power. The launching power is low, but you can get combos with an air attack.

tips.name: Bomb
tips.description: Fighters caught in the bomb's explosion can be hit a maximum of four times. Since it hits multiple times, the damage it deals is higher than Link's or Toon Link's bombs.

extra_info.name: Bomb Blast
extra_info.description: Your bombs are special ones—the explosion from hitting an enemy won't hurt you.

tips.name: Triforce Slash
tips.description: If the Triforce hits an opponent, the opponent will be bound and hit with the sword multiple times. The final blow can hit multiple enemies.
Young Link drinks the famous product of Lon Lon Ranch, Lon Lon Milk. Drinking it won't heal any damage, but it sure is delicious!
Young Link's launch power is the weakest of the Links, but that makes it easier to perform combos. He's quicker in the air, can unleash moves faster, and is less vulnerable when landing.
He appears in The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time as the king of the Gerudo. He pretends to pledge his loyalty to the King of Hyrule while plotting to make the kingdom his own.
Ganon appears as the final boss in The Legend of Zelda. In that title, he looks more beast than human, making him a sharp contrast to Ganondorf.

tips.name: Warlock Punch
tips.description: After pressing the attack button, quickly turn around to make Ganondorf unleash an even more powerful attack as he turns.

extra_info.name: Midair Warlock Punch
extra_info.description: This attack is much more powerful when used in the air. However, you'll have to use a Bunny Hood or high platforms to find enough time to pull it off.

tips.name: Midair Flame Choke
tips.description: An enemy hit by this in the air will be slammed to the ground and can't recover quickly. It's also slightly more powerful than when used on the ground.

extra_info.name: Flame Choke Travels
extra_info.description: You can use this to grab an enemy and drag them offstage with you. However, the point will go to the last person to strike that enemy before your grab.

extra_info.name: Escaping from Flame Choke
extra_info.description: If you get caught by Flame Choke in midair, you can try to wriggle about to escape. However, it's harder to escape if you've taken more damage than Ganondorf.

tips.name: Wizard's Foot
tips.description: Used on the ground, this move is a horizontal flying kick. In the air, it goes downward instead and has a meteor effect at the very start.

extra_info.name: Aerial Wizard's Foot
extra_info.description: When done in the air, this move has immense launching power. Sometimes it can also be effective to use against a fighter who is waiting below.

tips.name: Ganon, The Demon King
tips.description: After transforming into Ganon, the Demon King, he rushes forward with his swords to obliterate the enemy. The first swing stuns foes. Hit with that, and it'll be easier to hit with the finishing rush.
All of Ganondorf's smash attacks use a sword. They leave him vulnerable, but the moves are very powerful.

tips.name: Volcano Kick
tips.description: It's a slow attack, but it can do more damage than a smash attack. Even if defended against, the attack can break all but a perfect shield.

tips.name: Leg Sweep
tips.description: A crouching kick that sends opponents flying upward. It has good reach and is one of Ganondorf's faster attacks.
Mewtwo first appears in Pokémon Red and Pokémon Blue. After the trainer comes out victorious in the Pokémon League, Mewtwo can be found in a cave on the outskirts of Cerulean City.
Mewtwo was born from recombined genes of a Mythical Pokémon, Mew. Its fighting ability is incredibly high, and it's said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.
In its Final Smash, Mewtwo Mega Evolves into Mega Mewtwo Y. In the Pokémon games, it can Mega Evolve into this form if it holds on to Mewtwonite Y.

tips.name: Charging Shadow Ball
tips.description: This move does more damage the longer it's charged...but it also has more recoil. The recoil is stronger when this move is used in midair.

extra_info.name: Holding Charge on Shadow Ball
extra_info.description: Mewtwo won't automatically attack when this move is fully charged. You can press the shield button to cancel the move and keep the charge.

tips.name: Confusion
tips.description: This special grabbing move lets Mewtwo repel foes with telekinesis. It can also reflect enemy projectiles.

extra_info.name: Confusion's Traits
extra_info.description: This move sends the opponent spinning, and sometimes you can get in follow-up attacks since the time it takes for an opponent to be able to move again is affected by how much damage they've taken.

tips.name: Teleport
tips.description: Mewtwo will disappear for a moment, reappearing elsewhere on the stage. Mewtwo is completely invulnerable while teleporting.

extra_info.name: Teleport Landing Position
extra_info.description: If Mewtwo finishes this move on the ground, Mewtwo will stop in place, but if the move finishes in the air, Mewtwo's momentum will carry it a bit farther.

extra_info.name: Teleport's Traits
extra_info.description: If Mewtwo hits a platform while teleporting, it will slide across it. You can adjust its destination with directional inputs.

tips.name: Disable
tips.description: The higher the target's damage, the longer they'll be stunned for. However, if the move is reflected, Mewtwo will be stunned instead.

extra_info.name: Warnings for Disable
extra_info.description: This powerful move requires eye contact to work. If the enemy is looking, it can stun them, but if not... Well, it's not much of a staring contest.

extra_info.name: Disable Techniques
extra_info.description: Immediately after unleashing this move, you'll be invincible for a quick moment, allowing you to avoid attacks. It's a powerful move to use just as an opponent begins their attack.

tips.name: Psystrike
tips.description: Mewtwo Mega Evolves into Mega Mewtwo Y and fires a projectile that penetrates terrain and opponents. It'll stop the movement of any opponent it touches, and then launches them.

tips.name: Tail Sweep
tips.description: You can change the direction you launch enemies in by hitting them with different parts of Mewtwo's tail.

tips.name: Shadow Blast
tips.description: The shock wave does more damage at the very edges than at the core. Watch what you're doing, and try to attack with the edge of the wave.

tips.name: Galaxy Force
tips.description: This attack can strike an enemy multiple times if you can hit them at the right time. It's very difficult to escape this attack before it's over.

tips.name: Shadow Bomb
tips.description: This attack only strikes right beside Mewtwo's feet and doesn't do so much damage, but it has a high launching ability to make up for it.

tips.name: Body Spark
tips.description: Hitting an enemy with this doesn't do much damage, but it hits multiple times. If you time it right, it can even launch foes.

tips.name: Shadow Scratch
tips.description: This attack might be short range, but it's fast and has a lot of launch power. Use it while ascending to get the most out of it!

tips.name: Shadow Cannon
tips.description: Mewtwo will throw an enemy and quickly fire Shadow Ball five times. Each projectile doesn't do much damage, but it can hit multiple foes.

tips.name: Psychic Whirlwind
tips.description: Mewtwo's up throw boasts top class launching power compared to all of the other fighters' throw moves. It's super effective in situations where there is no ceiling.
Despite Mewtwo's fierce appearance, Mewtwo is very light and easy to launch. When playing as Mewtwo, you'll need to prioritize your defense and evasion as much as your offense.
Roy, known as the Young Lion, is the main protagonist of Fire Emblem: The Binding Blade. His father, Eliwood, was the main protagonist of the 2003 Game Boy Advance game, Fire Emblem.

tips.name: Boosting Flare Blade
tips.description: As you hold down the button, the attack gradually grows in strength. It's unleashed when you let go of the button or when the move is fully charged.

extra_info.name: Overpower with Flare Blade
extra_info.description: Try charging this move right after launching a foe off of the stage, and then smack them with it when they try to recover!

extra_info.name: Flare Blade Techniques
extra_info.description: While holding the button, you can use the control stick to turn around and release your charged attack in the other direction. This is a good way to surprise unsuspecting opponents.

tips.name: Double-Edge Dance Patterns
tips.description: Press the button repeatedly for a four-hit combo. You can change the finishing move by pressing up or down during the combo.

extra_info.name: Double-Edge Dance Techniques
extra_info.description: If you aim up on the third hit and to the side on the fourth, the power of the fourth hit increases and it will be easier to hit with the full blade.

tips.name: Blazer's Traits
tips.description: Roy is momentarily invincible when he uses this move. Unleash it while on the ground to absorb a few hits, and then launch opponents.

extra_info.name: Turning Blazer
extra_info.description: Roy can lash out in the opposite direction with this move if you quickly tap backward before he begins his ascent.

extra_info.name: Blazer's Angle
extra_info.description: By pushing left or right during Roy's ascent, you can adjust the angle of the move. However, this stops him from climbing as high.

tips.name: Counter
tips.description: When Counter is used in midair, it has less launching power than when it's used successfully on the ground. For a match-deciding moment, you're probably better off getting into stance on the ground.

extra_info.name: Counter's Attack Power
extra_info.description: The move is a little slow to start and only lasts a short while, but if used successfully on an incoming attack, it can deal 1.35x damage back at the enemy.

extra_info.name: Using Counter
extra_info.description: One sneaky strategy is to counter an opponent in midair as they try to stop you from recovering. Roy falls pretty quickly, though, so it's risky!

tips.name: Critical Hit
tips.description: A blade surrounded by fire swings through nearby opponents. If you jump into a pack of enemies, you can take them all out in one shot.

tips.name: Slash
tips.description: A backhanded, upward sword slash. Unlike most neutral attacks, it doesn't include any follow-ups, but it has speed and power to make up for it.

tips.name: Sharp Edge
tips.description: Roy takes a step forward as he unleashes the move, making it easier to strike with the whole blade and do a lot more damage.

tips.name: Low Stab
tips.description: This fast, crouching sword strike has low power, but it turns Roy into a small target. It's great for disrupting enemy attacks.

tips.name: Flame Sword
tips.description: Roy's up smash, Flame Sword, is an upward jab of his fiery blade. His sword arm is invulnerable during this, making the move good against air attacks.

tips.name: Double Slash
tips.description: Roy performs a double sword slash in midair. This attack's speed makes it ideal for disrupting enemy attacks and getting in close.

tips.name: Half Moon
tips.description: If you can hit the foe with the full blade, this move has a meteor effect. It's powerful straight down, but aim carefully, because it lacks horizontal range.

tips.name: Slam
tips.description: Enemies thrown down by this move will bounce and then momentarily float in front of Roy. That's a good time for a follow-up attack!
Unlike Marth, Roy's attacks are more powerful the closer you get to the base of his blade. If you can get up close and personal with your opponent, you can deliver some powerful blows.
Chrom made his debut in Fire Emblem Awakening released in 2013. He wields the Falchion that belongs to the royal family and has the Brand that proves he is a descendant of the Hero-King Marth.
The prince of the Halidom of Ylisse can be clumsy at times, but he cares deeply for his comrades. With Falchion in hand, he fights off the neighboring country of Plegia. Lucina is his daughter.

tips.name: Flare Blade
tips.description: Slashes opponents with a sword. You can change direction while charging up the attack, but if you overcharge it, you will receive damage.

tips.name: Double-Edge Dance
tips.description: Repeatedly attack up to four times, with additional input. The repeated attacks will change if you input up, sideways, or down on the control stick.

tips.name: Soaring Slash
tips.description: Slash upward and then slam down into the ground. You can't cancel the move until you land, so be careful to use it near edges.

tips.name: Counter
tips.description: Use this move right before an opponent's attack hits you to launch a quick and powerful counterattack.

tips.name: Awakening Aether
tips.description: Get close to an opponent and hit them with a technique called Sol. Then, follow up with the piercing Luna technique for a perfect combination.
Mr. Game & Watch first appeared in the early '80s in the Game & Watch handheld game series—predating even the NES and Game Boy!
This game character is older than Mario, coming from a series of handheld Nintendo games with LCD screens. Within the Game & Watch games, he took on various shapes and roles.
Mr. Game & Watch's moves Chef, Judge, Fire, Oil Panic, and Octopus are all named after games released in the Game & Watch series.

tips.name: Chef
tips.description: Food jumps out of Mr. Game & Watch's frying pan in all directions. Use this to protect yourself.

extra_info.name: Chef Techniques
extra_info.description: You can change the timing of the flying food by varying the length of time you hold the button. Push it repeatedly to throw more food more quickly.

tips.name: Judge
tips.description: This move's power depends on the number displayed. Swinging a 1 is nearly useless, but a 9 has incredible launching power!

extra_info.name: Judge
extra_info.description: Three apples appear if the number seven shows up. You can eat these to heal up, but if you're not careful, an opponent might snap them up.

tips.name: Fire
tips.description: A pair of firemen launch Mr. Game & Watch high into the air. Nearby opponents will also be pushed back a bit.

extra_info.name: Launching with Fire
extra_info.description: You can launch enemies sideways by hitting them on your ascent. This is a good way to turn the tables on someone waiting for you to pop up from a ledge.

tips.name: Oil Panic
tips.description: This bucket can hold all manner of projectiles. Once filled, unleash the contents on an unsuspecting foe.

extra_info.name: Oil Panic and Projectiles
extra_info.description: This will only absorb energy-based projectiles. Physical projectiles, like Link's arrows, will be reflected instead.

extra_info.name: Oil Panic's Gauge
extra_info.description: Absorbing a particularly powerful energy blast can fill the gauge in one go. However, even the weakest of attacks will always fill it by one segment.

extra_info.name: Oil Panic and Bombs
extra_info.description: You can use this to absorb explosions from Link's bombs or from Bob-ombs. Don't try it with the Bob-ombs that fall during Sudden Death, though.

extra_info.name: Oil Panic and Getting KO'd
extra_info.description: The gauge doesn't reset when Mr. Game & Watch is KO'd. Try surprising enemies with a bucket full of boiling-hot oil as soon as you respawn!

extra_info.name: Oil Panic
extra_info.description: This move can reflect items and physical projectiles that can't be absorbed. If something comes flying at you, give it a try!

tips.name: Octopus
tips.description: For his Final Smash, he'll turn into a giant octopus and catch opponents in his tentacles. If an opponent doesn't escape by pressing buttons rapidly, they'll be dragged offstage.

extra_info.name: Octopus and Obstacles
extra_info.description: An opponent caught in the giant octopus's tentacles will be dragged off-screen. If it seems like they might escape, you could purposefully hit a floor or wall to flick them away.

extra_info.name: Octopus Tentacles
extra_info.description: You can extend the giant octopus tentacles farther by pressing the special-move button. They'll extend and retract even if you don't do anything, but pressing the button speeds up the movement.
