tips.name: Spitball Sparky
tips.description: Release a puff of air right above you to push opponents. You can even try to KO an opponent with this if you can hit them from a high position onstage.
Meta Knight debuted in Kirby's Adventure on the NES as a mysterious figure who stood in Kirby's way as a boss but also sometimes helped him.
Meta Knight first appeared in Kirby's Adventure. He leads the Meta-Knights. He wears a mask, but when he loses to Kirby, you can see a glimpse of his true face when his mask cracks.

tips.name: Galacta Knight
tips.description: Meta Knight's seventh color variation is based on Galacta Knight, who makes an appearance in Kirby Super Star Ultra and is said to be the strongest warrior in the galaxy.

tips.name: Dark Meta Knight
tips.description: Meta Knight's eighth color variation is based on a shadow version of Meta Knight, Dark Meta Knight, who appears in Kirby & The Amazing Mirror.

tips.name: Mach Tornado
tips.description: The tornado created around Meta Knight can destroy any weak projectiles it hits.

tips.name: Drill Rush
tips.description: While Meta Knight's rushing through the air like a drill, you can adjust his direction.

extra_info.name: Drill Rush Technique
extra_info.description: If you direct Meta Knight toward the ground, he'll cancel the move and do a little hop. Use that to avoid an enemy who is about to counter you.

tips.name: Shuttle Loop
tips.description: Meta Knight thrusts his sword upward, loops in midair, and then thrusts upward once more. A swift and powerful attack, useful against falling foes.

extra_info.name: Shuttle Loop with Care
extra_info.description: This powerful attack is useful for pursuing enemies directly above you. However, you're vulnerable during the postmove descent.

extra_info.name: Shuttle Loop's Characteristics
extra_info.description: You can use the attack slightly more quickly in the air. However, the first strike is more powerful when executed from the ground.

tips.name: Dimensional Cape
tips.description: Meta Knight wraps himself in his cape and disappears. If you keep holding the button, he'll strike when he's back!

extra_info.name: Dimensional Cape Techniques
extra_info.description: While Meta Knight is invisible, he'll move in the direction you input. He won't move if you don't input a direction, which is useful as a feint.

extra_info.name: Dimensional Cape Warning
extra_info.description: While unseen, you can move in a direction you input, but if you move backward, your attack and launch power will be reduced.

tips.name: Darkness Illusion
tips.description: Lightning strikes and catches nearby opponents for a multi-hit attack. It wraps up with a side slice that sends opponents flying.

tips.name: Flurry Attack
tips.description: Press the attack button to unleash a flurry of strikes. Meta Knight doesn't care for the singular "attack."

tips.name: Third Attack's Launch Power
tips.description: Keep pressing the button to unleash three attacks! The third strike of Meta Knight's side tilt attack is powerful enough to launch opponents.

tips.name: Midair Up Sweep
tips.description: Use this move to launch an opponent at an upward diagonal angle, then follow up with another of the same attack. If you then land an up special, you can launch your opponent up even further into the air.

tips.name: Trample
tips.description: Your enemy will fly diagonally upward after this, so that's a good time to use a combo. If their damage is low, try a dash attack and then a forward air attack.

If Meta Knight uses of any[sic] his special moves in the air, he'll fall to the ground in a defenseless state afterward. He's an adept midair fighter, but you have to be careful when using his specials.
Pit's debut was in Kid Icarus, released in North America in 1987. His goal was to collect the Three Sacred Treasures and defeat the evil Medusa.

The protagonist of Kid Icarus, Pit is an angel who serves Palutena, the Goddess of Light, as captain of her royal guard. He aims to defeat Medusa, the Goddess of Darkness, by retrieving the Three Sacred Treasures.
Pit and Dark Pit are similar in some ways, but they both summon different weapons and both celebrate victory to a different tune.
When Pit grabs a Hammer, he swings it from the wrist, with only two frames of movement—just like how he used the mallets in the original Kid Icarus!

tips.name: Palutena Bow
tips.description: Press up before firing to aim the arrow that way. Also, you can guide the arrow while it's in the air.

extra_info.name: Palutena Bow's Direction and Timing
extra_info.description: The arrow fires automatically once it's fully charged, but it won't fire while Pit's changing his aim. You can delay firing that way.

tips.name: Upperdash Arm
tips.description: A dash forward followed by an uppercut. While Pit is dashing, the Upperdash Arm deflects projectiles! Cool!

extra_info.name: Upperdash Arm Techniques
extra_info.description: When used midair, it has less launching power, but you're not vulnerable for as long after the uppercut. When recovering from offstage, you can use it to rush a waiting opponent.

tips.name: Power of Flight
tips.description: Pit's wings glow and then send him flying in the direction you choose. This move travels a long way, making it an excellent recovery move.

extra_info.name: Power of Flight's Vulnerability
extra_info.description: This move travels very far, making it great for recovering. However, what goes up must come down, so use it wisely.

tips.name: Guardian Orbitars
tips.description: These handy items will block enemy attacks as well as reflect projectiles. They'll even push back foes who touch them!

extra_info.name: Guardian Orbitars' Recovery Time
extra_info.description: If the shields take too much damage, they'll break. When that happens, it'll be 10 seconds before they can be used again.

tips.name: Lightning Chariot
tips.description: Pit summons the Lightning Chariot and flies offstage. Use the reticle to aim at opponents. When the gauge inside the reticle runs out, he'll come rushing back and launch any opponents that he hits.

extra_info.name: Lightning Chariot Timing
extra_info.description: For Pit's Final Smash, take aim at an opponent and then come rushing in. If you don't want to wait for time to pass, you can trigger the move instantly with the attack or special-move buttons.

tips.name: Anti-air Attack
tips.description: A two-part kick that sends an opponent into the air. It'll send them up even if they haven't taken a lot of damage, so try to follow up with another attack afterward.

tips.name: Under-Arc Slash
tips.description: Pit swings his sword below him with a lot of power. If you hit the opponent when the sword is directly below him, it'll have a meteor effect.
In the Palutena's Temple stage, you can trigger a special smash taunt if you use Pit's down taunt for a brief instant. It'll only work once per battle.
Dark Pit debuted in Kid Icarus: Uprising, released in 2012. Created by the Mirror of Truth, he has the same abilities as Pit but a different personality.
Dark Pit appears in Kid Icarus: Uprising. He was created by the Underworld Army's Mirror of Truth. He's a copy of Pit, but was born incomplete. Palutena gave him the nickname Pittoo.
This divine weapon from Kid Icarus: Uprising was a prototype for the Palutena Bow. In that game, it had the unusual trait of doing more damage when fired from close range.

tips.name: Silver Bow
tips.description: Compared to those from Pit's Palutena Bow, these arrows disappear more quickly and can't be steered as much. But they deal more damage in return.

tips.name: Electroshock Arm
tips.description: Dark Pit charges forward and launches foes upward with great force at a lower angle than Pit's Upperdash Arm. This move also deals electrical damage.

tips.name: Dark Pit Staff
tips.description: This Final Smash shoots in a straight line, going through foes and terrain. If it hits more than one opponent, the closer foes will take more damage.
Pit and Dark Pit are similar in some ways, but they both summon different weapons and both celebrate victory to a different tune.
Samus first donned her blue Zero Suit in the 2004 title Metroid: Zero Mission.
Her blue suit, known as the Zero Suit, first appeared in Metroid: Zero Mission. In that game, Samus tries to infiltrate the Space Pirates' Mother Ship armed with nothing but a handgun.
Samus uses this in Metroid: Zero Mission when she's not wearing her Power Suit. In that game, she uses her handgun to temporarily paralyze enemies.

tips.name: Zero Suit Samus's 7th Color
tips.description: Zero Suit Samus's seventh color variation is a unique orange outfit. This outfit first appeared in certain endings of Metroid: Zero Mission.

tips.name: Paralyzer
tips.description: Attack with this to paralyze foes for just a moment. When they can't move, that's your chance to strike!

tips.name: Plasma Whip
tips.description: This move swings a whiplike stream of plasma directly in front of Zero Suit Samus. Hold down the button to raise the whip up. This move works as a follow-up attack, or a counter to aerial attacks.

extra_info.name: Grabbing Ledges with Plasma Whip
extra_info.description: If the whip reaches an edge, you'll grab on to it. Handy! You can use the whip with either grabs or your side special.

tips.name: Flip Jump
tips.description: Fighters struck while on the ground will be buried, while airborne fighters will be meteored.

extra_info.name: Flip Jump's Traits
extra_info.description: If you hit an opponent in the air with this move, it has a meteor effect. Hit an opponent on the ground to bury them. You can also press the special-move button again for an extra kick with good launch power.

extra_info.name: Flip Jump's Meteor Effect
extra_info.description: Press the attack button during this move to launch a kick. This kick deals a lot of damage and has a powerful meteor effect too.

tips.name: Zero Laser
tips.description: The laser gradually gains power and lets out a powerful burst at the end. Pay close attention to the way the reticle looks to judge when power has fully charged.

extra_info.name: Zero Laser Controls
extra_info.description: The reticle moves faster prior to the laser firing, so first decide where you want to aim it. For the last shot, the sight grows slightly larger.

tips.name: Slant Paralyzer
tips.description: This attack fires Zero Suit Samus's Paralyzer diagonally down. Its victims will be stunned for a moment, leaving them open to another attack!

tips.name: Slash Dive
tips.description: This move sends Zero Suit Samus diagonally downward while attacking. When she lands, the impact damages enemies around her.

variants.name: Wario
variants.boxing_ring_title: Scoundrel with a Fart of Gold

tips.description: Wario made his debut in 1992 as the villain of Super Mario Land 2: 6 Golden Coins, where he stole a castle from Mario.
He calls himself Mario's childhood friend and biggest rival, and he cares for nothing but money and treasure. He loves to eat, and is particularly fond of garlic.
Wario's first starring role was in Wario Land: Super Mario Land 3 in 1994. Its appeal was Wario's powerful abilities, powering up with garlic, and tackling enemies to defeat them.

tips.name: Chomp's Traits
tips.description: Like his throw moves, Chomp can't be blocked by an opponent's shield. While chewing, Wario will slowly heal damage.

extra_info.name: Chomp's Healing
extra_info.description: Can be used to bite opponents or eat items. If he eats food, it'll restore more health than usual!

extra_info.name: Chomping for Power
extra_info.description: Eating items will make Wario more gassy. The gassier he is, the stronger his down special will be.

extra_info.name: Chomp Indigestion
extra_info.description: Wario's hunger knows no bounds, but eating explosives will cause him a bit of damage. The blast will hurt those around him too!

tips.name: Repurposing Wario Bike
tips.description: Wario's motorcycle can break into parts. You can't pick them up, but Wario can eat them to heal or fill his...gas tank.

extra_info.name: Controlling Wario Bike
extra_info.description: Press up to do a wheelie and then down to lower the wheel again. Any enemy hit by that front wheel has a good chance of getting launched.

extra_info.name: Wario Bike Taunting
extra_info.description: Wario does a special taunt when he's riding his bike. It doesn't have any particular effect on the bike's power, though.

tips.name: Wario Waft
tips.description: Fully charged, it will rocket Wario into the air, launching nearby foes and totally grossing them out. This can be very handy while recovering.

extra_info.name: Wario Waft's Gas
extra_info.description: When Wario's bursting at the seams with gas, he'll glow. Getting KO'd won't release any gas, either—there's only one way to let it out...

tips.name: Wario-Man
tips.description: After transforming into Wario Man, he splits himself into copies that attack all at once. The final fart isn't affected by the charge of his down-special fart.

tips.name: Hand Slap
tips.description: While it only attacks directly above Wario, this move has a lot of power, and you aren't left vulnerable for long after landing. If you can mix this into a combo, you can expect to deal good damage.

tips.name: Shoulder Tackle
tips.description: Wario often uses this move in the Wario Land series. It's still powerful in Smash Bros., and boasts serious launching power.
Snake made his debut in Metal Gear, released in North America in 1988. It's a stealth-based game that has Snake acquiring weapons and equipment while on various missions.
Snake's job is to infiltrate enemy bases alone to stop terrorist organizations. The player has the option to stay quiet and sneak around, or go loud with guns and bombs.

tips.name: Hand Grenade
tips.description: The hand grenade explodes 2.5 seconds after the pin is pulled out. Opponents won't flinch even if the grenade hits when thrown. You can get caught in the explosion as well, so be smart.

extra_info.name: Holding Hand Grenade
extra_info.description: Pressing and holding the special-move button will allow you to move around while holding the grenade. You can move forward, backward, jump, and even perform midair jumps.

extra_info.name: Throwing the Hand Grenade
extra_info.description: Depending on the direction you tilt the control stick, you can throw the grenade near, medium, or far. Press the shield button to drop it at your feet.

tips.name: Remote Missile
tips.description: You can control the missile freely, but you cannot move around. If opponents try to attack you, press the shield button to cancel.

tips.name: Cypher
tips.description: Attack or dodge while rising up in the air to let go of the Cypher. If you take damage while airborne, you can use this move again.

tips.name: C4
tips.description: The C4 bombs that you've set can be detonated by using your down special again. When trying to recover, you can detonate it in midair to blast yourself back to the stage.

extra_info.name: C4 - Butterfly Form
extra_info.description: Sometimes Snake will use a butterfly-shaped C3 explosive instead of a C4 bomb. They may look different, but both bombs behave identically.

extra_info.name: C4 Placement
extra_info.description: You can attach C4 bombs to not only the ground, but to walls and opponents as well. The bomb will detonate automatically once enough time has passed. Don't blow yourself up!

tips.name: Covering Fire
tips.description: Snake's Final Smash allows him to lock on to opponents and shoot five missiles at them. The missiles will fire even if you are unable to lock on.

tips.name: Silent Takedown
tips.description: Snake's unique downward throw lays opponents flat on the ground. If the opponent has high damage, this can be a great setup for a KO.
Snake's taunt involves him hiding in his beloved cardboard box. When an opponent gets near and picks up the cardboard box...well...you'll just have to see for yourself.
The cardboard box you use during your taunt can also be used to deal damage to opponents when cast off. The box falls off of Snake's back, so be mindful of the direction you use it.
Ike debuted in Fire Emblem: Path of Radiance, a 2005 title for the Nintendo GameCube. He's a strong fighter who wields a two-handed sword with ease.
Leader of the Greil Mercenaries. He's stoic and doesn't say much, but he's also kind and compassionate. He's the protagonist of Fire Emblem: Path of Radiance and appears in Fire Emblem: Radiant Dawn.

tips.name: Radiance and Radiant
tips.description: Ike's odd-numbered costumes are from Fire Emblem: Path of Radiance, while the even-numbered costumes are from Fire Emblem: Radiant Dawn. The two look and sound different, but they're equally strong.

tips.name: Eruption
tips.description: Charge this move by pressing and holding the button. Additional flames appear depending on the charge level. At full charge, it sends three pillars, allowing you to hit somewhat distant opponents.

extra_info.name: Eruption at the Edge
extra_info.description: This attack reaches up and down as well as left and right. You can use it to ambush opponents who are trying to recover from offstage.

tips.name: Quick Draw
tips.description: If you hit an opponent with Quick Draw in the air, you can quickly start your next action. But if you don't do anything afterward, Ike merely falls. Keep this in mind when trying to recover.

extra_info.name: Quick Draw's Traits
extra_info.description: Using this move a bit before landing from a jump and you will reduce the amount of time you are vulnerable.

tips.name: Aether and Super Armor
tips.description: Ike will be protected from flinching while he prepares this attack. This move can send foes flying upward!

extra_info.name: Meteor with Aether
extra_info.description: During the downward strike, this attack has a meteor effect. You'll fall straight down until you hit solid ground, so make sure you aim well!

tips.name: Counter
tips.description: If an opponent attacks while Ike's in his stance, it'll nullify the attack and Ike will counterattack. The power of the counterattack is based on the power of the opponent's attack.

tips.name: Great Aether
tips.description: This sweeping move can be used on the ground or in the air, and can catch opponents in front of Ike and above Ike. The final slash can also damage nearby opponents when it hits the ground.
There are three levels of attack: a punch, a kick, and a sword swing. All pretty handy, since they're quicker than Ike's other moves!

tips.name: Cast Off
tips.description: The backward strike of Ike's down smash is most powerful at the start of the move, dealing more damage and launching a foe farther than the forward strike.

tips.name: Repositioning Slash
tips.description: This is the quickest of all Ike's air attacks, and one of the most damaging too. A valuable weapon for a fighter whose attacks tend to be slow.

tips.name: Rising Swing
tips.description: This attack covers a wide vertical and horizontal range, allowing you to hit opponents even if they're a little ways away. Try to follow up with this attack after using neutral air, down throw, or up throw.
Certain moves, such as Ike's Aether, have limits on how many times they can be used to grab on to edges without touching the ground.

variants.name: Male
variants.boxing_ring_title: Wants to Be the Very Best

variants.name: Female
variants.boxing_ring_title: Wants to Be the Very Best

tips.description: The Pokémon Trainer stands in the background and instructs Squirtle, Ivysaur, and Charizard. You can play as a female Pokémon Trainer by changing Pokémon Trainer's costume when choosing your fighter.
tips.name: Pokémon Trainer

tips.description: Pokémon Trainer will react when taunting or when a Pokémon takes damage. The girl and boy Pokémon Trainer have different reactions.

tips.name: Pokémon Trainer: Pokémon Change
tips.description: You can cycle through Pokémon from Squirtle → Ivysaur → Charizard. You can switch them as many times as you like, so choose your favorite Pokémon and get to Smashin'!

tips.name: Pokémon Trainer: Triple Finish
tips.description: The three Pokémon combine their powers for this Final Smash. The effect of the attack is the same for all three Pokémon.

extra_info.name: Squirtle: Triple Finish
extra_info.description: The three Pokémon combine their powers for this Final Smash. Squirtle's Hydro Pump is wide, covering the top and the bottom, and can draw in opponents.

extra_info.name: Ivysaur: Triple Finish
extra_info.description: The three Pokémon combine their powers for this Final Smash. Ivysaur's Solar Beam has a long range and traps opponents hit by the attack.

extra_info.name: Charizard: Triple Finish
extra_info.description: The three Pokémon combine their powers for this Final Smash. Charizard dishes out continuous Fire Blasts for massive damage.

tips.description: Squirtle debuted in Pokémon Red and Blue, released in North America in 1998. It's a Tiny Turtle Pokémon that excels at Water-type moves.
Squirtle is one of three Pokémon you can get from Professor Oak in Pokémon Red and Blue. Squirtle was one of the few Water-type Pokémon you could get until you got a Fishing Rod.

tips.name: Squirtle: Water Gun
tips.description: Squirtle shoots water from its mouth to push opponents away. It doesn't do any damage, but it's great for interfering with opponents trying to return to the stage.

extra_info.name: Ivysaur: Bullet Seed
extra_info.description: Ivysaur's Bullet Seed move not only shoots seeds, but if an opponent touches its body while it's using the move, that opponent can get launched!

extra_info.name: Ivysaur: Bullet Seed Firing Time
extra_info.description: Holding the special-move button will make Bullet Seed last longer. You'll be open to attack from the side, but if it hits, it deals a lot of damage.

extra_info.name: Charizard: Flamethrower
extra_info.description: Charizard will keep breathing fire as long as you hold the button down, but it'll get weaker and weaker as you use it.

tips.name: Squirtle: Withdraw
tips.description: Spin across the ground quickly to tackle opponents. Squirtle won't take damage while it is in its shell.

extra_info.name: Squirtle: Advancing with Withdraw
extra_info.description: Tilt the control stick in the opposite direction of the movement to shorten the distance you slide. If you hit an opponent or their shield, you will be reflected.

extra_info.name: Squirtle: Withdraw's Weakness
extra_info.description: If Squirtle gets stepped on from above, it will flip over and become completely vulnerable. Squirtle can't move until it gets back up, leaving you wide open to attack.

extra_info.name: Ivysaur: Razor Leaf
extra_info.description: Quickly flick the control stick to the side as you press the button to shoot leaves farther. The trajectory of the leaves is random. They may fly diagonally instead of straight to the side.

extra_info.name: Charizard: Flare Blitz
extra_info.description: This move engulfs Charizard in flames. It's extremely powerful, but it hurts Charizard too.

extra_info.name: Charizard: Flare Blitz
extra_info.description: There's a big explosion when Charizard hits a fighter or an obstacle. This can hit other nearby fighters too!

tips.name: Squirtle: Waterfall
tips.description: Tilt the control stick forward or backward while rising up to slightly change the angle you rise. It can be used as a recovery move, but it can also be used to attack opponents above.

extra_info.name: Ivysaur: Vine Whip
extra_info.description: Vine Whip can be used as many times as you want in midair and the direction it shoots out can be changed slightly. It can be used to grab the edge when falling off the stage.

extra_info.name: Charizard: Fly
extra_info.description: Fly doesn't have much horizontal movement, and it leaves Charizard vulnerable afterward, but it has great launch power. It can make a good counterattack after blocking an attack with your shield.

tips.description: Ivysaur debuted in Pokémon Red and Blue, released in North America in 1998. Due to the favorable matchups, Ivysaur was very useful against Rock-type and Water-type opponents in beginning Gyms.
Ivysaur is the evolved form of Bulbasaur, one of the three Pokémon you can choose in the beginning. Compared to the other two Pokémon, you can reach the third form, Venusaur, quickest.

tips.name: Ivysaur: Exploding Flower
tips.description: It has very high launch power, but the move is slow to activate. Use this move a bit early and try to predict where the opponent will land or where they will go after dodging.
Ivysaur's up smash, up air, and down air attacks all release pollen from the bud on its back. These moves excel at launching opponents.
Charizard made its debut in North America in the 1998 games Pokémon Red and Pokémon Blue. It was even featured on the cover of Pokémon Red.
Charizard, the Flame Pokémon, graced the package cover of Pokémon Red. Its fiery breath can melt anything, but it never turns its flame on an opponent weaker than itself.

tips.name: Charizard: Wing Thrust
tips.description: Charizard's wings won't take damage, making this attack good for overpowering foes who are attacking from above.

tips.name: Charizard: Aerial Tail
tips.description: In this aerial move, Charizard sweeps away opponents behind it with its long tail. The fiery tip of its tail has high attack and launch power.
Charizard can perform two successive midair jumps. The second one doesn't get as much height as the first, though.
Don't talk to Charizard or Inkling about swimming. They'll take gradual damage while they're in the water.
The small and agile Diddy Kong first appeared in the SNES title Donkey Kong Country in 1994, where he was a valuable partner to DK.

Diddy Kong appears as Donkey Kong's best friend and trusted partner in Donkey Kong Country. He lacks the power to defeat some enemies, but there's something to be said for his quick dash and high jumps.

tips.name: Peanut Popgun
tips.description: Overcharging the Peanut Popgun makes it backfire, but it'll do a ton of damage to foes that it hits!

extra_info.name: Peanut Popgun's Sign
extra_info.description: While charging, watch for when Diddy grabs his hat! That's the sign that the Peanut Popgun's about to backfire.

extra_info.name: Peanut Popgun Projectiles
extra_info.description: The peanuts you fire can be caught by enemies if they time it right. Charge the move to make the peanuts harder to catch.

tips.name: Monkey Flip
tips.description: If you grab foes in the air, you'll release them more quickly than if you grab them on the ground. Be ready to use a speedy follow-up attack.

extra_info.name: Monkey Flip to Flying Kick
extra_info.description: While performing this move, you can press the attack button to launch a flying kick that deals considerable damage and has good launching power.

extra_info.name: Uses for Monkey Flip
extra_info.description: After grabbing on to opponents with this move, press the attack button to launch them sideways or press up to jump off their head!

extra_info.name: Monkey Flip Technique
extra_info.description: You can use this move again in the air after jumping off the first enemy you attack. Useful for when you're fighting a lot of enemies...

extra_info.name: Monkey Flip's Characteristics
extra_info.description: If you use this move without triggering the flying kick part, you can use Rocketbarrel Boost! It's a great way to recover if you've been launched!

tips.name: Steering Rocketbarrel Boost
tips.description: You can indicate a direction while this is charging to fly in that direction. You can even steer while flying!

extra_info.name: Rocketbarrel Boost
extra_info.description: If someone attacks Diddy Kong when he's wearing his Rocketbarrel Pack, it'll break free and fly off on its own.

extra_info.name: Warnings for Rocketbarrel Boost
extra_info.description: You can also move horizontally. You'll go right through enemies even if they're shielding, but if you go over the edge, you're done for!

extra_info.name: Rocketbarrel Boost Techniques
extra_info.description: If you fly up through a platform and land on it, the Rocketbarrel Pack will explode on contact with the platform, blasting nearby enemies.

extra_info.name: Rocketbarrel Boost Cut Loose
extra_info.description: If Diddy Kong is hit while using this move, the Rocketbarrel will fly off on its own. You can control it slightly using the control stick.

tips.name: Banana Peel
tips.description: In this move, Diddy throws a banana peel behind him. Foes who step on it fall clumsily to the ground. Have a nice trip!

extra_info.name: Banana Peel
extra_info.description: You can't whip out another banana peel if one of yours is still in play, but each one will vanish after being thrown twice.

extra_info.name: Items and Banana Peel
extra_info.description: If you're holding an item when you throw the peel, you'll throw both at the same time. However, if it's a two-handed item, you won't throw it.

tips.name: Hyper Rocketbarrel
tips.description: Diddy Kong flies around the screen in all directions using the latest Rocketbarrel technology. For the final hit, it'll target the opponent who was hit the most, but it'll catch nearby fighters as well.

tips.name: Hand Clap
tips.description: It's a quick move that doesn't leave Diddy Kong vulnerable long, making it easy to use on the ground as a diversion. You also have a chance for a follow-up attack (such as an up smash) after it hits.

tips.name: Screw Kick
tips.description: With long reach and long hit time, this move is good at restricting an opponent's actions. It can be used as a diversion in the air, or even as an attack after dashing into a short hop.
The main protagonist from Mother 3. He lived in Tazmily Village with his parents and twin brother Claus, until a fire led to tragedy. He sets off on adventure with his telekinetic PSI powers.

tips.name: PK Freeze's Traits
tips.description: The icy projectile can freeze enemies. Try using it to stop anybody returning to the stage!

extra_info.name: PK Freeze's Duration
extra_info.description: Hold down the button and a direction to extend the projectile's arc, but remember, the ice only bursts once you let go, so plan carefully!

tips.name: PK Fire's Traits
tips.description: Shoot a blast of fire straight forward. Once it connects, it will become pillars of fire. A short-range attack that is great for hitting and launching multiple enemies.

extra_info.name: A PK Fire Diversion
extra_info.description: This move can disrupt incoming attacks and projectiles, but it leaves Lucas vulnerable for a time. Be careful!

tips.name: PK Thunder's Traits
tips.description: This move can help you return to the battle. Be careful, though, since the orb vanishes if it runs into solid ground.

extra_info.name: Multiple Attacks with PK Thunder
extra_info.description: The orb from this attack won't disappear when it hits your rivals. Shock multiple opponents!

tips.name: PSI Magnet
tips.description: This move lets Lucas absorb energy-based projectiles and heal himself. It'll even slow his descent while airborne!

extra_info.name: PSI Magnet - Movement and Disruption
extra_info.description: After grabbing a projectile with this move, Lucas can roll backward. By releasing the button at the right time, you can also disrupt enemy attacks.

tips.name: PK Starstorm
tips.description: With Kumatora and Boney, a shower of meteors descend toward Lucas. You can press up or down on the control stick to slightly raise or lower the position of where the meteors cross paths.

tips.name: PK Smash Geyser's Pros and Cons
tips.description: This attack has very high launching power compared to many other moves. However, it will leave you wide open after using it, and envious enemies may punish you for missing.

extra_info.name: PK Smash Geyser's Traits
extra_info.description: This attack will move upward when you use it. It's strongest if you catch an enemy with the start. Not even enemies hanging on an edge are safe!

tips.name: PK Blow
tips.description: Lucas fires three PSI blasts that are good for foes on ledges or under you. Each one is weaker than the last, but the range increases.

tips.name: PK Meteor Kick
tips.description: This powerful kick has a meteor effect. Try to hit with Lucas's knee for the best result!

tips.name: PK Foot Stomp
tips.description: Unleash a sequence of four kicks downward. Start the attack at the peak of a jump to make it easier to land all four blows. The last kick has a meteor effect!

tips.name: Rope Snake's Traits
tips.description: The Rope Snake extends Lucas's reach for grabbing foes and edges. It can reach especially far in midair.

extra_info.name: Midair Rope Snake
extra_info.description: You can't grab and throw opponents in midair, but don't worry—this attack will still hurt them!

tips.description: Sonic's first appearance was a cameo in the 1991 arcade racing game Rad Mobile. You can see him dangling near the top of the windshield.
His first starring role was at blistering speeds in the side-scrolling action game Sonic The Hedgehog. The high-speed action unfolds using simple controls with only directional buttons and jumping.

tips.name: Homing Attack
tips.description: Sonic rises into the air while charging the move. Once charged, it'll automatically attack. You can press the button midway to instantly attack, but if you miss an opponent, you're left vulnerable for a while.

tips.name: Spin Dash
tips.description: When Spin Dash is used on the ground, Sonic becomes invincible for a brief period of time right after he starts rolling. You can use this to avoid an opponent's attack and counterattack!

tips.name: Spring Jump
tips.description: Use this in midair to drop a spring on someone below. Give yourself a boost while disrupting your rivals!

extra_info.name: Spring Jump Care
extra_info.description: Even after a Spring Jump, Sonic can still attack and dodge in the air. However, he can't perform a midair jump or use any more special moves.

tips.name: Spin Charge
tips.description: If you jump while spinning forward, you'll keep spinning in the air. You can hit opponents like that or use other attacks.

extra_info.name: Boosting Spin Charge
extra_info.description: If you hold down and keep tapping the button, you can charge up this move, making it faster and stronger.

extra_info.name: Spin Charge Care
extra_info.description: Jump while charging a Spin Charge to do a Spin Jump. After a midair jump, you won't be able to Spin Jump until you hit the ground.

tips.name: Super Sonic
tips.description: Sonic transforms into Super Sonic and bashes into opponents at high speeds. The moment his transformation ends can also launch nearby opponents.

tips.name: Shooting Star Kick
tips.description: If you jump, Spring Jump, and then use an attack like this one in quick succession, it will end right before you land, which means no landing delay!
#1 is Sonic, #2 is Captain Falcon, and #3 is Little Mac. In contrast, the slowest of all the fighters is Incineroar.
King Dedede debuted alongside Kirby in the 1992 title Kirby's Dream Land. He's a big eater who wants all the food in Dream Land for himself!
The mastermind who stole all the food in Kirby's Dream Land. He calls himself the ruler of Dream Land and gets the Waddle Dees and others to serve him.
King Dedede appeared in this form in Revenge of the King from Kirby Super Star Ultra. Rather than his usual wooden hammer, he's equipped with a mechanical New Dedede Hammer.

tips.name: Inhale
tips.description: After inhaling opponents, press the button again to spit them out as a projectile. This way, you can hit your opponents with each other!

extra_info.name: Inhale
extra_info.description: Dedede is already perfect, so his Inhale won't copy abilities. Instead, it has a longer range than Kirby's.

extra_info.name: Inhaling Projectiles
extra_info.description: You can inhale an opponent's projectiles, then spit them back out at them. The projectiles you spit back out have higher attack power than normal.

extra_info.name: Inhale Techniques
extra_info.description: The Gordo that appears when using a side special can also be inhaled and spit back out. You're not vulnerable for long after spitting it out, so you can use it for a combo too.

tips.name: Gordo Throw
tips.description: Gordos will stick to any walls they hit. Try doing this on walls close to edges to cause serious trouble to enemies trying to climb up.

extra_info.name: Returning Gordo
extra_info.description: With the right timing, an opponent can hit a Gordo back at you. But then you can attack it or hit it with your side special again, and now a fierce battle has turned into a game of Gordo tennis.

extra_info.name: Gordo Throw's Many Tricks
extra_info.description: Choose up, middle, or down right after starting this move to hit the Gordo in three different ways, changing how it bounces.

tips.name: Super Dedede Jump
tips.description: After leaping up high, Dedede crashes down in a crushing body slam. This move can be canceled by the special-move button or up on the control stick.

tips.name: Jet Hammer
tips.description: It's possible to charge this move to deal more damage, but charging for too long will start to hurt Dedede too!

extra_info.name: Jet Hammer's Characteristics
extra_info.description: If King Dedede's damage goes over 100%, he can keep charging this move past maximum without taking damage.

tips.name: Dede-rush
tips.description: Stuck in a cage match, an opponent is subjected to Dedede Missiles and then a powerful Hammer attack. It's an over-the-top Final Smash that launches the opponent as well as the chain-link cage.

tips.name: Rolling
tips.description: He can attack while prone by cartwheeling forward. This lets him attack while moving, like a dash attack.

tips.name: Belly Flop
tips.description: This attack leaves King Dedede wide open to attacks, but it can also launch opponents very far. King Dedede's been using this move since the first game in the series, Kirby's Dream Land.
King Dedede can float after taking in a big gulp of air. This move is the result of lots of hard work and training so he could put up a fight against Kirby after previously losing to him.
Olimar made his first appearance in the original Pikmin, released in 2001. He's a hardworking space pilot for Hocotate Freight.
Alph first appeared in the 2013 title Pikmin 3. He's a cheerful and optimistic engineer who loves his job.
tips.name: Alph's Origins

tips.description: The S.S. Dolphin runs into trouble during a space vacation, causing it to crash-land on an unexplored planet. Olimar tries to repair the ship using the help of the Pikmin he meets there.
Olimar came up with the name "Pikmin" because the creatures look similar to a favorite vegetable of his, Pikpik carrots.

tips.name: Pikmin Pluck
tips.description: Olimar will always pluck the Pikmin in this order: red, yellow, blue, white, purple.

extra_info.name: Pikmin Pluck's Limit
extra_info.description: You can have up to three Pikmin in tow at any time. Plucking for more while three are already active is, sadly, futile.

tips.name: Pikmin Throw
tips.description: Most Pikmin latch on to opponents and attack them repeatedly. Purple Pikmin, though, deal one heavy blow and then bounce off.

extra_info.name: Pikmin Hang Time
extra_info.description: The less damage an opponent has accumulated, the longer the Pikmin can hang on to it.

tips.name: Pikmin Order
tips.description: This move will change the order of your Pikmin. Use it to plan your attack strategy.

extra_info.name: Orderly Pikmin
extra_info.description: Olimar's whistle calls back his scattered Pikmin. Even the ones that are goofing off.

extra_info.name: Super Armor with Pikmin Order
extra_info.description: This move gives Olimar super armor at the start of the move, so it's great not only for calling Pikmin, but also to avoid being launched by an opponent's attacks.

tips.name: Winged Pikmin
tips.description: The number of Pikmin you have will affect how high up you rise. Also, White Pikmin are light and Purple Pikmin are heavy. These characteristics affect how high you go, too.

tips.name: End of Day
tips.description: When Olimar jumps into the Hocotate Ship, nearby enemies will be buried. If the ship hits anyone while it's airborne, they'll be slammed to the ground.

extra_info.name: Starship Landing Site
extra_info.description: You can adjust the trajectory of the Hocotate Ship as it comes back down. If you aim it well, you can deal some serious damage to your opponents.

tips.description: All three of Olimar's smash attacks count as projectiles, which can be reflected by moves like Reflector.

tips.name: Pikmin Left/Right Dash
tips.description: If there are at least two Pikmin with Olimar, they'll attack on both sides of him. If there's only one, it'll attack in front of him.

tips.name: Tornado Attack
tips.description: Olimar attacks by twirling. It's his only aerial attack that doesn't use any Pikmin, so you can use it even if all your Pikmin are gone.
Olimar always attacks with the Pikmin at the front of the line. You can tell which one this is by the arrow pointing to it.
When separated from Olimar, Pikmin are vulnerable to enemy attacks.
After being plucked, it takes some time for the flower on a Pikmin's head to bloom. As the flower grows, the Pikmin will move faster!
Red Pikmin perform attacks that deal 1.4 times the standard damage. They're also immune to fire attacks, but their throw is weak.
Attacks performed using Purple Pikmin deal 1.6 times the standard damage and launch foes farther too! They're lacking in reach and speed, however.
Yellow Pikmin attacks have 1.5 times the reach of the other colors. The Pikmin can't be killed by electric attacks.
Throws performed with Blue Pikmin deal 1.7 times the standard damage. The rest of their abilities are average, with no real weaknesses.
White Pikmin that stick to opponents will deal twice the normal amount of damage. They're quite speedy and can jump very high.
Blue Pikmin can survive water in Smash Bros., just like they can in games from the Pikmin series. Other colors of Pikmin? Not so much.
Lucario first appeared in Pokémon Diamond and Pearl. Lucario has the power to detect its opponent's thoughts and actions by reading their Aura.

Lucario is the evolved form of Riolu, the Emanation Pokémon, and can detect Aura from living things. It's said that Lucario can use this power to learn someone's hiding place or understand what people are saying.

tips.name: Aura Sphere
tips.description: Aura Sphere can damage opponents while it's charging. Try dropping onto a foe from above while charging it!

tips.name: Force Palm
tips.description: Force Palm deals more damage and has a greater range if Lucario's Aura is stronger.

extra_info.name: Force Palm Throws
extra_info.description: If used close to opponents, it will become a throw. It has high launching power, so you might be able to land a one-hit KO if you have enough Aura charged up.

extra_info.name: Projectiles and Force Palm
extra_info.description: Force Palm becomes a projectile against opponents when used from a distance. It has a risk of being reflected. The more Aura you have charged up, the more reach and power the move will have.

tips.name: Extreme Speed
tips.description: This move will send Lucario flying in the direction you indicate. The more damage Lucario has taken, the farther it will fly.

extra_info.name: Launching with Extreme Speed
extra_info.description: The end of this move launches nearby foes. With enough Aura, you can dash in and attack from far away, but try not to zoom right off the screen!

tips.name: Aura Storm
tips.description: Lucario will use Aura Storm downward from above. You can adjust the beam left or right while shooting it.
Thanks to Aura, an injured Lucario can pull off this move more quickly and do a lot more damage than other fighters can.
Lucario's Aura power increases as it takes damage. At 190%, Lucario's attacks are 2.5 times normal strength, but it's possible to grow even stronger...
R.O.B. made his debut in 1985 as a peripheral for the NES. The ability to move a real robot using in-game actions made a splash with media and gamers alike.
A robot toy that worked with the NES using similar technology to the Zapper. It was supported by two games: Stack-Up and Gyromite. The robot's model number is HVC-012.

tips.name: Color Variations
tips.description: R.O.B.'s red-and-white second color is based on the Japan version of R.O.B. In fact, it's even his default color when the language is set to Japanese!

tips.name: Robo Beam
tips.description: After charging, R.O.B. fires a beam from his eyes. The red light on his head shows the charge level.

extra_info.name: Aiming the Robo Beam
extra_info.description: The beam can be angled up or down. Also, if it hits the ground, it'll reflect and change direction.

extra_info.name: Pointblank Robo Beam
extra_info.description: You can't fire a beam if you don't have any energy charged, but the sparks will still harm enemies right in front of you!

extra_info.name: Charging Robo Beam
extra_info.description: The beam will constantly charge while it's not in use, reaching three levels of power. R.O.B. starts each battle at the second charge level.

extra_info.name: Robo Beam Path Changes
extra_info.description: If the beam is fired at a low angle toward the terrain, it'll bounce once. If fired at a more extreme angle, the beam will disappear.

tips.name: Arm Rotor
tips.description: The spinning arms can deflect enemy projectiles. Also, press the button repeatedly for a slightly longer attack!

tips.name: Robo Burner
tips.description: The Robo Burner's fuel slowly replenishes while R.O.B. is on the ground. Be careful if you find yourself in the air a lot—it won't refill there.

extra_info.name: Robo Burner Fuel Consumption
extra_info.description: R.O.B. will use his burner to fly up. Don't jump—just press the special- move button repeatedly to save fuel and let yourself fly longer.

extra_info.name: Robo Burner Care
extra_info.description: The Robo Burner won't recharge while R.O.B. is hanging from an edge. You'll need to spend some time on the ground to make sure you're fueled!

extra_info.name: Canceling Robo Burner
extra_info.description: Even if you stop midflight or attack, you can still use Robo Burner as long as it has fuel. Good for chasing foes off the edge to finish them!

tips.name: Gyro
tips.description: If you shoot out a gyro, you won't be able to shoot out another gyro while the first one is still present in the stage. If it's still lying around, go and pick it up!

tips.name: Guided Robo Beam
tips.description: Shoot quick guided laser beams and then shoot an enormous laser beam. The laser beam can be moved up or down. Hit as many opponents as you can!

tips.name: AirN
tips.description: Attack 360 degrees with the burner. If the attack lands, you'll be able to act again quickly, so you can use it after short hopping to transition into the next attack.

tips.name: Air Boosters
tips.description: When R.O.B. fires the burner on his base, the force of the attack will move him forward a bit.
Toon Link debuted in the Nintendo GameCube title The Legend of Zelda: The Wind Waker, released in North America in 2003. The cel-shaded art style cast Link in a whole new light.
The Legend of Zelda: The Wind Waker starts on Link's birthday on Outset Island. He rides a ship named The King of Red Lions and sets out on a journey across the sea.

tips.name: Hero's Bow
tips.description: You can attack immediately after using this move, so take advantage of that to really surprise your opponents. Fire while in the air with no fear of downtime when you land.

extra_info.name: Hero's Bow Techniques
extra_info.description: If you don't charge the attack, the arrow won't fly very far and will drop toward the ground. Do this close to an edge to attack enemies below!

tips.name: Boomerang
tips.description: The boomerang is able to go through enemies as it returns. It flies to wherever you are, so move up or down after you throw it to hit more opponents!

tips.name: Bomb
tips.description: If the bomb hits an opponent, Toon Link will not be damaged by the explosion. However, if the bomb hits the ground or time passes and the bomb explodes, Toon Link will be damaged by the explosion.

tips.name: Triforce Slash
tips.description: Toon Link shoots out the Triforce with his left hand to trap opponents, and then strikes them repeatedly with his sword. The attack won't hit if there is a wall between Toon Link and the opponent.

tips.name: Half-Moon Slash
tips.description: It doesn't leave you vulnerable and can be used repeatedly, so it's great for juggling. Once the opponent is in the air, follow up with an up smash or an air attack.

tips.name: Two Part Slash
tips.description: Toon Link swings his sword in front and behind. The attack doesn't have much power, but it's quick enough to use repeatedly in a short time frame.

tips.name: Sword Plant
tips.description: Unlike Link's and Young Link's down air attack, Toon Link's doesn't bounce up when it hits a foe. If you try to dunk someone with it, you're gonna have a bad time.

tips.name: Midair Hookshot
tips.description: Using the grab button will fire the Hookshot even in midair, but you can't grab opponents with it. You can, however, use it to attack or grab edges.

extra_info.name: Midair Hookshot Techniques
extra_info.description: Land with the Hookshot out, and you'll put it away quickly. It's handy as a feint—use it just before landing, and then surprise 'em with a different attack.

tips.description: Toon Link's shield blocks lower down than Link's and Young Link's and can better cover his smaller body. However, he can't guard while moving faster than a walk.
Wolf's debut was in Star Fox 64, released in 1997. His first appearance would have been in Star Fox 2, but that title was delayed a bit.
Wolf is an ace pilot and leader of the elite team Star Wolf. He's been known to work for the evil Andross. Wolf not only has a connection to Fox, but also to Fox's father James.

tips.name: Blaster
tips.description: A two-part attack where you first attack with the blade on the Blaster and then fire a shot. Use it near an opponent to hit with both. The attack is powerful, but you cannot continuously fire.

tips.name: Wolf Flash
tips.description: Input up or down with the control stick to slightly change the direction you dash forward. If the claw makes contact, it hits hard. The center area has a powerful meteor effect!

tips.name: Fire Wolf
tips.description: Input a direction to make Wolf move in that direction while attacking. He doesn't go far, but it has great launching power, making it great for KOs.

tips.name: Reflector
tips.description: Projectiles opponents fire will be reflected back at them with more speed and power. Opponents directly hit with the Reflector will be thrown slightly upward.

tips.name: Team Star Wolf
tips.description: Team Star Wolf launch a coordinated attack on the targeted fighters. Wolf's dialogue changes if Fox or Falco have been targeted.
The Villager debuted in Animal Crossing, released in North America in 2002. In Animal Crossing, players enjoyed a peaceful village life with a variety of animal neighbors.
The main character from Animal Crossing. The Villager lives a free, slow life in a world populated by many different animals. These animals speak a unique language called Animalese.
tips.name: In Their Series
Pocket
tips.description: The Villager uses this move to pocket items or incoming projectiles. Press the button again to bring the pocketed item back out.

tips.name: Lloid Rocket
tips.description: Hold the button when using this move to ride on the Lloid Rocket.

extra_info.name: Lloid Rocket's Power
extra_info.description: The Lloid Rocket is more powerful when you're riding it, but you can bail at any time just by pressing the button.

tips.name: Balloon Trip
tips.description: The Villager's up special Balloon Trip is named after a mode from the NES game Balloon Fight.

extra_info.name: Balloon Trip's Balloons
extra_info.description: Your balloons can be popped if hit. If you lose one, you'll lose lift. If you lose both, down you go!

tips.name: Timber
tips.description: A three-stage move: planting a seed, watering it, and then chopping down the tree. Don't use the first stage in the air—it'll just leave you wide open.

extra_info.name: Timber Techniques
extra_info.description: The Villager can move sideways while using the watering can. It's also possible to water and chop down trees planted by other Villagers.

extra_info.name: Timber
extra_info.description: The axe is surprisingly powerful! You can swing it at your rivals as long as the tree is standing.

extra_info.name: If a Tree Falls in a Battle...
extra_info.description: If the felled tree hits an opponent, it deals some serious damage. The wood that pops out as it falls can be thrown as a projectile, too.

tips.name: Dream Home
tips.description: Tom Nook rushes over and builds a house around the opponent. If you look closely, you can see the Villager giving Tom Nook Bells, the currency of the Animal Crossing games.

tips.name: Bowling Ball
tips.description: If you stand next to a cliff above your opponent, you can use this smash attack to drop a bowling ball on them from above like a projectile.

tips.name: Dig
tips.description: Attack forward and backward with a shovel, and bury any opponent hit with the attack in the ground. Once you bury them, follow up with a powerful attack.

tips.name: Downward Turnips
tips.description: One to three turnips will appear. More turnips means more damage, as well as a stronger meteor effect.

tips.name: Net
tips.description: Swing the net over an item to put the item in your pocket. You can then retrieve the item from your pocket at any time by pressing the special-move button.
Mega Man made his 1987 debut in the NES game of the same name, in which the weapons acquired from defeating each boss were the key to victory.
Mega Man was created as a lab assistant by the scientist Dr. Light. He volunteered to be converted to a fighting robot to fight for justice. Solar energy powers his movement.
Since its debut in 1987, the Mega Man series as a whole has sold more than 32 million copies worldwide.

tips.name: Metal Blade
tips.description: Mega Man's neutral special, Metal Blade, can throw circular saws in any of eight directions. It was the special weapon of Metal Man in Mega Man 2.

extra_info.name: Metal Blade as an Item
extra_info.description: The Metal Blade will deal less damage if it gets stuck in the ground and is then picked up and used as a weapon. Also, your foes can pick it up!

tips.name: Crash Bomber
tips.description: Mega Man's side special Crash Bomber fires an explosive that sticks to walls and fighters. It was originally Crash Man's special weapon in Mega Man 2.

extra_info.name: Crash Bomber Connection
extra_info.description: This bomb is incredibly clingy, latching on to walls, floors, and even fighters! It can be given to other fighters by bumping into them.

tips.name: Rush Coil
tips.description: Mega Man's up special Rush Coil lets him leap to great heights with a little help from Rush, his robot-dog partner. Rush debuted in Mega Man 3.

extra_info.name: Midair Jump after Rush Coil
extra_info.description: After using Rush Coil you can move around freely and even perform a midair jump. When trying to return to the stage, you may want to use Rush Coil before using your midair jump.

extra_info.name: Rush Coil's Downtime
extra_info.description: When Rush is standing there after you use this move, he'll bounce you higher than when you first use it. Other players can jump on him too!

tips.name: Leaf Shield
tips.description: Mega Man's down special is Leaf Shield, a rotating barrier of leaves. It was originally the special weapon of Wood Man, a boss in Mega Man 2.

tips.name: Mega Legends
tips.description: Mega Man, X, MegaMan Volnutt, MegaMan.EXE, Star Force Mega Man, Proto Man, and Bass all appear in Mega Man's Final Smash.

extra_info.name: Mega Legends Timing
extra_info.description: The Black Hole Bomb will explode when it hits a foe, or you can blow it up by pressing the special-move button. Use that method to draw in nearby foes.

tips.move: Neutral Attack / Side Tilt Attack / Neutral Air Attack
tips.name: The Mega Buster
tips.description: Mega Man's neutral attack, side tilt attack, and neutral air attack all fire the Mega Buster—his main weapon ever since his first game.

Mega Man fires solar bullets made of hyper-compressed solar energy from his Mega Buster. Both arms can transform into a Buster, but the energy is so powerful that only one Buster can be used at a time.

tips.name: Mega Upper
tips.description: Mega Man's up tilt attack is Mega Upper, a move he uses in games like Mega Man 2: The Power Fighters.

extra_info.name: A Clean Mega Upper
extra_info.description: The start of this attack strongly launches foes straight upward. It leaves you quite open if you miss, but a clean hit can really turn things around!

tips.name: Sliding
tips.description: Mega Man's down tilt attack is simply called Sliding. It's based on the nonoffensive technique he's been able to use since Mega Man 3.

tips.name: Charge Shot
tips.description: Mega Man's side smash is the Charge Shot, an attack he gained in Mega Man 4 that lets his Mega Buster build up energy before firing.

extra_info.name: Charging Charge Shot
extra_info.description: Mega Man unleashes a powerful Charge Shot. The damage and distance increase the longer you charge it!

tips.name: Spark Shock
tips.description: Mega Man's up smash is the electrifying Spark Shock. He originally obtained this special weapon by defeating Spark Man, a boss in Mega Man 3.

tips.name: Flame Blast
tips.description: Mega Man's down smash is Flame Blast, which creates twin pillars of fire from his hands. He got this special weapon from Flame Man in Mega Man 6.

tips.name: Flame Sword
tips.description: Mega Man's forward air attack is a decisive strike with his Flame Sword. He obtained this special weapon by defeating Sword Man in Mega Man 8.

tips.name: Slash Claw
tips.description: Mega Man's back air attack is the multihitting Slash Claw. He obtained this special weapon by beating the boss Slash Man in Mega Man 7.

tips.name: Air Shooter
tips.description: Mega Man's up air attack is Air Shooter, which fires a tornado upward. He obtained this special weapon by defeating Air Man in Mega Man 2.

extra_info.name: Air Shooter's Tornado
extra_info.description: Mega Man can launch a little tornado upward. If opponents get caught in this minigale, they'll be carried up into the air!

tips.name: Hard Knuckle
tips.description: Mega Man's down air attack is Hard Knuckle, which fires his fist downward like a rocket. It was the special weapon of Hard Man in Mega Man 3.

extra_info.name: Hard Knuckle Meteor
extra_info.description: After Mega Man's fist flies a little way from his body, it has a meteor effect. To send foes flying down, you'll want to leave a little distance.

tips.name: Top Spin
tips.description: Mega Man's dash attack is a whirlwind of pain called Top Spin. It was originally a special weapon he acquired from Top Man, a boss in Mega Man 3.

tips.name: Super Arm
tips.description: Mega Man's grab is the Super Arm, a special weapon that lifts the heaviest of foes with ease. He got it from Guts Man in the very first Mega Man game.
If you use a move where both of Mega Man's arms turn into Busters, his arms will overheat and Mega Man will release heat from his arms after the move is complete.
Wii Fit Trainer debuted in the title Wii Fit, released in North America in 2008. Players could choose a male or female trainer to guide them through their fitness routines.
tips.name: Wii Fit Trainer's Origins

tips.description: There is a female version and a male version of the Wii Fit Trainer. In the Wii Fit games, they act as a fitness coach. Sometimes they run late, oversleep, or have the other trainer fill in for them.
tips.name: In Their Series
Sun Salutation
tips.description: Fully charge the attack before striking to recover a bit of health. Talk about flexible!

tips.name: Header
tips.description: The ball's trajectory depends on when you press the button again. You can also attack with the headbutt directly, which has a meteor effect.

extra_info.name: Canceling Header
extra_info.description: Shield before hitting the ball to cancel your serve and let the ball fall, dejected, to the ground.

tips.name: Super Hoop
tips.description: Press the button repeatedly to float up higher, making recovery easier. If foes are trying to stop you getting back to the stage, just fly out of reach!

tips.name: Deep Breathing
tips.description: Deep Breathing can reduce stress. Oh, it also heals a small amount of damage and increases your attack power, launch power, and movement speed.

extra_info.name: Timing Deep Breathing
extra_info.description: If you press the button again just as the outer circle meets the red inner circle, you'll give yourself a boost. However, the circle moves slower if used repeatedly, and it can leave you vulnerable.

tips.name: Wii Fit
tips.description: Wii Fit Trainer unleashes a wave of silhouettes that can push opponents off the stage. There are 10 different silhouettes.

tips.name: Lunge
tips.description: The third neutral attack in the sequence can bury your opponents in the ground for a time.

tips.name: Dancer
tips.description: It's not just the front arm thrust that deals damage—the leg raised up behind is also a powerful attack that launches foes straight up.

tips.name: Tree
tips.description: You're invincible for a moment just before unleashing this. Try using it to launch foes attacking from above.

tips.name: Arm & Leg Lift
tips.description: Launch opponents at a low angle. If these moves have been strengthened, you can sometimes even KO opponents who aren't significantly damaged.

tips.name: Single-Leg Extension
tips.description: You can deal damage with the extended back leg. It actually has a small meteor effect.
Rosalina and Luma made their debut in the 2007 title Super Mario Galaxy. Rosalina is a mysterious galactic traveler, and the Lumas her companions.
The keeper of the Comet Observatory, which floats in the middle of the galaxy. She has a mysterious backstory and travels with the Lumas until they turn into shooting stars.
Lumas come in various colors, but their abilities are identical. The six colors you can get are yellow, red, green, blue, cream, and Polari.

tips.name: Luma Shot
tips.description: This sends Luma hurtling forward with a high chance of launching opponents. Charge up this move to send Luma flying farther.

extra_info.name: Luma Shot Charges Forth
extra_info.description: Luma keeps going for a bit after it hits a foe, but it slows down and deals less damage.

extra_info.name: Robust Luma
extra_info.description: It's harder to launch Luma when it's sent out with Luma Shot than it is when Luma is near Rosalina. Try sending Luma away occasionally.

extra_info.name: Luma's Direction
extra_info.description: Luma sent out with Luma Shot will automatically face a nearby opponent and attack them when using moves such as neutral attack.

extra_info.name: Rebound Blows
extra_info.description: When it's on the way back after Luma Shot, Luma can attack as normal. Use this to surprise fighters heading for Rosalina or as a diversion.

tips.name: Star Bits
tips.description: Luma fires Star Bits that can scatter in three directions: high, middle, and low.

tips.name: Launch Star
tips.description: If you input backward just before Rosalina takes off, she'll fly straight up. Useful when you need as much height as possible.

tips.name: Gravitational Pull
tips.description: This move pulls in projectiles from a wide area around Rosalina. Use it to pull Ness's PK Thunder off course when he's trying to recover!

extra_info.name: Pulling in Items
extra_info.description: Rosalina and Luma's down special draws in items so Rosalina can pick them up. It can be used to nullify a number of projectiles, but it's especially useful for gaining ownership of items thrown at you.

tips.name: Grand Star
tips.description: The Grand Star scatters shooting stars across the stage. Eventually, the Grand Star will increase in size and explode. Opponents hit with the shooting stars will be drawn closer to the Grand Star.
You can attack with Luma even when Rosalina can't move, but you won't be able to while being thrown or right after being launched.
If Luma is KO'd, the time it takes for Luma to regenerate depends on how many opponents are in the match. It takes longer when it's 1 vs. 1, and shorter when it's a four-player battle.
When Luma has only a little bit of health it will look down and act tired. In this state, Luma will travel back slower to Rosalina when she uses a neutral special.
When Luma is next to Rosalina, it starts dancing if she grabs an opponent. When she gets hurt, you can see it flailing its little arms in panic!
Even though Punch-Out!! was released in 1984, Little Mac didn't receive his name until the 1987 NES release.
The main character from the Punch-Out!! game. Little Mac is 17 years old and from the Bronx in the United States. He aims to be the champion and trains to defeat opponents bigger than him.
At high damage levels, bruises and a bandage appear on Little Mac's face. Also, since he's a boxer, he never attacks using his legs.

tips.name: Straight Lunge
tips.description: This attack gives you super armor while charging up and while dashing forward. This is especially prominent when it's fully charged.

extra_info.name: Flipped Straight Lunge
extra_info.description: When unleashing a Straight Lunge, tilt the control stick toward Little Mac's backside to change the direction of the move regardless of how much it's been charged.

extra_info.name: Charging KO Uppercut
extra_info.description: Little Mac's KO Meter builds up when he deals or receives damage. Once it's full, he can use the KO Uppercut to deal some serious hurt!

tips.name: Jolt Haymaker
tips.description: You get a window of invincibility if you start this move on the ground, but not in the air. So be careful!

extra_info.name: Jolt Haymaker Warning
extra_info.description: You can move around after using the move in the air, but you can't use it again until you land. Be careful when using it to return to the stage.

extra_info.name: Jolt Haymaker's Movement Range
extra_info.description: Press the button during the jump, and you can deliver the punch more quickly. You won't move as far, though, so consider that before using it.

tips.name: Rising Uppercut
tips.description: Little Mac zooms upward with a powerful uppercut. He travels farther when used on the ground than when used in the air.

tips.name: Slip Counter
tips.description: Just when you think you've got him, Little Mac socks you with a nasty counter. He's invincible during the attack, so there's no stopping him!

tips.name: Giga Mac Rush
tips.description: Transform into Giga Mac and unleash a rapid series of punches. The final uppercut is incredibly powerful.

tips.name: Straight Smash
tips.description: If you press up when performing Little Mac's side smash, it will change into an uppercut that sends enemies flying upward.

extra_info.name: Angled Straight Smash
extra_info.description: Little Mac's side smash changes if you tilt the stick up or down immediately after inputting the command. Tilting the stick up will launch opponents upward, and tilting it down will deal more damage.

tips.name: Weak Jab
tips.description: Little Mac's neutral air attack is the fastest of his aerial attacks. It doesn't deal much damage, and it has low launch potential, but it's quick and doesn't leave you vulnerable after using it.
If you get launched after the Power Meter is full or some time has passed, the Power Meter will be emptied. Use it or lose it!
Greninja, the final evolved form of Froakie, made its debut in 2013's Pokémon X and Pokémon Y. What looks like a scarf is actually its tongue!
Greninja is a Ninja Pokémon that uses attacks like Spikes and Double Team. Water Shuriken, an attack using compressed water, was created specifically for Greninja.

tips.name: Water Shuriken
tips.description: The longer this attack is charged, the shorter its range is. The uncharged version can be more useful for keeping a foe from approaching.

tips.name: Shadow Sneak
tips.description: Greninja instantly travels to the location of the shadow and ambushes nearby opponents. Press and hold the button to make the shadow travel farther.

tips.name: Hydro Pump
tips.description: Aim the water jets by tilting the stick in a particular direction. You can then quickly tilt the stick in a different direction to go that way with the second burst.

extra_info.name: Hydro Pump's Flow
extra_info.description: The water flow doesn't just cause damage—it can also push your foes if you hit them just right.

tips.name: Substitute
tips.description: This move makes an enemy attack hit a substitute instead of you. You then reappear somewhere else and attack!

extra_info.name: Substitute
extra_info.description: After successfully countering with this, input a direction, and you'll reappear while attacking in that direction.

tips.name: Secret Ninja Attack
tips.description: Greninja uses Battle Bond to transform and unleash its Final Smash. An opponent hit with the Mat Block will be slashed repeatedly and smashed downward, while the moon glows in the background.
Greninja's down taunt involves Greninja performing water tricks. Opponents hit by the water will take damage and float a bit.

tips.name: Shot Put
tips.description: Throws a metal ball diagonally upward, just like the real shot-put event. It's effective at blocking opponents' attempts at recovery.

extra_info.name: Flashing Mach Punch
extra_info.description: Unleash a super-fast flurry of punches. If the rush of punches hits an opponent, you become invincible and unleash a powerful uppercut.

extra_info.name: Exploding Side Kick
extra_info.description: Input in the opposite direction right after pressing the button to turn around and deliver this kick the other way! It'll also deal more damage.

tips.name: Onslaught
tips.description: The more damage you've taken, the harder your final kick will hit. Once used, it will take some time for this ability to be at full strength again.

extra_info.name: Burning Dropkick
extra_info.description: This move combines a big forward leap with a drop kick. Also, it's on fire. If you hit an opponent, you'll gain some air from the recoil.

extra_info.name: Suplex
extra_info.description: Dash forward and grab the opponent, and then throw them backward. If you grab the opponent in the air, you'll dive downward. If you dive off the stage, you'll be KO'd before the fighter you grabbed is KO'd.

tips.name: Soaring Axe Kick
tips.description: Rise with a somersault kick. Hit the button right before the peak and you'll perform a falling heel kick. The heel kick has a powerful meteor effect, but it can carry you down to your doom if you aren't careful.

extra_info.name: Soaring Axe Kick Recovery
extra_info.description: When recovering with this move, it often goes past the edge, leaving you open to attack. Try waiting until exactly the right height to grab on to the edge.

extra_info.name: Helicopter Kick
extra_info.description: When performing this move, pressing left or right will let you change the direction of the kick.

extra_info.name: Thrust Uppercut
extra_info.description: This powerful upward punch will drag in your opponents as you rise. You can also move a little to the right or left while attacking.

tips.name: Head-On Assault
tips.description: When used in the air, this move becomes a descending headbutt. You can even grab an edge during your rapid descent, but if there's no edge or ground beneath you...

extra_info.name: Feint Jump
extra_info.description: Invincible at the start, then becomes a rising backflip. Avoid attacks with it, then descend quickly with another button press. You'll kick at anyone nearby when you land.

extra_info.name: Counter Throw
extra_info.description: Grab and throw the opponent if they attack you. You cannot counter projectiles or attacks aimed at your feet.

tips.name: Omega Blitz
tips.description: Kick opponents into the sky and follow up with a rush of strikes. At the end, deal a powerful downward strike that sends opponents hurtling toward the ground.

tips.name: Cartwheel Kick
tips.description: An upward-kicking backflip. Your legs are invulnerable while you perform it, so it's perfect for countering enemy attacks from above.
Mii characters first appeared in 2006, with the debut of the Wii console and the Mii Channel. They can be used in a variety of games.

tips.name: Gale Strike
tips.description: With Gale Strike, a wide swing of the sword creates a tornado that moves forward. Opponents hit by the tornado are thrown straight up into the air.

extra_info.name: Gale Strike's Traits
extra_info.description: The tornado will become weaker as time passes, but you can follow this slow-moving projectile and attack along with it!

extra_info.name: Gale Strike's Path
extra_info.description: A large swing of the sword that summons a tall, damaging tornado. The tornado's path sinks slightly just after it appears.

extra_info.name: Shuriken of Light
extra_info.description: The farther the shurikens travel, the more damage they deal. Throwing them at nearby foes won't even make them flinch.

extra_info.name: Blurring Blade
extra_info.description: A flurry of sword stabs right in front of you, followed by a slash forward. Good for keeping your opponents at a safe distance!

extra_info.name: Charging Blurring Blade
extra_info.description: Mii Swordfighter attacks with a flurry of fire stabs, then launches the opponent diagonally upward. Hold the button longer to charge the attack. The longer the charge, the greater the attack power.

tips.name: Airborne Assault
tips.description: The Mii performs a somersault leap forward. If it strikes foes, they will be blasted back and the user will jump upward.

extra_info.name: Gale Stab
extra_info.description: The more this move is charged, the faster you'll move and more power you'll have. After using it in midair, you'll descend helplessly down.

extra_info.name: Chakram
extra_info.description: Throws a ring-shaped projectile. If it hits the stage, it'll stick there. You can adjust the angle of the throw by pressing up or down.

extra_info.name: Chakram's Traits
extra_info.description: You can raise the speed and power of the chakram by flicking the stick as you press the button. You can adjust the angle of the attack with up or down right before it's thrown.

tips.name: Stone Scabbard
tips.description: A rising upward slice followed by a plummeting stab down. The falling stab has a meteor effect.

extra_info.name: Piercing Stone Scabbard
extra_info.description: At the end of this move, the sword sticks right through the ground. You can use it to attack unsuspecting enemies below the platform.

extra_info.name: Skyward Slash Dash
extra_info.description: Slash repeatedly while dashing in the direction you've pressed. Use it in the air to ambush opponents waiting below!

extra_info.name: Hero's Spin
extra_info.description: A spinning sword slice that attacks enemies on both sides. When used in midair it makes you rise upward, so you can use it for recovery.

tips.name: Blade Counter
tips.description: This move counters an enemy attack. Succeeding with this move lets you move around quickly—perfect for launching a follow-up attack!

extra_info.name: Blade Counter Technique
extra_info.description: You can use this move in midair to slow your fall. This can be useful for throwing off the timing of a foe waiting below you to attack.

extra_info.name: Reversal Slash
extra_info.description: A gust of wind from the Mii character's sword sends projectiles back the way they came. It can also flip opponents around if they get too close.

extra_info.name: Power Thrust
extra_info.description: This dashing thrust will travel sideways on the ground but diagonally down in the air. The sword's tip is especially powerful.

tips.name: Final Edge
tips.description: Quickly swing the sword and unleash fan-shaped shockwaves. It cuts through the stage and has a long range.
Mii characters first appeared in 2006, with the debut of the Wii console and the Mii Channel. They can be used in a variety of games.

tips.name: Charge Blast
tips.description: This move is super powerful when fully charged. You can also charge it in midair. Jumping or shielding will cancel the charge.

extra_info.name: Laser Blaze
extra_info.description: This move fires a beam from your arm cannon. It will cause the enemy to flinch slightly, and it can be fired repeatedly.

extra_info.name: Grenade Launch
extra_info.description: These grenades fly in an arc—perfect for foes returning to the stage. It's a predictable arc, though, so you'll have to be clever to surprise them.

tips.name: Flame Pillar
tips.description: Once it hits the ground, it will erupt in a pillar of fire. It lasts a while, making it useful for blocking projectiles or serving as a shield.

extra_info.name: Stealth Burst
extra_info.description: Hold the button down to get more distance, and release to detonate. Any enemies caught in the blast will be sent flying.

extra_info.name: Gunner Missile
extra_info.description: Use this move normally to fire a missile that homes in on opponents. Deliver it like a smash attack to fire a stronger missile that will fly straight.

tips.name: Lunar Launch
tips.description: The Mii fires straight down, using the recoil to gain height. How fortunate if an opponent gets caught in the blast as well!

extra_info.name: Cannon Jump Kick
extra_info.description: Use your cannon to launch yourself upward. If the blast hits an opponent in the air, it will have a meteor effect. The move doesn't provide a lot of vertical movement, so don't hesitate before using it.

extra_info.name: Arm Rocket
extra_info.description: This move fires a rocket jet from your arm cannon, carrying you upward. It doesn't deal damage, but you can adjust the direction while ascending.

tips.name: Echo Reflector
tips.description: This move creates a Reflector that sends projectiles back the way they came. They'll be stronger than they were before too!

extra_info.name: Bomb Drop
extra_info.description: The bomb can be detonated by pressing down and the special-move button again. Opponents can hit them back, so be careful!

extra_info.name: Absorbing Vortex
extra_info.description: A barrier that absorbs energy-based projectiles. When you activate the barrier or absorb a projectile, you will deal damage for a brief second.

tips.name: Full Blast
tips.description: Shoot out a long-range laser that can be aimed up or down. It pushes opponents slowly, so use it on the edge of the stage for maximum effect.
Mii characters first appeared in 2006, with the debut of the Wii console and the Mii Channel. They can be used in a variety of games.
Palutena debuted in Kid Icarus for the NES. She only appeared in the ending sequence, but the Japanese version had her name in the title!
The goddess that rules over Angel Land in Kid Icarus. Pit serves Palutena, the Goddess of Light and protector of humankind.

tips.name: Explosive Flame
tips.description: This move causes an explosion a short distance away from Palutena. It deals multiple hits to any opponents in its vicinity and then sends them flying.

extra_info.name: Explosive Flame Positioning
