extra_info.description: Flick the control stick when you press the button to make the explosion occur farther away. Gauge the distance between you and the opponent, and try to make the explosion occur at the right spot.

tips.name: Warp
tips.description: Palutena disappears and then warps to a location a short distance away. Warp will take Palutena in the direction you press.

tips.name: Counter/Reflect Barrier
tips.description: Used as a shield, this move can counter attacks, or reflect barrier can reflect projectiles! It is automatic, but both can't happen at once.

tips.name: Black Hole Laser
tips.description: Palutena's Final Smash traps opponents in a Black Hole before Palutena fires a laser beam to launch them. The beam's angle can be slightly adjusted.

tips.name: Purging Kick
tips.description: Compared to other attacks that have a meteor effect, Palutena's down air can be used very quickly. Going for a dunk is always a risky proposition, though.
The shield used during her dash attack and back air attack will nullify incoming attacks. This means that you're unlikely to lose a face-off between these attacks and attacking opponents.
PAC-MAN made his debut in 1980. His debut title was later recognized by Guinness World Records as the "Most Successful Coin-Operated Arcade Game."
PAC-MAN, released in 1980, is both the name of the arcade game and its main character. In that game, PAC-MAN navigates a maze, dodging ghosts while trying to gobble up Power Pellets.
In PAC-MAN, players were rewarded with an intermission after clearing a set number of rounds. In one of them, Blinky shows a little bit of...leg?!
If you got a perfect score on all 256 stages, the highest score you could get would be 3,333,360 points. Once you get a perfect score, it's time to start racing your friends!

tips.name: Cherry Bonus Fruit
tips.description: Cherries fly in a low arc. After hitting the floor, they'll bounce only once.

extra_info.name: Orange Bonus Fruit
extra_info.description: Oranges don't deal much damage, but they fly straight and at high speed, making it pretty easy to hit foes with them.

extra_info.name: Strawberry Bonus Fruit
extra_info.description: PAC-MAN's strawberry flies a little farther than other fruit and bounces twice when it lands.

extra_info.name: Melon Bonus Fruit
extra_info.description: PAC-MAN's melon moves slowly through the air, making it difficult to hit opponents. But when it does hit, it deals big damage.

extra_info.name: Apple Bonus Fruit
extra_info.description: PAC-MAN's apple bounces repeatedly across the ground. It travels quite a distance!

extra_info.name: Key Bonus...Fruit?
extra_info.description: The key flies forward at high speed while spinning. It deals the most damage of all the Bonus Fruit items.

extra_info.name: Boss Galaxian
extra_info.description: The Boss Galaxian Bonus Fruit moves forward a little, twirls around once, and continues forward. It's great for combos since it can hit the same opponent at least two times.

extra_info.name: Bell Bonus Fruit
extra_info.description: The Bell Bonus Fruit can stun opponents. It's a great opportunity to follow up with a smash attack or a powerful move.

extra_info.name: Canceling Bonus Fruit
extra_info.description: You can cancel charging Bonus Fruit with a jump or by shielding. Press and hold the button again to resume charging. However, if you press and release the button immediately, you will throw the fruit.

tips.name: Power Pellet
tips.description: If an opponent attacks the Power Pellet, this attack will be canceled. It's a useful move for recovery, but be careful when foes are around!

tips.name: Pac-Jump
tips.description: This move sets up a trampoline for PAC-MAN to jump on. If you jump on it repeatedly, you'll go higher with each bounce!

tips.name: Fire Hydrant
tips.description: PAC-MAN places a fire hydrant on the ground. The water that it spurts will push fighters back.

extra_info.name: Aerial Fire Hydrant
extra_info.description: If you use this attack in the air, the falling hydrant itself becomes a form of attack. Try dropping it on an opponent trying to return to the stage!

extra_info.name: Attack Using Fire Hydrant
extra_info.description: The water that sprays from the hydrant will even push PAC-MAN himself. You can use this to sneak up on a foe while charging an attack!

extra_info.name: Tumbling Fire Hydrant
extra_info.description: If you drop a hydrant on a steep slope, it'll go tumbling down. The tumbling hydrant can deal damage to enemies it hits.

extra_info.name: Launching Fire Hydrant
extra_info.description: The direction the fire hydrant is launched depends on the last move that hits it. Try to learn what moves make the fire hydrant fly in certain directions.

tips.name: Super PAC-MAN
tips.description: A Final Smash where PAC-MAN becomes Super PAC-MAN and eats opponents to launch them. He'll loop around the other side of the screen and speed up as time goes on!

tips.name: PAC Dash Bite
tips.description: Opponents can easily predict this move since it travels straight forward, but the move doesn't leave you vulnerable to attack, so it's safe to use almost anytime.
The durability of the trampoline will be reduced when attacked by other fighters. The more powerful the attack, the more the durability will suffer.
Robin debuted in Fire Emblem Awakening, a 2013 release in North America. As the customizable avatar of the player, he or she fought alongside Chrom.
The avatar from Fire Emblem Awakening. You can set the avatar's personality and appearance. In Awakening, Robin is saved by Chrom and joins his militia as a tactician.
In Fire Emblem Awakening, Robin wasn't able to use Nosferatu in his or her base class of Tactician—only by reclassing into a Dark Mage.

tips.name: Thunder
tips.description: If you launch this move without charging it up, Robin will cast Thunder. It's not very powerful, but its fast speed can take foes by surprise.

extra_info.name: Thunder Types
extra_info.description: Robin will use one of four magic spells, depending on how long you charge Thunder. The color the tome glows indicates which spell will be used.

extra_info.name: Thunder Charge Level 1
extra_info.description: Once the tome starts glowing yellow, you can cast Elthunder. It's more powerful than Thunder and travels farther, but it's not quite as fast.

extra_info.name: Thunder Charge Level 2
extra_info.description: A reddish purple glow means that Thunder has charged into Arcthunder! A current with high attack power appears when this move connects.

extra_info.name: Thunder at Max Charge
extra_info.description: When Thunder is fully charged, it becomes Thoron! The high-speed beam can deal damage to multiple opponents at once.

extra_info.name: Reaching Thunder's Limits
extra_info.description: Thunder can be used up to 20 times in a row, but stronger tomes use it up faster. Elthunder counts as 3 uses, Arcthunder as 5, and Thoron as 8.

tips.name: Reaching Arcfire's Limits
tips.description: Arcfire can be used up to six times in a row. Once it's used up, it'll be about 12 seconds before you can use the move again.

tips.name: Elwind
tips.description: The first Elwind has a meteor effect right after it comes out. When the Elwinds hit the ground, they create a gust of wind that pushes opponents back.

extra_info.name: Reaching Elwind's Limits
extra_info.description: Elwind causes two blasts to propel you upward, and it also uses your tome twice. If you've only got one tome use left, you won't be able to double Elwind!

tips.name: Reaching Nosferatu's Limits
tips.description: Nosferatu is a useful technique that can recover health, but it can only be used three times in a row and takes up to 30 seconds to recharge.

tips.name: Pair Up
tips.description: If Chrom's attack connects and you perform this successfully, all of Robin's tomes and the Levin Sword will be fully recovered afterward.
If the third hit is fire, it will launch opponents with great force. The wind flurry attack repeatedly slashes opponents and deals a lot of damage. Both will use up a tome charge.

tips.name: Undercut
tips.description: This attack doesn't have a meteor effect with the Bronze Sword, but it does with the Levin Sword.
Press the special move and attack buttons at the same time in the air to switch to a Levin Sword even when holding a Bronze Sword. You can perform a neutral air attack by doing this.
After eight uses of the Levin Sword, you'll throw it away and be unable to use it for 10 seconds. Use a neutral attack while grounded to draw a Bronze Sword.
When you've used up the Levin Sword, perform smash attacks with the Bronze Sword or directional midair attacks to reduce the wait.
You cannot use the Levin Sword at the start of the battle, but once time passes, it will be available to use. On the other hand, tomes can be used from the start.
When Robin discards tomes and Levin Swords after using them up, they can hurt opponents they hit. They can even be grabbed and thrown!
As a general rule, the remaining uses of tomes and Levin Swords won't reset until they're all used up or Robin gets KO'd.
The third strike of Robin's neutral attack combo uses the same tome as Arcfire. When you run out of one attack, you also run out of the other.
It's difficult to notice in the middle of a battle, but the cover of the tome Robin uses actually changes depending on the spell being used.
Robin's flurry attack uses the same tome as Elwind. If you use it up with the flurry attack, you won't be able to use Elwind for recovery!
If you've used up a tome or your Levin Sword, try KO'ing some opponents. That'll shorten the time you have to wait to use them again.
Shulk made his debut in North America with the 2012 release of Xenoblade Chronicles. A budding scientist, this young man has a kind and curious heart.
The main character from Xenoblade Chronicles. Shulk is a gentle young man who hopes for peace, but he also has the courage to confront any opponent.
The line Shulk speaks when he uses his up taunt, "Now it's Shulk time!" is a reference to Shulk's childhood friend Reyn.

tips.name: Summertime Shulk
tips.description: Shulk's eighth color variation puts him in a pair of swimming trunks! In the original game, this is how he appeared with all his equipment removed.

tips.name: Monado Arts
tips.description: Shulk switches between five modes—Jump, Speed, Shield, Buster, and Smash—that change his abilities for a short time.

extra_info.name: Monado Arts - Speed
extra_info.description: In Speed mode, you'll sacrifice damage and jump height in order to move faster.

extra_info.name: Monado Arts - Shield
extra_info.description: In Shield mode, the power of your shield is boosted but you can't move as fast, jump as high, or deal as much damage.

extra_info.name: Monado Arts - Buster
extra_info.description: In Buster mode, you deal more damage than usual, but you can't launch opponents as far and you'll receive more damage.

extra_info.name: Monado Arts - Smash
extra_info.description: In Smash mode, you can launch opponents more effectively, but you deal less damage and can be launched more easily yourself.

extra_info.name: Monado Arts - Jump
extra_info.description: In Jump mode, it's not just Shulk's standard jump and midair jump that go higher—his Air Slash move gives him more height than usual, too.

extra_info.name: Monado Arts and Being Launched
extra_info.description: Shulk can switch between Monado Arts even while being launched. If you get launched off a cliff, switching to Jump mode might save you!

extra_info.name: Limitations of Monado Arts
extra_info.description: You can't use a Monado Art immediately after it's run out. If you wait a certain period of time, it will become available for use again.

extra_info.name: Canceling Monado Arts
extra_info.description: To cancel the current Monado Art, press the special-move button quickly three times in a row.

extra_info.name: Chain Attack and Monado Arts
extra_info.description: Shulk's Monado Arts even affect his Final Smash. If you select Buster, your launch power will go down but your attack will go up, causing you to deal more damage!

extra_info.name: Shortcuts for Monado Arts
extra_info.description: Press and hold the special-move button to see all the Monado Arts. Select the Monado Art you want with the control stick, and let go of the button to confirm your selection.

tips.name: Back Slash
tips.description: Shulk leaps forward and performs a vertical slice downward. As the name implies, it deals more damage if you hit the enemy from behind.

extra_info.name: Back Slash Details
extra_info.description: After using Back Slash, you won't be able to grab stage edges until enough time has passed. While swinging the Monado downward, you can move slightly left and right by tilting the control stick.

tips.name: Air Slash
tips.description: Press the attack button while using an Air Slash to perform another slash forward and rise a little farther. It won't work after you start falling.

tips.name: Length of Vision
tips.description: Shulk's Vision is a powerful counter move, but its activation timing becomes stricter when used repeatedly. It recovers slowly while not in use.

extra_info.name: Strike from Behind with Vision
extra_info.description: Input toward your foe when you pull off a counter, and you'll swing around them to attack from the other side. This launches farther but won't work in the air.

tips.name: Shouts in Chain Attack
tips.description: There are variations on this attack, depending on whether Shulk calls for Dunban, Fiora, or Riki. They don't change anything but the lines spoken, though.

extra_info.name: Chain Attack
extra_info.description: Shulk's Final Smash hits opponents within a set radius. Dunban, Riki, and Fiora join in to deal repeated attacks.

tips.name: Behind Thrust
tips.description: Shulk's back air has tremendous reach, but the timing can be a bit tricky.
Bowser Jr. first appeared in the 2002 title Super Mario Sunshine. He's a bit of a brat, but he does have a lot of love for his daddy.
Larry Koopa's debut was in Super Mario Bros. 3, released in North America in 1990. He's the youngest of the Koopalings.
tips.name: Larry's Origins

tips.description: Roy Koopa's debut was in Super Mario Bros. 3, released in North America in 1990. If you see a big Koopaling wearing pink shades, that's him.
tips.name: Roy's Origins

tips.description: Lemmy Koopa debuted in Super Mario Bros. 3, released in North America in 1990. He's the smallest and quickest of the Koopalings.
tips.name: Lemmy's Origins

tips.description: Ludwig von Koopa first appeared in Super Mario Bros. 3, released in North America in 1990. You could call him the brains of the Koopalings.
tips.name: Ludwig's Origins

tips.description: Morton Koopa Jr.'s debut was in Super Mario Bros. 3, released in North America in 1990. As the largest of the Koopalings, he's proudly more brawn than brains.
tips.name: Morton's Origins

tips.description: The stylish Wendy O. Koopa made her debut in North America with the 1990 release of Super Mario Bros. 3. She's the only female Koopaling.
tips.name: Wendy's Origins

tips.description: Trickster Iggy Koopa debuted in Super Mario Bros. 3, released in North America in 1990. Fun fact: he's the only nearsighted Koopaling!
tips.name: Iggy's Origins

tips.description: Bowser's openly selfish son Bowser Jr. made his first appearance in Super Mario Sunshine. He kidnaps Peach and calls her Mama.

tips.name: Clown Cannon
tips.description: This move fires a cannonball from the Junior Clown Car. The longer this attack is charged, the faster and more powerful it will be.

tips.name: Clown Kart Dash
tips.description: The Junior Clown Car becomes a kart and charges forward. The higher the speed, the more damage it will deal.

extra_info.name: Clown Kart Dash and Jump
extra_info.description: Input a jump while performing this, and you'll cancel the dash. This can be handy for recovery, as a lead-in to other moves, or as a feint.

tips.name: Abandon Ship
tips.description: Bowser Jr. abandons his Junior Clown Car and performs a massive leap. After a short delay, the abandoned vehicle will self-destruct.
After leaping out of the Junior Clown Car, Bowser Jr. can swing a hammer with the neutral-attack button. This attack is great for launching.

tips.name: Mechakoopa
tips.description: The Junior Clown Car spits out a Mechakoopa that explodes on contact with an enemy. It'll explode if you just leave it alone for a while too.

extra_info.name: Picking Up Mechakoopa
extra_info.description: After taking out a Mechakoopa, Bowser Jr. can pick it back up himself. It's a versatile tool that can be used in a variety of ways to corner foes.

tips.name: Shadow Mario Paint
tips.description: Shadow Mario paints a big X across the screen. If opponents touch the X, they'll receive damage and be launched. Bowser Jr. can move around after painting, so follow up with an attack!

tips.name: Smash Twin Drills
tips.description: Bowser Jr.'s side smash can hit multiple times, making it hard to dodge. Even if the attack is blocked by a shield, Bowser Jr. isn't left vulnerable, so it's hard for the opponent to counter.
The Junior Clown Car takes less damage than Bowser Jr.'s body. When you're under attack, try to get the opponent to hit the Junior Clown Car instead of Bowser Jr.
Duck Hunt was released on the NES in North America in 1985, but did you know Nintendo released another toy in Japan with the same title in the '70s?
Duck Hunt for the NES was played using the special NES Zapper. In that game, you hunted ducks that flew out of bushes, and the dog would collect them.
The game Duck Hunt sold over 28 million copies worldwide. A big contribution to this was its inclusion as a pack-in title with the NES console.

tips.name: Trick Shot
tips.description: This move kicks an exploding can toward your enemies. Keep pressing the button to fire shots that move the can forward.

extra_info.name: Risks with Trick Shot
extra_info.description: If the Duck Hunt dog and duck are near the can when it's shot, the duo will crouch down and cover their eyes, leaving them vulnerable for a moment.

extra_info.name: Trick Shot Anytime
extra_info.description: Even while grabbing opponents, being grabbed, or using Duck Jump, you can keep shooting the can.

extra_info.name: Trick Shot Techniques
extra_info.description: You can move the can by shooting it with the special-move button or by hitting it with other attacks. Try to position the can in an advantageous position while you move around the stage.

tips.name: Clay Shooting
tips.description: The clay pigeon's speed will change if you're on the move. Tap the special-move button a second time for the gunman to open fire, hurting nearby foes if he hits.

tips.name: Duck Jump
tips.description: The duck grabs the dog and carries him through the air. You're very vulnerable while in the air. You can cancel with attacks or with a midair dodge once a bit of time passes after initiating the move.

tips.name: Wild Gunman
tips.description: This move summons a pixelated gunman who shoots straight ahead. The gunman selected is random, and each one works a little differently.

extra_info.name: Defeated Wild Gunman
extra_info.description: The gunmen can be defeated if they take enough damage. For a surprise, hit the one in a black suit near the top or hit the lanky one near the bottom!

extra_info.name: Wild Gunman Order
extra_info.description: The gunmen's order is random, but all five will take a turn before cycling. Remember the first four, and you can predict the last one!

extra_info.name: Wild Gunman Attack Power
extra_info.description: The gunmen's firepower, from best to worst: the sombrero guy, the cool leader, the guy in a suit, and then a tie between the lanky and beardy ones.

extra_info.name: Wild Gunman Range
extra_info.description: The lanky gunman has a longer range than any of the others, and the one in the sombrero has the shortest range. The others are pretty much equal.

extra_info.name: Wild Gunman Speed
extra_info.description: The gunmen from fastest to slowest: the cool leader (earning that title!), the one in a suit, the one in a sombrero, the beardy one, and then the lanky one.

tips.name: NES Zapper Posse
tips.description: Three games from the Light Gun Series team up: Duck Hunt, Hogan's Alley, and Wild Gunman.

tips.name: Zapper
tips.description: You can charge it up to extend the range a bit, which may catch opponents off guard if they don't expect it to reach that far.

tips.name: Wild Duck
tips.description: The duck will peck upward three times. The last peck has good launch power, so use it as a finisher.
While the clay pigeon is flying, you cannot take out the can with Trick Shot. However, you can use a clay pigeon after taking out a can.
Of the characters in the Street Fighter series, Ryu is one of the most well-known. His goal is to be a true fighter. In the first game, his hair was red.

tips.name: Hadoken
tips.description: A projectile from a world-class fighting game. Hold down the button to vary how fast it travels. It'll disappear after a set distance.

tips.name: Hadoken
tips.description: Input the commands from the original series to increase the Hadoken's power. Use it in midair to make Ryu glide slightly.

extra_info.name: Hadoken's Power
extra_info.description: When facing right, quickly input ↓ ↘ → for 1.2x power. You might have to practice a bit to really get the hang of a Hadoken.

extra_info.name: Shakunetsu Hadoken
extra_info.description: When facing right, input ← ↙ ↓ ↘ → to unleash a fiery Shakunetsu Hadoken that can deal multiple hits.

tips.name: Tatsumaki Senpukyaku
tips.description: Ryu will float up slightly when using this move after a jump, making this spinning side kick great for recovering.

tips.name: Tatsumaki Senpukyaku
tips.description: Input the commands from the original series to power up the attack. One thing to remember: you can't use it repeatedly in midair.

extra_info.name: Tatsumaki Senpukyaku's Power
extra_info.description: When facing right, enter ↓ ↙ ← for 1.16x power. Increasing the strength also increases the move's speed and travel distance.

tips.name: Shoryuken
tips.description: When you enter the original commands, Ryu is invincible for longer, the move is stronger and looks different...and his shout even changes!

extra_info.name: Shoryuken's Power
extra_info.description: When facing right, input → ↓ ↘ for 1.2x attack power. Pull it off for some extra damage!

tips.name: Focus Attack
tips.description: Ryu will hold his stance until you release the button or a certain amount of time has passed. Then he'll unleash the punch.

extra_info.name: Looking Back then Focus Attack
extra_info.description: By turning the opposite way the moment Ryu punches, he will strike in that direction instead.

extra_info.name: Focus Attack Follow-ups
extra_info.description: Hitting an opponent on the ground with the fully charged version of this move will make them slowly crumple. Time for a follow-up attack!

extra_info.name: Canceling Focus Attack
extra_info.description: You can cancel this move by pressing left or right twice quickly, either while charging or right after the punch connects.

extra_info.name: Defend with Focus Attack
extra_info.description: Ryu can take one hit while readying for this move. This is true until right before he attacks, even when charging the move to maximum power.

tips.name: Shin Shoryuken
tips.description: When an opponent is near, the move turns into Shin Shoryuken. It deals more damage than Shinku Hadoken, and if you land a KO, there will be flashy special effects in the background.

extra_info.name: Shinku Hadoken
extra_info.description: If the enemy is far away, it becomes Shinku Hadoken. This move penetrates through the stage, making it easy to hit multiple enemies at once.

tips.description: Ryu's Final Smash can be used after canceling various attacks. It's perfect as a combo finisher.

tips.name: Collarbone Breaker
tips.description: Keep up the pressure on a guarding enemy with Ryu's Collarbone Breaker, a double hit that's good for shield breaking.

tips.name: Light Ankle Kick
tips.description: Use this attack by crouching and tapping the attack button. If you hit a downed opponent with it, you'll knock them back to their feet.

tips.name: Heavy Ankle Kick
tips.description: You might have seen this kick in the original games. Just like in those, you can cancel the move after striking by going straight into a special.

extra_info.name: Canceling Heavy Ankle Kick
extra_info.description: Hold down the attack button while crouching to use this move. You can cancel it into a special even if the foe is shielding, but only if you hit.

tips.name: Joudan Sokutogeri
tips.description: Ryu steps forward and unleashes his Joudan Sokutogeri. The tip of his foot is the strongest part, but it won't hit a small crouching enemy.

tips.name: Tobigeri
tips.description: Hitting an enemy with Ryu's foot has higher launch power, while his upper leg is perfect for shield breaking. It's a good move for combos.

tips.name: Sukui Tsuki
tips.description: This uppercut hits the enemy twice and has a deceptively large range. It's fairly easy to hit enemies directly above you.

tips.name: Straight Punch
tips.description: This is the only attack in Ryu's arsenal that has a meteor effect. If it hits, your victim will fly diagonally downward.
Air attacks can be canceled immediately after they hit by pressing the special-move button just like some other attacks can. You can create powerful combos like Grab → Up Throw → Up Air → Shoryuken.

tips.name: Heel Drop
tips.description: This move kicks a grabbed opponent straight upward. As Ryu brings his leg back down, he'll hit anyone else unlucky enough to be in the way.
Ken first debuted in 1987 in the arcade version of Street Fighter. He showed up as the P2 character. He was exactly the same as Ryu besides his looks.
Ken is friends with Ryu and also trained with him. He considers Ryu as his rival. He is the son of the well-known Masters family.

tips.name: Hadoken
tips.description: Press the button longer to increase the speed, distance, and power. The Hadoken's shape is a bit different than Ryu's, and you can see the shape of Ken's hands within the Hadoken.

tips.name: Tatsumaki Senpukyaku
tips.description: It can hit multiple times, so get all the hits in to do a lot of damage. Input ↓ ↙ ← + special-move button to increase the attack's power even more.

tips.name: Shoryuken
tips.description: This version of the attack increases invincibility time at the start, and boosts attack power. The light version leaves you less vulnerable when you land, and the medium version hits foes twice.

tips.name: Flaming Shoryuken
tips.description: Press and hold the button to unleash a Shoryuken engulfed in flames. It can hit three times and does big damage, and it launches opponents farther horizontally than Ryu's Shoryuken.

tips.name: Focus Attack
tips.description: Ken can take one hit without flinching while charging this spinning kick. If it lands at max charge, the opponent will fall slowly to the ground.

tips.name: Shippu Jinraikyaku
tips.description: If an opponent is nearby when you activate your Final Smash, you'll execute this flashy kick, rising into the air and hitting your opponent multiple times before launching them.

extra_info.name: Shinryuken
extra_info.description: If your opponent is far away, your Final Smash becomes Shinryuken. It does good damage and can hit opponents in front of you and around you.

tips.name: Hell Wheel
tips.description: Unlike Ryu's Somersault Throw, Ken rolls backward once and then throws his opponent with Hell Wheel. Ken gains the distance he moved during the throw, making it easy to follow up quickly.

tips.name: Nata Otoshi Geri
tips.description: Input → ↘ ↓ + attack button while facing right to unleash a high spin kick. You can perform this immediately after canceling a neutral attack.

extra_info.name: Oosoto Mawashi Geri
extra_info.description: Input ← ↙ ↓ ↘ → + attack button while facing right to execute this move. You can perform this immediately after canceling a neutral attack.

extra_info.name: Inazuma Kick
extra_info.description: Hold down the button after entering → ↘ ↓ + attack button or ← ↙ ↓ ↘ → + attack button while facing right to execute this move. It does major damage to shields.

tips.description: Cloud is a former SOLDIER turned mercenary, hired by Avalanche—a group that stands against the Shinra Electric Power Company. Cloud's the strong, silent type, often using "not interested" as his go-to phrase.
Fully charging your Limit Gauge lets you perform a Limit Break, which alters your special moves and gives them a boost.
Once charged, Cloud's Limit Gauge can release when a special move is used, Cloud is KO'd, or a certain amount of time passes.

tips.name: Blade Beam
tips.description: Cloud releases a shock wave along the ground. As you'd guess by looking, the attack range is quite wide, so you'll need to jump high to clear it.

extra_info.name: Blade Beam During Limit Break
extra_info.description: The Limit Break version of this attack bursts on impact to hit multiple times. It can even KO opponents if their damage is high enough.

extra_info.name: Blade Beam and Projectiles
extra_info.description: The Limit Break version of this move neutralizes projectiles and thrown items. Try using it just as you're about to be hit by a projectile!

tips.name: Cross Slash
tips.description: If you press the button again when connecting with an enemy, you can hit them up to three times. Otherwise, Cloud only attacks once.

extra_info.name: Cross Slash During Limit Break
extra_info.description: You only need to press the button once to unleash the full attack if you use your Limit Break. Even better, launching enemies becomes much easier.

tips.name: Climhazzard
tips.description: Press the special-move button again on the way up to slash back down. You can't move sideways on the way down, so be careful during recoveries.

extra_info.name: Climhazzard During Limit Break
extra_info.description: This move's Limit Break version allows you to climb even higher into the air, as well as letting you move sideways. It's great for recoveries!

tips.name: Limit Charge
tips.description: The Limit Gauge's charge will drop if you're attacked while charging. Make sure you're in a safe spot before you begin charging.

extra_info.name: Canceling Limit Charge
extra_info.description: You can cancel charging by pressing the shield button or dodging left or right, or jumping. Use this to get in a quick charge during quiet moments!

extra_info.name: Finishing Touch
extra_info.description: This is Cloud's down special once the Limit Gauge is charged. The tornado doesn't do much damage, but it can KO enemies with its high launching power.

tips.name: Omnislash
tips.description: When using one of the odd-numbered color variations, toss opponents upward with the sword, unleash powerful slashes, and then send them downward. Fighters near where the opponent lands will be launched.

extra_info.name: Omnislash Ver. 5
extra_info.description: The even color variations will launch an opponent into the air and then slash them with a barrage of hits before striking with one final blow. Damage dealt and time taken by the other color variations is identical.

tips.name: Sliding
tips.description: Cloud does a sliding kick over a set distance. He slides so low that this move can be used to dodge some thrown items.

tips.name: Triple Buster
tips.description: This quick three-hit smash has high launching power on the last hit. The first hit isn't so powerful, so the penalty for getting countered isn't high.

tips.name: Double Thrust
tips.description: Cloud attacks a foe and then sends the foe flying off behind him. Use this when close to an edge to get out of sticky situations.

tips.name: Meteor Slash
tips.description: Cloud takes a huge vertical swing at an enemy. Hitting an opponent with the tip of the sword has a meteor effect.
Corrin, the main character from Fire Emblem Fates, is of royal blood. As a descendant of the First Dragons, Corrin can transform into a dragon.
When Corrin's Yato blade resonates with the four other divine weapons, it will turn into its final form, the Omega Yato.

tips.name: Charging Dragon Fang Shot
tips.description: The two stages of this move—shooting water and the bite attack—can both be charged by holding down the button at the right point.

extra_info.name: Dragon Fang Shot's Blast
extra_info.description: The water projectile will paralyze whoever it hits, giving you the time you need to follow up with a fully charged biting attack!

tips.name: Dragon Lunge and Terrain
tips.description: Skewering surfaces with this move allows for follow-up moves, such as recovering from falls by skewering a wall and then leaping to safety!

extra_info.name: Lockdown with Dragon Lunge
extra_info.description: You can keep enemies from moving for a while if you pierce them while skewering a surface. Beware: opponents can escape by wiggling around!

extra_info.name: Dragon Lunge Tips
extra_info.description: After launching this move from the ground, quickly press the attack button to instantly skewer the ground.

extra_info.name: Actions after Dragon Lunge
extra_info.description: On the ground, Corrin won't thrust the spear until you press the button again after jumping. Once the spear is in the ground, tilt the stick to the side to kick, up to jump, and down to cancel.

tips.name: Dragon Ascent's Direction
tips.description: You can slightly adjust your flight angle by pressing in the direction you're facing. Push in the opposite direction to fly straight up and to a greater height.

extra_info.name: Invincibility for Dragon Ascent
extra_info.description: Corrin is invincible while charging up power at the start of this move. Practice with the timing and you'll be able to interrupt enemy attacks!

tips.name: Counter Surge
tips.description: This move will knock foes skyward with a fair amount of force, so try using it in a high-up place to really boot them off the stage!

extra_info.name: Area Attack with Counter Surge
extra_info.description: This counter is a bit slow compared to others, but it can hit foes on both sides. It's great for when you're in the middle of a brawl!

tips.name: Torrential Roar
tips.description: Summon pillars of light and release a powerful torrent of water to launch opponents. The pillars of light will also hit opponents above you.

tips.name: Options for Dragon Fang Thrust
tips.description: Charge this move to juggle foes with Corrin's sword Yato before unleashing the coup de grâce! It has a long range and can be aimed up or down a little!

extra_info.name: Tip on Dragon Fang Thrust
extra_info.description: Try to get the hang of hitting opponents with the point of Corrin's lance-arm, as that part inflicts more damage than the rest.

tips.name: Vertical Dragon Fang Thrust
tips.description: Corrin gets low and attacks straight up with lance-like arms. Try to hit enemies with the points for more damage.

tips.name: Yato Dragon Fang
tips.description: Corrin strikes forward with a sword and backward with a lance-like foot. Try hitting sneaky backstabbers with the point of the lance!

tips.name: Dragon Fang Wings
tips.description: Propel yourself forward with a flap of the wings. You can deal some damage and get yourself out of tight spots.

tips.name: Dragon's Piercing Drop
tips.description: Transform both legs into a single lance and drop straight down. This move deals damage when landing but leaves you open if the enemy uses their shield.

tips.name: Dragon-Form Kick
tips.description: Hits in a wide radius, sending multiple foes skyward when Corrin stomps the ground, lining them up for a nice follow-up attack.
Corrin's arms change into spears when performing a forward throw or a backward throw. You can hit other fighters with the spears. If you can hit opponents with the tip, you can launch them far.
Bayonetta was a witch that lost her memory. Her real name is Cereza. She uses her arms and legs to wield four guns, and has mastered the Bullet Arts fighting style.

tips.name: Rapid Fire with Bullet Climax
tips.description: Bayonetta alternates which guns she fires each time you use this move. Keep hitting the button while she's firing to let off more shots.

extra_info.name: Bullet Climax's Charge
extra_info.description: Hold down the button to unleash more powerful Charge Bullets. Only the first four shots are boosted; the follow-up shots won't be affected.

tips.name: Heel Slide
tips.description: Performs a low, sliding kick. Hold down the button to get in a few gunshots, and then end with a flip attack for good measure.

extra_info.name: Two-Part After Burner Kick
extra_info.description: If you land a hit with After Burner Kick, you'll have a one-second window to use the kick again. This allows you to travel long distances and combo opponents.

extra_info.name: After Burner Kick Angle
extra_info.description: Tilt the control stick down immediately before or after using After Burner Kick to make the move go diagonally down.

tips.name: Witch Twist's Traits
tips.description: This versatile move is great for combos or recovery! Do a midair jump after using the move and you can get in a sneaky extra Witch Twist.

extra_info.name: Witch Twist's Angle
extra_info.description: The angle foes are launched at changes between the first and second Witch Twists. Adjust your tactics to the situation to get the best combos.

tips.name: Witch Time's Effect
tips.description: A successful counter will cause time to slow down. The more damage the opponent has, the longer time will slow down for.

extra_info.name: Witch Time Confirmation
extra_info.description: A clock will appear over an enemy to tell you how much Witch Time is left. Try to get your attacks in before the clock counts down!

extra_info.name: Witch Time's Traits
extra_info.description: Witch Time won't last long if you keep using it against every single attack. Keep it in reserve for when you really need it!

extra_info.name: Witch Time and Projectiles
extra_info.description: If you can counter the opponent's projectile, you can slow the opponent down if you're not too far away from them.

tips.name: Infernal Climax
tips.description: For Bayonetta's Final Smash, she enters Witch Time. If you fill up the Climax Gauge by attacking opponents, you can summon Gomorrah. The Climax Gauge has a time limit, so don't dilly dally.

extra_info.name: Infernal Climax Additional Damage
extra_info.description: The Climax Gauge stays around even after Gomorrah is summoned. If you press attack and light up all eight symbols, you'll deal more damage. If the opponent has over 100% damage, they'll be KO'd.

tips.name: Launching with Consecutive Kicks
tips.description: Keep pressing the button and Bayonetta will do a three-kick attack. The last kick launches foes, so go for a quick midair attack right after!

tips.name: Against Aerial Attacks
tips.description: This move covers a big area above Bayonetta, and it also hits opponents in front of her. Like many other tilt attacks, it's a great setup for a follow-up attack.

tips.name: Kick Attack and Gun Fire
tips.description: Bayonetta quickly sweeps enemies off their feet and will shoot ahead and behind if you hold the button. The guns firing ahead deal more damage.

tips.name: Stomp to Meteor
tips.description: Summon the crushing foot of a demon to slam down with a meteor effect, particularly effective in preventing foes from recovering from falls or climbing up edges.

tips.name: Consecutive Midair Attacks
tips.description: Keep pressing the button for three shots in the air. Perfect for foes trying to recover. Or hold the button to pile on the pressure with a hail of bullets!

tips.name: Rapidly Descending Meteor
tips.description: Bayonetta drops straight down into a split kick. She'll constantly deal damage as she drops, and her foot has a meteor effect.

tips.name: Tetsuzankou
tips.description: For Bayonetta's forward throw, she borrows Akira's infamous move from the Virtua Fighter series. When Bayonetta performs this move, she sometimes delivers his catchphrase along with it.
Keep the button held down after any normal attack (and some specials) to fire your guns.
Even if an attack misses, you can still hold the button and get a few shots in. You can even steal a KO by hitting a launched foe on their way out! Cheeky.
If Bayonetta is hit during the dodge or during her down special, she'll change into a burst of bats, taking half the damage.

The gauge fills as you land attacks in Witch Time during Bayonetta's Final Smash. If you focus on a single target, you'll waste time chasing them each time they're launched, so try to attack multiple opponents.
Inkling first appeared in Splatoon released in 2015. Inklings can change between human form and squid form. They battle by using weapons that shoot ink to cover the ground and walls.
In the Splatoon series, Inklings cover the ground and walls in ink, and then swim through that ink as a squid. But if they fall into water, the ink in their bodies will diffuse and they'll dissolve.

tips.name: Splattershot
tips.description: Hold down the button to shoot ink. Adjust the angle with up or down. The attack lacks power, but it can quickly ink opponents.

tips.name: Splat Roller
tips.description: Run your Splat Roller along the ground to ink it. If you run into an opponent while rolling, they'll be buried.

tips.name: Super Jump
tips.description: Squirt ink to rocket into the air. It's great for returning to the stage. Nearby opponents will be launched if hit by the ink spray during takeoff and landing.

tips.name: Splat Bomb
tips.description: Charge the attack to throw farther, deal more damage, and have more ink in general. If the move is not charged, the bomb will land directly in front of you, which can be useful.

tips.name: Killer Wail
tips.description: Unleashes a laser that penetrates the landscape. While it's wailing, you can control the Inkling freely and adjust the angle of the laser. It can be set up on the ground or in the air!

extra_info.name: Killer Wail after Being KO'd
extra_info.description: Even if the Inkling is KO'd during the Final Smash, the laser will remain and can still be controlled.

tips.name: Slosher
tips.description: A down smash attack that splashes ink left and right with a Slosher. It's very effective at covering opponents with ink.
Shield and press the special-move button to refill your ink tank. If you're totally empty, you can use neutral special or down special on the ground to refill your ink tank.
When you don't have enough ink, special moves and smash attacks will become less effective. Replenish ink by holding down the special-move button while shielding.
The up taunt is the same as the "C'mon!" gesture used in the original Splatoon game. The down taunt, "Booyah!" is also from the original game.
Don't talk to Charizard or Inkling about swimming. They'll take gradual damage while they're in the water.
Ridley debuted in Metroid, released in North America in 1987. Ridley appeared as the boss in the depths of Norfair. He only attacked with a bouncing fireball, but he was still a fierce foe.
Ridley has taken many different forms as Samus's archnemesis. You can check out Ridley's transformation over time in Metroid: Other M.

tips.name: Plasma Breath
tips.description: Charge up energy and spit out balls of plasma. The longer you charge, the more plasma balls you'll spit. However, if you get hit in the mouth while charging, you'll take a lot of damage.

tips.name: Space Pirate Rush
tips.description: Rush forward and grab an opponent, then drag them across the ground while moving forward. Press the button while charging forward or reach the edge of the platform to toss the opponent.

extra_info.name: Airborne Space Pirate Rush
extra_info.description: If performed in the air, Ridley lunges forward and grabs an airborne opponent before diving downward with them still in his clutches. Press the button again to release the opponent.

extra_info.name: Escaping from Space Pirate Rush
extra_info.description: The opponent can move their control stick back and forth to escape from the move. The more damage the opponent has compared to Ridley, the harder it is to escape.

tips.name: Wing Blitz
tips.description: Tilt the control stick up, down, left, or right to charge in that direction. When charging downward, Ridley can unleash a kick with a meteor effect.

tips.name: Skewer
tips.description: Hit an opponent with the tip of the tail to land a critical hit that deals immense damage. It's easier to use when fighters are clustered together. It's super effective in stamina mode.

tips.name: Plasma Scream
tips.description: Throw opponents against Samus's Gunship and fire a plasma beam. You can hit up to two fighters with this move. When it's finished, opponents with more than 100% damage will explode on the spot.
Simon made his debut in Castlevania, released in North America in 1987. Armed with nothing but his legendary whip, he enters Dracula's Castle alone to slay all manner of foul beasts.
Simon is a vampire hunter of House Belmont. He uses not only whips, but also daggers, axes, Holy Water, crosses, and even stopwatches as weapons.

tips.name: Axe
tips.description: A projectile that curves through the air when thrown. You can adjust the angle of the throw by moving the stick left or right. It can pass through the stage, hitting unsuspecting opponents!

tips.name: Cross
tips.description: You can increase the distance and power by quickly moving the stick as you press the special-move button. It can hit while flying out and when flying back, but it's more powerful when flying out.

tips.name: Uppercut
tips.description: A jumping punch that can hit repeatedly. The final hit has the most launch power.

tips.name: Holy Water
tips.description: Throw a bottle of Holy Water forward. If it hits an opponent or the landscape, a red flaming pillar will appear, stopping the opponent's movements. Try following up with a whip attack!

tips.name: Grand Cross
tips.description: Summon a coffin that traps opponents who touch it. Using this on an opponent who is trapped in your Holy Water's fire or an opponent who's fallen down is a good strategy.

tips.name: Sliding
tips.description: Press the attack button again while sliding to perform an additional jump kick. Use it to catch your opponent off guard or as part of a combo.
Press and hold the attack button to make the whip dangle. Rotate the control stick in a circular motion to swing the whip around.
Richter debuted in Castlevania: Rondo of Blood in 1993. He heads to the castle to defeat Count Dracula and rescue his kidnapped girlfriend, Annette.
Richter uses a whip passed down through generations, along with various subweapons. In Castlevania: Symphony of the Night, he can appear as the final boss if you take a certain route.

tips.name: Axe
tips.description: A projectile that curves through the air when thrown. You can adjust the angle of the throw by moving the stick left or right. It can pass through the stage, hitting unsuspecting opponents!

tips.name: Cross
tips.description: You can increase the distance and power by quickly flicking the control stick as you press the special-move button. It can hit while flying out and back, but it's more powerful when flying out.

tips.name: Uppercut
tips.description: A jumping punch that can hit repeatedly. The final hit has the most launch power.

tips.name: Holy Water
tips.description: Throw a bottle of Holy Water forward. If it hits an opponent or the landscape, a blue flaming pillar will appear, stopping the opponent's movements. Try following up with a whip attack!

tips.name: Grand Cross
tips.description: Summon a coffin that traps opponents who touch it. Using this on an opponent who is trapped in your Holy Water's fire or an opponent who's fallen down is a good strategy.
King K. Rool made his debut in 1994 in Donkey Kong Country. He is the leader of the Kremlings and his favorite hobby is stealing Donkey Kong's bananas.
King K. Rool appears as the last boss in Donkey Kong Country. He will pretend to be defeated during the fight. After the fake ending and "THE END?" shows up, he will get up and attack again.

tips.name: Blunderbuss
tips.description: Shoots a slow but powerful iron ball from a pirate-themed firearm. If you continue to hold the special-move button, you can suck the iron ball back in and then shoot it back out.

extra_info.name: Blunderbuss Suck Up
extra_info.description: Suck up not only fighters, but the iron ball that was shot out. After sucking something up, you can aim the shot forward, above, or backward with the control stick.

extra_info.name: Slipping through Platforms while Vacuuming
extra_info.description: If you press down on the control stick while holding the special-move button to suck things up with the gun, you can fall through platforms.

tips.name: Crownerang
tips.description: Throw the crown like a boomerang. While you're throwing it, you'll have super armor and can withstand weak attacks. If you don't catch the crown, you can't attack with it again until you retrieve it.

extra_info.name: The Crown as an Item
extra_info.description: If your crown falls to the ground, it can be picked up by other fighters and used as an item. If your crown falls off the stage, it will reappear after a while.

tips.name: Propellerpack
tips.description: Tilt the control stick to fly in that direction. You can travel pretty far, and you can also attack with the propeller.

tips.name: Gut Check
tips.description: A counter move where King K. Rool sticks out his belly. If his belly is hit during this time, he'll counterattack not only direct attacks, but projectiles as well.

extra_info.name: Gut Check Behind
extra_info.description: If you enter an input toward King K. Rool's backside right after successfully using this counter, he can quickly look back and counter.

tips.name: Blast-o-Matic
tips.description: Shoot Donkey Kong Island with the Blast-o-Matic from the island fortress. All opponents hit by King K. Rool's initial attack will be blasted.

tips.name: Piledriver
tips.description: Throw a grabbed opponent downward to bury them in the ground. The more damage an opponent has, the longer they will stay buried.
Some attacks, like side tilt attacks and neutral air attacks, cause King K. Rool's belly to have super armor. This allows you to land attacks even when getting hit by incoming attacks.
His belly's super armor is very reliable, but once it takes damage, a crack will appear. The crack will grow as it takes more damage, until finally King K. Rool becomes stunned while his belly heals.
Isabelle first appeared in Animal Crossing: New Leaf in 2013. She is a secretary that supports the main character, who happens to become the mayor of the town.
Isabelle is loved by all the residents and is always happy to help. She loves festivals and changes her outfit depending on the season and event.

tips.name: Pocket
tips.description: Use this move to put items or incoming projectiles into Isabelle's pocket. Press the button again to take the item or projectile back out of her pocket.

tips.name: Toss with Fishing Rod
tips.description: Cast the fishing hook to catch opponents, then reel them in and launch them forward, backward, up, or down. Opponents and even items can be caught while reeling in the line as well!

extra_info.name: Fishing Rod's Hook
extra_info.description: If the fishing hook catches the edge of the stage, you can pull yourself up with it. You can also catch nearby opponents with the hook and bring them down with you.

tips.name: Balloon Trip
tips.description: Unlike the Villager, Isabelle will float up sitting on a swing-like seat attached to bunny-shaped balloons. The move is pretty much the same as the Villager's, but it's, like, super cute.

tips.name: Lloid Trap
tips.description: If someone walks over the buried Lloid, it will rise up and deal damage. After burying Lloid, you can do another down special to make Lloid rise on command.

tips.name: Dream Town Hall
tips.description: Tom Nook and his gang will show up and build the town hall from Animal Crossing: New Leaf on top of an opponent. At the end, it will explode, launching anyone nearby.
Incineroar's debut was in Pokémon Sun and Moon released in 2016. Incineroar has a pro-wrestler vibe going on, and can use the powerful Blaze ability.
Incineroar is the final form of Litten, one of the three Pokémon you can choose at the beginning of Pokémon Sun and Moon. It's a well-balanced Pokémon with very high attack and defense.

tips.name: Darkest Lariat
tips.description: Become invincible right after using the move and deal big damage. When Incineroar is swinging its arms, you can make it go forward or backward with the control stick.

tips.name: Alolan Whip
tips.description: Grab an opponent and throw them into the ropes before following up with an attack. The follow-up attack will change depending on the timing you press the button when the opponent comes back.

tips.name: Controls during Cross Chop
tips.description: When falling down, tilt the control stick left or right to slightly change your landing location. Also, you can go through platforms by tilting the control stick downward.

extra_info.name: Recovery after Cross Chop
extra_info.description: Even after diving downward with Cross Chop, you can use your midair jump and side special. You might get lucky and make it back to the stage!

tips.name: Revenge
tips.description: If hit shortly after using Revenge, the attack power of Incineroar's next move will increase and Incineroar will glow red. However, if Incineroar is thrown or gets hit hard, the effect will vanish.

tips.name: Max Malicious Moonsault
tips.description: Incineroar charges forward, grabs an opponent, and hits them with a series of attacks. It's easier to land the attack right after the opponent breaks free from a grab.
After certain attacks hit, Incineroar will pose and taunt like a pro wrestler. You can move during these taunts, so they don't actually leave you vulnerable.
#1 is Incineroar, #2 is Robin, and #3 is Jigglypuff. Incineroar may be slow, but has some serious launching power.
Piranha Plants first appeared in Super Mario Bros., released in 1985 for the NES. Many of them live in pipes and like to suddenly pop out at regular intervals.
There are about 60 subspecies of Piranha Plant. Their biologies and personalities are unique.
The first Piranha Plant was green with yellow dots. The red version with white spots first appeared in Super Mario Bros. 3.

tips.name: Ptooie
tips.description: If you tilt the control stick left or right while blowing the spiked ball upward, you can launch it in that direction.

extra_info.name: Ptooie Height
extra_info.description: Press and hold the special-move button to keep the spiked ball in the air. The closer it is to Piranha Plant's mouth, the farther you can launch it left or right.

extra_info.name: Repeated Ptooie
extra_info.description: You can have only one spiked ball out at a time. If you try to use this move again before the first spiked ball disappears, you will be left vulnerable.

extra_info.name: Ptooie Power
extra_info.description: The spiked ball loses power each time it hits the stage or a fighter. Also, once it loses enough speed and hits the ground, it won't deal damage to anyone.

tips.name: Charging Poison Breath
tips.description: Press the special-move button to start charging Poison Breath, then press it again to spit out a poison cloud. The cloud's size, duration, and damage will increase the longer it's charged.

extra_info.name: Storing Poison Breath Charge
extra_info.description: If you shield, dodge, or jump while charging the poison cloud, you can store the charge, move around, and unleash it later.

extra_info.name: Poison Breath Damage
extra_info.description: The poison cloud can't be destroyed, but it can be countered or absorbed. Also, the center of the cloud deals the most damage.

extra_info.name: Poison Breath's Duration
extra_info.description: The duration of the poison cloud becomes shorter as the number of fighters increases.

tips.name: Piranhacopter
tips.description: Tilt the control stick left or right while rising to move slightly in that direction. The spinning leaves will hit and damage anyone they hit.

tips.name: Long-Stem Strike Charge
tips.description: Press and hold the special-move button to charge up power. The longer you charge, the more distance the attack will have.

extra_info.name: Long-Stem Strike Direction
extra_info.description: Tilt the control stick while charging to aim the attack. You can tilt farther in the air than on the ground.

extra_info.name: Long-Stem Strike Armor
extra_info.description: Piranha Plant gains super armor while charging this move. You'll lose the armor as soon as you stop charging.

extra_info.name: Long-Stem Strike Bite
extra_info.description: If a fighter is in range when Piranha Plant extends, it will automatically bite the fighter. If you press the special-move button while Piranha Plant is extending, you can bite before it fully extends.

tips.name: Petey Piranha
tips.description: Petey Piranha can trap up to two fighters in cages and deal a great amount of damage. You can't escape until the Final Smash ends or Petey Piranha is KO'd.

extra_info.name: Petey Piranha's Movement
extra_info.description: You can make Petey Piranha move left or right. You can also make Petey jump.

tips.name: Push and Pull
tips.description: Press the attack button after unleashing the move to perform an additional bite attack. But if you miss the first attack, missing the second attack as well may leave you open to counterattack.

extra_info.name: Pull Technique
extra_info.description: The second attack can be delayed to catch opponents off guard. If the first attack is blocked, you still may be able to land the second attack.

tips.name: Prickly Swing
tips.description: When Piranha Plant unleashes a side smash attack, it turns into a Prickly Piranha Plant from Super Mario Galaxy 2, complete with red spikes on its head.

tips.name: Fire Breath
tips.description: Piranha Plant spits out an explosive flame. As with most explosions, it has great launch power.

tips.name: Flowerpot Meteor
tips.description: Piranha Plant swings the flowerpot (or pipe) downward quickly. The move comes out fast and may surprise opponents. It has a meteor effect at the beginning.
Piranha Plant will automatically bite if it's crouching and an opponent steps on it. It's an involuntary reflex learned from other games Piranha Plant stars in.

tips.name: Costumes
tips.description: Piranha Plant's odd-numbered costumes feature flowerpots, and its even-numbered costumes feature pipes.
Joker made his debut in Persona 5, released in 2017. He's a young man with a strong sense of justice and is the leader of the Phantom Thieves of Hearts, a group who changes the hearts of evildoers.
Joker transferred to Shujin Academy after a certain incident and then acquired Persona skills. While in his thief form, he gives off a different vibe than his usual personality.

tips.name: Gun / Gun Special
tips.description: Joker quickly fires a gun with his left hand. Press the button repeatedly or hold it to fire continuously. While Arsene is summoned, the number of shots fired and the power of the shots increases.

extra_info.name: Jumping during Gun / Gun Special
extra_info.description: If you press the jump button immediately after firing your gun on the ground, you'll jump into the air and shoot straight down. You can also use the stick to move left and right in the air.

extra_info.name: Dodging during Gun / Gun Special
extra_info.description: While holding the special-move button to fire your gun, quickly flick the stick to the left or right to make Joker dodge while continuing to fire.

extra_info.name: Vulnerability While Gun Dodging
extra_info.description: Dodging while shooting makes you invincible for a shorter time than a normal dodge, and repeated dodging will slow your reaction time and leave you even more vulnerable.

extra_info.name: Gun / Gun Special in the Air
extra_info.description: Once you fire a shot in the air, tilt the stick up and press the button to fire rapidly. If you tilt the stick down and press the button, you can continue your move by shooting downward.

tips.name: Eiha
tips.description: Fire a bolt that explodes and deals continuous damage. If the bolt hits an opponent, it will knock the opponent into the air.

extra_info.name: Eigaon
extra_info.description: While Arsene is summoned, Joker will use Eigaon, which is bigger and faster than Eiha and also has more launch power and higher continuous damage. When it hits the terrain, it explodes.

extra_info.name: Eiha/Eigaon Damage
extra_info.description: The continuous damage after the bolt explodes is greater than the damage of hitting an opponent with the bolt itself.

tips.name: Grappling Hook
tips.description: Shoot a grappling hook diagonally upward. If it hits an opponent, they'll be pulled closer and thrown. If it hits an edge while you're in midair, you'll cling to the edge. If it hits an item, you'll pull it toward you.

extra_info.name: Wings of Rebellion
extra_info.description: When Arsene is summoned, you can use Wings of Rebellion to fly upward a long distance. It has great range, but it can't be used to attack.

extra_info.name: Wings of Rebellion and Moving Left and Right
extra_info.description: If you tilt the stick left or right before rising up, you can slightly change the angle of ascent. Tilting the stick while falling will allow you to move slightly as well.

tips.name: Rebel's Guard
tips.description: Joker will pose once using the move and take a hit from the opponent. You will take less damage from the attack, and your Rebellion Gauge will fill up greatly.

extra_info.name: After Rebel's Guard
extra_info.description: Press and hold the button to extend the duration of the move. Once you're hit with an attack, release the button to unleash a force that pushes opponents.

extra_info.name: Tetrakarn / Makarakarn
extra_info.description: A move that can be used while Arsene is summoned. You can counter direct attacks from opponents with Tetrakarn. Projectiles will be reflected with Makarakarn.

tips.name: All-Out Attack
tips.description: Joker dashes forward to attack. If it hits an opponent, the Phantom Thieves will perform an All-Out Attack. There are two versions and different members will appear. There are also six different spoken lines.

extra_info.name: Charging with All-Out Attack
extra_info.description: You can hit up to four opponents with the initial dash forward. Even if it doesn't hit anyone, you can tilt the stick the opposite direction immediately after missing to dash the other way.

extra_info.name: End the Battle with an All-Out Attack
extra_info.description: If an opponent is damaged enough by the end of the attack, they'll be instantly KO'd. If you end the battle with this KO, you might get to see a special cinematic.

tips.description: While Arsene is summoned, your down air attack gains a meteor effect.

If you receive damage or get hit while using Rebel's Guard, the Rebellion Gauge will fill up. Once it's full, Joker will summon Arsene. Your special moves will change, and your attacks will be stronger.
While Arsene is present, the Rebellion Gauge will not charge. The gauge will deplete as time passes or if Joker takes damage, and once it's completely empty, Arsene will exit the scene.
When your teammates are damaged or KO'd during Team Battle, the Rebellion Gauge will fill a little bit.
When it's 1 vs. 1 or the opponent is a CPU, there is a cut-in when Arsene appears.

This hero first appeared in DRAGON QUEST XI: Echoes of an Elusive Age, released in 2018 in Europe and North America. He leaves his hometown to meet with the king before being labeled "The Darkspawn" and hunted.
tips.name: Hero:Origins

tips.description: This hero first appeared in DRAGON QUEST III: The Seeds of Salvation, released as Dragon Warrior III in 1992 in North America. The hero carries on his father's will and embarks on a journey to defeat the archfiend Baramos.
tips.name: Hero:Origins

tips.description: This hero first appeared in DRAGON QUEST IV: Chapters of the Chosen, released in 1992 in North America. A descendant of the Zenithians, the hero teams up with his friends to save the world after his hometown is destroyed.
tips.name: Hero:Origins

tips.description: This hero first appeared in DRAGON QUEST VIII: Journey of the Cursed King, released in 2005 in North America. A royal guardsman, he embarks on a journey to undo a curse placed upon his king and princess.
tips.name: Hero:Origins
Charging Up Specials
tips.description: The more they're charged, the more powerful these moves become. If you have enough MP, you can charge them up to a maximum of three levels.

tips.name: Frizz/Frizzle/Kafrizz
tips.description: Start charging with the special-move button. Press the button again to unleash a fireball spell. You can store your charge if you shield, dodge, or jump while charging.

tips.name: Zap/Zapple/Kazap
tips.description: Press and hold the special-move button to imbue your sword with lightning magic. The move will be unleashed when you release the button or when it reaches maximum charge. The charge cannot be stored.

extra_info.name: Kazap
extra_info.description: Charge it all the way to point the sword skyward and summon lightning. You'll then unleash a spin attack that has a wide range. Foes that you hit with the spin attack will be struck by a lightning bolt.

tips.name: Woosh/Swoosh/Kaswoosh
tips.description: Use a wind spell to summon a tornado underneath you that will slash nearby enemies and lift you up. The more it's charged, the higher you will rise. If you don't have enough MP, you will hop slightly.

extra_info.name: Swoosh and Kaswoosh Characteristics
extra_info.description: Swoosh allows you to move left or right easily, and Kaswoosh allows you to rise higher in the air. Choose which move to use depending on the situation.

tips.name: Canceling Command Selection
tips.description: You can close the command window with the jump or shield buttons. However, you'll need to wait a little while before opening it again.

extra_info.name: Bang/Kaboom
extra_info.description: A light spell that unleashes a bolt that explodes when it hits an opponent or after a time. Kaboom draws opponents near before exploding and causes a bigger explosion than Bang.

extra_info.name: Sizz/Sizzle
extra_info.description: A fire spell that sends out a quick spark. When it hits the landscape or an opponent, it bursts into flames. Sizzle's shot is bigger than Sizz, and it is more powerful.

extra_info.name: Whack/Thwack
extra_info.description: These moves have a chance to instantly KO opponents they hit. The KO success rate increases as you and your opponent take damage. Thwack has a wider range than Whack.

extra_info.name: Kamikazee
extra_info.description: Deal damage to nearby opponents by self-destructing. You will be KO'd. Your opponent gets credit for the KO in a 1-on-1 battle.

extra_info.name: Magic Burst
extra_info.description: Use all your MP to create an explosion with you at the center. The power and size of the explosion grows with every point of MP used.

extra_info.name: Snooze
extra_info.description: Shoot a projectile that puts opponents to sleep. The size of the projectile increases the longer it travels. However, the closer the opponent is, the deeper they'll sleep.

extra_info.name: Heal
extra_info.description: Heal some of the damage you've taken. If you use it multiple times, it will not come up as a selectable command again until you get KO'd.

extra_info.name: Oomph
extra_info.description: Increase your launch power and melee attack power for a while. However, your defense will decrease a little.

extra_info.name: Acceleratle
extra_info.description: Increase your movement speed and jump for a while. You will also become easier to launch.

extra_info.name: Bounce
extra_info.description: Automatically reflects opponents' projectiles for a while. The reflected projectiles will be powered up after being reflected.

extra_info.name: Kaclang
extra_info.description: Block attacks by becoming metal for a while. However, you cannot move until the status wears off, and you cannot cancel it yourself.

extra_info.name: Zoom
extra_info.description: Rise straight upward in order to return to the stage. You can't be hit by opponents while rising, but you can still run into the stage's terrain.

extra_info.name: Hocus Pocus
extra_info.description: Try your luck and hope for the best. This move may randomly trigger other commands, or it may change your status or size.

extra_info.name: Flame Slash / Kacrackle Slash
extra_info.description: Wreathe your sword in flame for Flame Slash or in ice for Kacrackle Slash. When Kacrackle Slash hits, it freezes opponents for a short time.

extra_info.name: Hatchet Man
extra_info.description: Slash opponents with full force. It takes a bit of time to attack, but if it hits, it lands a critical hit that deals massive damage.

extra_info.name: Metal Slash
extra_info.description: Hit an opponent that is metal or has the Kaclang status to instantly KO them. However, nonmetal opponents will only receive one percent damage.

extra_info.name: Psyche Up
extra_info.description: Charge up power to make your next melee attack stronger. The effect ends once you hit an opponent, but it will not end if you miss.

tips.name: Gigaslash
tips.description: Hero makes a wide sweep with his sword. If it hits an opponent, protagonists from different generations will lend you their strength so you can unleash a deadly blow.
Press the attack button a second time to unleash an additional attack. The first attack uses the shield to repel incoming attacks.
Jab the sword downward. The move has a meteor effect at the beginning of the move.
You will miss and become vulnerable if you try to use a special move without enough MP. You'll recover MP as time passes and as you deal damage to opponents.
Smash attacks have a 1/8 chance to be critical hits. If a critical hit strikes an opponent, it'll deal massive damage!
The first time the duo appeared was in Banjo-Kazooie, released in 1998. Banjo first appeared in Diddy Kong Racing, which was released in 1997.
The two set out on a journey to save Banjo's sister Tooty who was abducted by Gruntilda. They adventure through many stages that require them to work together to overcome obstacles.

tips.name: Egg Firing
tips.description: Kazooie can fire eggs from her mouth. The eggs will bounce when they hit the ground and will explode if they hit an opponent.

extra_info.name: Breegull Blaster
extra_info.description: Press and hold the button to make Banjo pick Kazooie up and rapidly shoot eggs. You can move and jump while firing eggs this way.

extra_info.name: Canceling Breegull Blaster
extra_info.description: Breegull Blaster will end if you try to crouch, shield, or use a different special move. However, the action you used to cancel Breegull Blaster won't happen unless you press and hold the inputs.

extra_info.name: Breegull Blaster Power
extra_info.description: The egg's power will decrease if fired continuously. The power will drop up to two times, but it will start to return to normal power once you put the Breegull Blaster away.

tips.name: Wonderwing
tips.description: Use a Golden Feather to become invincible and charge forward. The powerful tackle is useful for returning to the stage, but if you use up all the feathers, you can't use the move anymore.

extra_info.name: Wonderwing Tackle
extra_info.description: You won't take damage or get knocked back by attacks while charging forward. However, being blocked by a shielding opponent will leave you vulnerable!

extra_info.name: Wonderwing Feather
extra_info.description: You can use five Golden Feathers in standard battles and one during Sudden Death. Your Golden Feathers will not be replenished until you are KO'd.

extra_info.name: Wonderwing in Stamina Mode
extra_info.description: In a stamina battle, the number of available Golden Feathers will vary depending on the initial stamina amount.

tips.name: Shock Spring Jump
tips.description: Use a Shock Spring Pad to jump straight up. After using this ability, you can freely move and attack while in the air.

extra_info.name: Shock Spring Jump Charge
extra_info.description: You can jump higher if you press and hold the button to charge up power before jumping off of the Shock Spring Pad.

extra_info.name: Attacking with Shock Spring Pad
extra_info.description: The dropped Shock Spring Pad can hit opponents. The attack power won't change even if the move is charged.

tips.name: Rear Egg
tips.description: Kazooie will shoot grenade eggs diagonally upward in the direction she isn't facing. Make sure to turn around before letting loose.

extra_info.name: Rear Egg's Characteristics
extra_info.description: Grenade eggs aren't perfect circles and have an odd bounce as a result. You can pick one up to throw, but if the timed fuse runs out while you're still holding it, the explosion will damage you!

extra_info.name: Only One Rear Egg
extra_info.description: Only one grenade egg can exist on the stage at any time. If you try to use the move again while your grenade egg exists, you'll be left vulnerable.

tips.name: The Mighty Jinjonator
tips.description: Summon a statue from the ground. If it hits an opponent, the Mighty Jinjonator will be awakened from the statue and unleash continuous high-speed strikes alongside other Jinjos to launch the opponent.

tips.name: Twirling Wing Whack
tips.description: Kazooie will spread her wings and twirl to attack in all directions. This attack will reach farther when used while moving to the side rather than up or down.

tips.name: Beak Buster
tips.description: This move has a meteor effect at the beginning. However you will dive quickly, so be careful of self-destructs.
Buries a grabbed opponent in the ground. The higher their damage is, the longer they will be buried.
You can perform up to two midair jumps. You can jump after using their up special too, making it easy to recover.
Kazooie won't usually get hit by opponents' attacks. However, she will get hit while running.
Kazooie will come out of the backpack and carry Banjo while running. In the original game, you could run faster and run up steep hills with this ability.
Terry first appeared in 1991 in the FATAL FURY: King of Fighters arcade game. The game was unique for its quirky characters and its two-lane fighting system!
To defeat Geese Howard and avenge his adoptive father, Jeff Bogard, Terry mastered martial arts. He joins and wins the ultimate martial arts tournament, The King of Fighters, to get near Geese.

How long the special-move button is held down will affect its strength. Attacks will reach farther and be more powerful the longer the button is held, but you'll also be open to attacks while you're charging up.

tips.name: Power Wave
tips.description: Hit the ground with a fist to send a shock wave out ahead of you. The longer you hold down the special-move button, the farther the move will go and the faster it'll travel!

extra_info.name: Airborne Wave
extra_info.description: Terry's neutral special isn't just for ground attacks. Use this ability while in the air or near an edge and the fire will continue forward. It has a short range, but it's great for keeping someone from returning.

extra_info.name: Power Wave Hang Time
extra_info.description: The first time Terry's neutral special is used while airborne will cause Terry to stop descending for a moment. This is a good way to throw off an opponent who was expecting you to fall normally!

tips.description: Terry has two different side specials at his disposal. You can trigger them by either tilting the stick forward or backward and then pushing the special-move button. Both moves make Terry charge forward.

tips.name: Burning Knuckle
tips.description: Terry will raise his hands then charge forward, fist extended. This move is most powerful right when he starts to charge. Be careful, though; Terry is vulnerable at the start and end of the attack!

tips.name: Burning Knuckle
tips.description: Quickly use command input ↓ ↘ → + attack button while facing right to increase the power of the attack. This command input will also make the extended arm invincible at the beginning of the move.

tips.name: Crack Shoot
tips.description: Perform a flying somersault forward, dropping the heel for a powerful blow. The tip of the foot is the strongest point, and the arch of the attack makes it a good counter to aerial attacks.

extra_info.name: Reversed Crack Shoot
extra_info.description: While airborne, tilt the stick away from the direction Terry is facing for a bit and he'll perform his Crack Shoot in that direction instead. Using the command input while airborne will still attack forward!

tips.name: Crack Shoot
tips.description: Quickly use the command input ↓ ↙ ← + attack button while facing right to deal more damage and increase the move's launch power.

tips.name: Rising Tackle
tips.description: Raise up into the air with a combo of spinning arms and legs, damaging enemies above you. You can slightly control the direction of the attack by tilting the stick left or right as you ascend.

extra_info.name: Rising Tackle Traits
extra_info.description: You will be invincible from the waist down at the beginning of the move, so it's easy to overcome attacks from above. However, you will be left vulnerable after the attack ends.

tips.name: Rising Tackle
tips.description: Hold ↓ for a moment, then input ↑ + attack button to increase the power of the attack and how high up you'll travel. Also, your whole body will be invincible at the start of the move.

tips.name: Power Dunk
tips.description: Hit the opponent into the air with your knee, then slam downward with a powerful punch. Land the first blow and it will become a combo. This continued attack can leave you vulnerable in the air, however.

tips.name: Power Dunk
tips.description: Quickly use the command input → ↓ ↘ + attack button while facing right to increase the power of this move. Additionally, using this command input will make your whole body invincible when you first jump.

extra_info.name: Power Dunk's Meteor Effect
extra_info.description: Unleashing this move using the command input will cause the end of the downward punch to have a meteor effect but only if the attack button is held. Otherwise the punch will not have a meteor effect.

tips.description: You can unleash a Super Special Move in a pinch using a command input. You must have 100% or more accumulated damage, or your stamina must be at 1/3 or less in Stamina mode.
When you see Go near your accumulated damage, that means you can use Super Special Moves! You won't be able to use them in the air, though, so be careful!
Input the command with right and left reversed to unleash the move behind you instead!
These moves have super armor at the beginning. They are also able to withstand and overwhelm neutral attacks.

tips.name: Power Geyser
tips.description: You can unleash this move (after taking significant damage) by quickly using the command input ↓ ↙ ← ↙ → + attack button while facing right. The base of the attack deals more damage than its higher points.

extra_info.name: Buster Wolf
