extra_info.description: You can use this move only after taking significant damage. Quickly use the command input ↓ ↘ → ↓ ↘ → + attack button while facing right. Terry will charge forward, fist extended, dealing tons of damage if he connects.

tips.description: You can use Super Special Moves with shorter inputs. Power Geyser is ↓ ← ↓ → + attack button while facing right. Buster Wolf is ↓ → ↓ → + attack button.

tips.name: Triple Wolf
tips.description: This powerful three-part move starts with Triple Geyser, continues with Power Dunk, and ends with Buster Wolf. Triple Geyser will launch opponents, then Terry will unleash his combo on the first person he hits.

extra_info.name: Triple Wolf's Pull
extra_info.description: The initial three pillars of energy will hit multiple opponents and deal tons of damage. You can launch only one opponent with the final blow, but the three pillars of energy are great for a hectic battle.

tips.description: Avoid becoming vulnerable by using a special move the moment you hit an opponent with attacks like neutral attacks or tilt attacks. You can carry out some amazing combos!
The second stage of the neutral attack is actually two separate hits! You can execute a special move at the end of the second hit for even more damage!
The launch power is weaker compared to other throws. However, you are less vulnerable after the throw, and it's easier to follow up with additional attacks.

If you press the attack button while dodging in place, you can use the Dodge Attack! It works like Terry's up tilt attack; you'll be invincible from the waist up, making it easier to fight against airborne foes!
In 1-on-1 fights, you will face the opponent automatically. Be aware of this when using your command-input moves!
Byleth first appeared in the Fire Emblem: Three Houses game, released in 2019. Byleth became a professor at the Officers Academy and led one of three classes.

Byleth lived as a mercenary but became a professor after rescuing students of the Officers Academy. Byleth would go on to teach at the school, fighting alongside and commanding the students in battle.
Each move uses a sword, axe, lance, or bow that appears in the original game. The axe, lance, and bow are the Heroes' Relics of the three house leaders, and the sword is Byleth's Hero's Relic.

tips.name: Failnaught
tips.description: Press the special-move button to draw the bow and prepare a powerful attack. It takes time to unleash, but the arrow moves fast and travels far!

extra_info.name: Failnaught Charged
extra_info.description: Hold down the button and charge to unleash a beam of light straight across. The attack will usually hit immediately and is extremely powerful against opponents directly in the line of fire.

extra_info.name: Canceling Failnaught
extra_info.description: It's possible to dodge in place or shield when you first begin to draw the bow, but the move will reset if you do so. Once the charge begins after holding down the button, you won't be able to dodge or shield.

extra_info.name: Turning with Failnaught
extra_info.description: While drawing the bow, you can turn and face the opposite way by tilting in that direction. This won't interrupt the attack, but it can only be done at the beginning of the move.

tips.name: Areadbhar
tips.description: Swing this legendary lance in front of you, using its long reach to cover the area ahead and upward. The bladed part of the weapon is the most powerful part.

extra_info.name: Flicking the Stick with Areadbhar
extra_info.description: If you flick the stick and input your attack, you will swing up while charging forward. Pay close attention to how far you are from your opponent, and use this technique to surprise them!

extra_info.name: Using Areadbhar in the Air
extra_info.description: When using this move in the air, the attack strikes in a wide arc from below upward. Flicking the stick will allow you to move forward a little too!

tips.name: Sword of the Creator
tips.description: Extend the sword upward and diagonally. If this attack hits an opponent, the blade will quickly shorten and bring you close to the enemy. After the jump, you can move about freely in the air.

extra_info.name: Sword of the Creator and Walls
extra_info.description: If the blade's tip hits a wall, the sword will shorten and pull you toward the wall. It's possible to then jump off the wall and follow up with another jump or an attack!

extra_info.name: Sword of the Creator and Edges
extra_info.description: If you use this move near an edge and it connects, the sword's blade will bury into the edge and you'll dangle freely. Tilt the stick or press a button and the sword will shorten, pulling you toward the edge.

extra_info.name: Sword of the Creator and Footstooling Opponents
extra_info.description: If the opponent's accumulated damage is 50% or more, the footstool part of this attack will have a meteor effect. Also, the farther the sword is extended, the more power it will have when footstooling someone.

tips.name: Aymr
tips.description: Charge up the axe, then swing down with incredible force. It takes a while to prepare this attack, so using it can leave you vulnerable, but enemies on the receiving end of this can suffer serious damage.

extra_info.name: Turn and Attack with Aymr
extra_info.description: If you draw the axe and then input the opposite direction from the way you're facing, you will turn and unleash this attack in the indicated direction instead.

extra_info.name: Aymr's Super Armor
extra_info.description: If this attack is charged while standing on the ground, you'll have super armor until you swing the axe.

tips.name: Progenitor God Ruptured Heaven
tips.description: Swing the sword, striking in a wide arc in front of you. This attack can hit up to three opponents. Combine Sothis's strength with the Sword of the Creator to unleash a powerful attack.
Extend the lance for a long-range, forward-facing attack! The tip of the lance is the strongest part, and you can adjust the attack's angle up or down. This attack is at its best when angled upward.
Extend the sword and swing it around and up in a wide area. This attack lasts longer than some foes may expect, so it's easy to catch unprepared opponents off guard.

You can swing the axe forward and backward, dealing significant damage to anyone it hits. Be careful, though; the damage and launch power of this attack is high, but the move can leave you vulnerable.
Swing the axe downward. Try to hit your opponent with the tip of the axe just as it reaches the lowest point. If you can pull it off, the blow will have a meteor effect.
Side specials, side smash attacks, forward air attacks, and back air attacks all make use of the lance. Remember that the tip of the blade is the most powerful point, and the lance has a long reach.
Down specials, down smash attacks, and down air attacks all use the axe. The axe is slow and can leave you vulnerable, but that lack of speed is made up for with the devastating power of its attacks.
Min Min first appeared in the game ARMS in 2017. She's a well-known worker at the Mintendo Noodle House, which serves delicious ramen. She actually entered the Grand Prix to advertise her shop!
Min Min is petite with skinny arms, but when she charges up, her left ARM turns into a dragon, empowering her punches. She also likes to employ kicks in the air, deflecting an opponent's ARM.

Use the attack button to unleash a punch with the left ARM, and use the special-move button to punch with the right ARM. Flick the stick with either move to turn it into a smash attack, increasing range and power.
The punch unleashed by flicking the stick is at its strongest just before the ARM is fully extended. The attack is weaker at the beginning of the punch and when the ARM reaches its maximum range.
Holding down the attack or special-move button and flicking the stick will charge up power for a charged smash attack. This will also change the move that is used.
If you're on the ground and you tilt up or down just before unleashing a punch to the left or right, you can move the ARMS in an arc. The way the ARM bends depends on the type of ARM.
Even with an ARM extended, you can still move around and jump. During smash attacks, you can attack with the other ARM, too!

It's possible for Min Min to attack in both directions! While attacking in one direction with one ARM extended, move the stick in the opposite direction and press the button for the other ARM to send it that way!
Your ARMS will bounce back if they hit a wall or the floor. They'll also bounce back if an opponent shields the attack, which will leave you vulnerable to a counterattack!

The left ARM will gain power and change into a dragon when you throw an opponent or when your Final Smash is ready. Shortly after throwing an opponent, or if Min Min cannot move, the ARM will return to normal.
The right ARM has three variations: Ramram is very bendy, making it easier to hit opponents. Megawatt is slow but powerful. The Dragon can extend out and shoot lasers!

The Ramram is a light ARM that will arc in flight. It's not powerful, but it extends quickly and is bendy, keeping enemies on their toes. It can extend through walls or the ground while you are airborne.

The Megawatt is a heavy ARM that is a lot like an iron ball. When charged, it becomes engulfed in electricity. It doesn't arc easily or extend quickly, but it's super powerful. Perfect for a final blow.
The Dragon is an ARM that is shaped like a dragon. It can be equipped on both arms. If you press the attack button when the ARM is fully extended in a smash attack, it fires a laser!

tips.name: ARMS Jump
tips.description: When neither ARM is extended and you're on the ground, this move will send you flying upward. You'll be invincible the moment you jump, and charging the move will extend the height and invincibility time!

extra_info.name: Maneuvering the ARMS Jump
extra_info.description: If you tilt the stick to the left or right before you jump, you can leap diagonally. You can also move to the left or right while falling.

extra_info.name: Using ARM Hook
extra_info.description: Use this move in the air to extend an ARM diagonally upward. If it hits an edge, you'll be able to dangle from the edge instead of falling. Tilt up on the stick or press a button to pull yourself up.

tips.name: ARM Change
tips.description: The right ARM can be swapped between Ramram, Megawatt, and Dragon. You won't be able to swap ARMS out while attacking or if you are currently unable to move.

tips.name: ARMS Rush
tips.description: The left ARM will transform into a dragon to unleash an attack. Only one opponent will get caught up in the rush of punches that various ARMS fighters will swoop in to deliver, sending that fighter flying.
Hit the attack or special-move button quickly on the ground to unleash a three-kick attack. Hit the button multiple times during the combo to turn it into a flurry attack!

tips.name: Reflecting Projectiles
tips.description: Min Min's up smash is a kick capable of reflecting projectile attacks. Reflected projectiles will increase in speed and power, however.
Min Min's neutral air attack swings an ARM out at opponents. The speed and power of the attack will change depending on which ARM is currently equipped.
Steve and Alex are two of the many incarnations of the player when they venture into the Minecraft world. Explore dungeons, create buildings, or trade with villagers—the adventure is in your hands!
tips.name: In Their Series

tips.description: The P7 appearance is the Zombie, known to appear from the darkness and attack people. Fortunately, the Zombies in Minecraft catch fire when they touch sunlight, burning away after a bit.
tips.name: Zombies from the Series

tips.description: The P8 appearance is the Enderman, one of the residents of the End. They like to teleport about, quietly carrying blocks. However, if you lock eyes with one, it will turn into a strong enemy and attack you!
tips.name: Enderman from the Series

tips.description: You'll make use of a sword, axe, shovel, or pickaxe, depending on which move you use. Each tool will break after some use, which will reduce the power of any attack that uses that tool.

Materials are necessary for the moves Steve brings to the battle. If you don't have the needed materials, the move will be weaker than normal or even fail outright! Keep track of the materials you have on hand.

tips.name: Mine
tips.description: Hold the button down, and you can dig for materials below you or in nearby walls. The speed that you mine and what materials you uncover changes depending on where you are mining.

extra_info.name: Mineable Materials
extra_info.description: In order of easiest to hardest to obtain, you can mine dirt, wood, stone, iron, redstone, gold, and diamond. Depending on the ground in the stage, you might dig up ice, sand, or wool instead of dirt.

extra_info.name: High-Quality Mineable Materials
extra_info.description: Iron, gold, redstone, and diamonds can be obtained from any kind of surface.

extra_info.name: Mining and the Material Gauge
extra_info.description: Some of the materials you collect will be shown as a gauge near your damage. However, gold and diamond will be displayed separately, and redstone won't be displayed at all.

extra_info.name: Quality of Mined Material
extra_info.description: The harder the walls and floors are, the better the quality of the materials you'll mine. However, it will also take longer to dig up those materials.

extra_info.name: Mining Limits
extra_info.description: There is a limit on how much of each material you can carry, and the rarer the material, the less of it you can hold. Any material you dig up beyond the limit will be discarded.

extra_info.name: Mining Tools
extra_info.description: You use different tools depending on the type of wall or ground you're digging into. These tools help you mine faster, and when one breaks, it'll slow down how quickly you can mine.

extra_info.name: Mining, Tools, and Quality
extra_info.description: There are five levels of quality for the various tools: diamond, gold, iron, stone, and wood. Generally, a higher-quality material is more durable and allows you to mine faster, though gold has its own properties.

extra_info.name: While Mining
extra_info.description: While mining, you can jump or move forward or backward, but you won't be able to attack. If you move forward or backward, you'll keep on mining, but jumping will cancel the action.

extra_info.name: Mining on Battlefield-Form Stages
extra_info.description: All of the Battlefield-form and Ω-form stages have the same amounts of the various materials available for mining.

extra_info.name: Craft
extra_info.description: While in front of the crafting table, pressing the special-move button will use the materials you have to create, upgrade, or repair tools.

extra_info.name: Crafting Tools
extra_info.description: Use the crafting table when you have a broken tool, and you'll craft the highest-quality tool with the materials available automatically. If this is an improvement on your other tools, they'll all be upgraded too!

extra_info.name: Using Craft to Upgrade
extra_info.description: If you have the materials to make a higher-quality tool, use Craft to create the upgraded tool. In fact, all tools will upgrade at the same time.

extra_info.name: Using Craft to Repair
extra_info.description: You can repair damaged tools by using the same materials that they were crafted with. This is much quicker than crafting a new tool after one breaks.

extra_info.name: Canceling Craft
extra_info.description: The move can be canceled by jumping, shielding, dodging, or pressing the attack button. You won't lose material if you cancel Craft this way.

extra_info.name: Crafting with Gold
extra_info.description: Gold tools break easier than ones made from any other material, but they are also the quickest to mine with and the quickest for unleashing moves.

extra_info.name: Crafting with Diamond
extra_info.description: Diamond tools are very powerful, but it takes longer to craft them than it does to craft tools using the other materials.

extra_info.name: Moving the Crafting Table
extra_info.description: Press the special-move button while shielding to use some materials and reposition your crafting table nearby. Moving the crafting table will use material in the following order: wood, stone, then iron.

extra_info.name: Using Create Block
extra_info.description: If you press the special-move button in the air, you'll use your materials to create and place a block at your feet. Holding down the button and moving or jumping will continuously place blocks.

extra_info.name: Materials and Create Block
extra_info.description: You create blocks from the materials you've mined, starting with dirt, then wood, then stone, and finally iron. The different materials all have different durabilities.

extra_info.name: Block Limits
extra_info.description: There is a limit on how many blocks you can place at one time. Once you've reached the limit, each new block you create will cause the oldest block you placed to go away.

extra_info.name: Block Rules
extra_info.description: You cannot place blocks near the edge of the screen. If a block you created starts flashing, this means it is close to that limit.

extra_info.name: Overlapping Blocks
extra_info.description: If you use this move in a spot where you've already placed a block, the old block will be destroyed and you can place a new one.

extra_info.name: Destroying Blocks
extra_info.description: As time passes, blocks placed with Create Block will eventually break. Being hit by attacks, stood on by fighters, or hit from below will cause the blocks to break faster.

extra_info.name: Mining Blocks
extra_info.description: It is possible to mine blocks you've placed, but this will only break the block and not yield any materials.

extra_info.name: Creating Blocks while Mining
extra_info.description: If you are mining and jump, you'll instead jump and place a block at your feet.

tips.name: Minecart
tips.description: Place rails and charge forward while riding in an iron minecart. If you jump, you'll leap out of the minecart, but it will continue forward. The first opponent caught in its path will be carried away.

extra_info.name: Stuck in the Minecart
extra_info.description: The more damage a fighter has taken, the longer they'll be carried along in the minecart. Trapped fighters can move the control stick back and forth to help escape the minecart faster.

extra_info.name: Using Minecart Repeatedly
extra_info.description: You can use the minecart repeatedly, but doing so will reduce the amount of time a fighter is trapped in it.

extra_info.name: Minecart on a Hill
extra_info.description: If the minecart tries to go uphill, it will slow down, stop, and even roll backward. However, if the minecart is going downhill, it will actually speed up!

extra_info.name: Minecart and Powered Rails
extra_info.description: If you've collected gold and redstone, you can place powered rails. This will increase the speed of the minecart and also allow it to go up hills!

extra_info.name: Charge with Minecart and Powered Rail
extra_info.description: Use the special-move button while riding the minecart to place powered rails and speed up. Don't forget that this only works if you've mined up redstone and gold.

extra_info.name: Reboarding the Minecart
extra_info.description: It's possible to get back in the minecart after you've jumped out, but you can do this only twice. Keep track of how many times you've jumped out!

extra_info.name: Minecart Controls
extra_info.description: Push left or right on the control stick to send the minecart rolling in that direction, placing rails along the way. Once you run out of materials and fall off the rails, the minecart will come to a stop.

extra_info.name: Materials for Rails
extra_info.description: In order to place rails, you'll need to collect the required materials. Easy-to-obtain materials, like dirt, will be used first. After that, you'll use wood, stone, and then iron.

extra_info.name: Destroying the Minecart
extra_info.description: The minecart won't last forever! After taking a certain amount of damage, the minecart will break. If Steve was the one who broke it, he'll be able to collect iron.

tips.name: Elytra
tips.description: Elytra will allow you to glide sideways. Tilt the stick up and down while gliding to adjust the angle and trajectory of the glide.

extra_info.name: Elytra and Firework Rocket
extra_info.description: At the beginning of the move, the firework rocket ignites to increase your speed. You can damage any opponents you hit while the speed increase is active.

extra_info.name: Cancel Elytra
extra_info.description: You can cancel Elytra midflight by dodging or attacking while gliding. You'll fall downward after doing so, but you'll be able to move left or right a little as you fall.

extra_info.name: Elytra's Time Limit
extra_info.description: After a certain period of time, the elytra will automatically fall off, and you'll fall downward. Be careful when this happens, as you'll be vulnerable to attacks as you fall.

extra_info.name: Crashing with Elytra
extra_info.description: While using the fireworks rocket, you won't crash into terrain and go into a free fall. However, once the rocket is out of fuel, hitting a ceiling or wall will send you falling, so be careful.

tips.name: TNT
tips.description: This move uses a lot of materials to create and place a powerful TNT block and a pressure plate. The block will explode after some time, if the block takes some damage, or if someone steps on the pressure plate.

extra_info.name: TNT Materials
extra_info.description: The easier to obtain materials will be used first when making the TNT block. While the power doesn't change depending on the materials, the block can be made with fewer rare materials.

extra_info.name: Remote TNT Detonation
extra_info.description: When on the ground, if you continue to hold the special-move button you can walk and place a redstone circuit. Release the button to create a pressure plate, which you can attack or step on to set off the TNT.

extra_info.name: Several TNT Blocks
extra_info.description: If you place your own TNT block next to another player's TNT block, stepping on the pressure plate will set off both blocks.

extra_info.name: TNT Explosion
extra_info.description: TNT doesn't see a difference between friend and foe. Stand too close, and the explosion from your own TNT will still hurt you! Be sure to place your pressure plate at a safe distance.

extra_info.name: TNT's Power
extra_info.description: Using a pressure plate to set off a TNT block will increase the amount of damage the explosion does compared to waiting a while or damaging the block.

tips.name: House of Boom
tips.description: Create a large piston to launch multiple opponents to the side. One unlucky foe will get trapped in a dark room—filled with enemies and explosives—which quickly explodes.
You can hold down the attack button to continuously swing the sword. You can also move while continuing to attack!

tips.name: Flint and Steel
tips.description: If you use this move near the edge, the fire you create will fall down, damaging opponents below or making it difficult for someone to get back to the stage.
Swing a sword in the air by jumping while pressing the attack button. Using neutral attack while jumping will leave you less vulnerable to enemy attacks.

tips.move: Forward Air Attack / Back Air Attack
tips.name: Pickaxe Attacks
tips.description: Your forward and back air attacks will make use of the pickaxe, and there is a small opportunity to cause a meteor effect while swinging the pickaxe in the forward air attack.

tips.name: Anvil
tips.description: Create an iron anvil and drop it to attack below you. The anvil will vanish after a short time, but you can stand on top of it until it does.

tips.name: Fishing Rod
tips.description: This move cannot be used in the air, but if you hook an opponent on the ground, you'll capture them in a fence.
The downward throw will slam an opponent into the ground, and if you have iron, this will set your opponent up perfectly for the anvil that gets dropped on them.
Sephiroth made his debut in FINAL FANTASY VII, released in 1997. He is a SOLDIER of the Shinra Electric Power Company, has phenomenal physical strength, and wields the long Masamune blade.
Sephiroth was thought of as a hero, due to being the best SOLDIER, and many young people aspired to emulate him. Following a certain incident, however, he decided to stand against Cloud as an enemy.

tips.name: Flare/Megaflare/Gigaflare
tips.description: Hold down the button to charge—up to three levels—and release to launch a fireball that eventually explodes. Charging for longer will reduce the range of the attack but increase the size of the explosion.

extra_info.name: Flare
extra_info.description: Press and release the button to shoot a large fireball in front of you, but be careful of enemy attacks while the move charges. The projectile you launch moves slowly but has great range and power.

extra_info.name: Gigaflare
extra_info.description: This version of the move has very short range, and charging it leaves you open to enemy attacks. However, the explosion is very large and the closer an enemy is to the center of the blast, the more damaging it is.

extra_info.name: Canceling the Charge
extra_info.description: You can cancel the charge by jumping, shielding, or dodging. However, you will lose any charge if you do so.

tips.name: Shadow Flare
tips.description: Launch a small, quick blast of energy. If the energy hits an opponent, it will surround them until it eventually comes together and explodes.

extra_info.name: Multiple Shadow Flares
extra_info.description: You can hit an opponent with up to five blasts from this attack. Do this with a combination of charging up the shot and hitting the opponent multiple times.

extra_info.name: Avoiding Shadow Flare
extra_info.description: With good timing, it is possible for a fighter to avoid the damage from Shadow Flare. This is done by shielding or dodging the moment the energy comes together and explodes.

extra_info.name: Reduced Shadow Flare
extra_info.description: The amount of energy that circles fighters will decrease when a third fighter is hit by this attack. Also, it will decrease if the attack is reflected or absorbed.

tips.name: Blade Dash
tips.description: Choose a direction at the start of the move, then slash at high speed in that direction. This can also be useful in getting back to the stage if you've been launched!

extra_info.name: Octaslash
extra_info.description: Hold down the button and unleash Octaslash, which triggers eight rapid sword slashes in a row. This move is very powerful but will leave you open to enemy attacks at the end.

tips.name: Scintilla
tips.description: Create a barrier in front of you, which will transform into Scintilla. While active, the barrier will block direct attacks or projectiles that strike it.

extra_info.name: Scintilla Characteristics
extra_info.description: Unlike other counters, the barrier of light you create will become Scintilla even if no one hits it. However, Scintilla can be absorbed or reflected by your opponents!

extra_info.name: Scintilla's Power
extra_info.description: The barrier of light immediately becomes Scintilla if it is attacked. Scintilla is more powerful if the barrier absorbs a lot of damage, but there is a limit—if the damage is too great, the barrier will break.

tips.name: Supernova
tips.description: Slash a wide area in front of you, hitting up to three opponents. The slash is followed up with a powerful supernova explosion that deals significant damage and sometimes inflicts status effects.

extra_info.name: Effects of Supernova
extra_info.description: Foes hit by Supernova may be affected by one of the following status effects: dizzy, sleep, reversed controls, slow, or a flower bloom. Anyone hit while off the stage won't be dizzy, asleep, or have reversed controls.

tips.description: When you are at a disadvantage, such as having more damage than your opponent, a wing can grow from your back. If this happens, your power and movement speed will increase and you can jump twice in midair.

The greater the disadvantage you're at, such as in damage, score, or stock, the easier it will be for the wing to grow. Likewise, it will be more difficult to grow the wing when you're at an advantage.

The middle part of the blade is the strongest when it comes to slashing attacks. For stabbing attacks, the tip of the blade is the strongest. This is especially true at the moment you stick the blade out.
Swing your blade in a far-reaching attack. You can choose for the move to swing upward or downward by inputting up or down at the start of the attack.
Stab upward with the tip of the blade. This move has long range, but it is difficult to hit enemies who aren't directly above you. However, the upward stab can catch opponents in front of you.

Stick the blade out diagonally and downward. If it strikes the ground and a cloud of dust rises, you'll deal increased damage. The dust cloud will also weaken enemy shields, making it strong against defensive foes.
Stab forward with the sword. Use this attack to keep your opponents at bay for a while.
If this attack stabs into a wall, you can hang out there for a little while. It's also possible to jump in this situation!
Stab the blade directly downward, and unleash Hell's Gate. This move has a meteor effect at the beginning of the attack.
Pyra and Mythra made their debut in Xenoblade Chronicles 2, released in 2017. They are known as Blades, living weapons, and are specifically of a type called Aegis.

Pyra and Mythra are the same Blade, but they have their own identities, each with a different look and personality. Mythra is the original persona, but for her own reasons, she sealed herself up and created Pyra.

Rex is the protagonist of Xenoblade Chronicles 2. He is a Driver who forged a bond with Pyra and Mythra. In this title he appears in Mythra and Pyra's Final Smash and joins them on the victory screen.

Pyra has more power between the two. Her side smash has especially high launch power. Mythra is much faster, though, and if you time your dodges well, you can execute effective counterattacks against your foes.

tips.name: Pyra/Mythra: Swap to Pyra / Swap to Mythra
tips.description: You can swap between Pyra or Mythra at any time during the battle. You'll be briefly invincible as you swap, but afterward you'll be vulnerable to enemy attacks.

tips.name: Pyra: Flame Nova
tips.description: Cause the sword to revolve around you, launching nearby opponents. Charge up the attack by holding down the button, increasing the power of the sword and how many times it swings. The charge can't be stored.

extra_info.name: Pyra: Flame Nova and Moving
extra_info.description: While the sword is revolving, tilt the stick left or right to move slightly forward or backward without canceling the sword's movement.

extra_info.name: Mythra: Lightning Buster
extra_info.description: Attack first with a diagonal blow, then follow up with three wide-range slashes. Charge up the attack to slash four times as well as to increase the range and power. However, this charge cannot be stored.

extra_info.name: Mythra: Invincibility during Lightning Buster
extra_info.description: You'll be invincible during the initial diagonal slash of this move. If you time it right, you can counterattack unopposed.

extra_info.name: Mythra: Turning during Lightning Buster
extra_info.description: If you tilt the stick behind you before unleashing the first swing, you can turn and attack in that direction. Just watch out for anyone trying to hit you when your back is turned!

extra_info.name: Mythra: Lightning Buster and Enemies behind You
extra_info.description: If there is an opponent directly behind you when you perform the first slash, the follow-up spinning slashes are able to hit them and launch them forward.

tips.name: Pyra: Blazing End
tips.description: Throw the sword forward, slashing in that direction. The sword will spin briefly at its destination and then return to you. You're able to freely move when this happens, but you can't attack until the sword returns.

extra_info.name: Pyra: Blazing End and Flicking the Stick
extra_info.description: Flick the control stick to extend the distance the sword flies. This will increase the time it takes for the sword to return to you, so be careful not to be without your sword when you need it most.

extra_info.name: Pyra: Beginning of Blazing End
extra_info.description: The initial attack will swing right in front of you in an upward motion. If it hits an opponent, it will launch them and make it easy for the sword to hit them. Flick the stick to increase the damage and launch power!

extra_info.name: Pyra: Blazing End near a Wall
extra_info.description: If you use this move too close to a wall, the sword will hit the wall and return to you without spinning.

extra_info.name: Mythra: Photon Edge
extra_info.description: Charge forward and jump around, quickly slashing five times. You can strike in a very wide range with this attack, but you're still vulnerable to enemy projectiles, so be careful.

extra_info.name: Mythra: Photon Edge and Stage Edges
extra_info.description: The forward charge of this move is useful if you're trying to return to the stage. However, if you use it while standing on an edge, you can accidentally hurl yourself off the stage. Whoops!

tips.name: Pyra: Prominence Revolt
tips.description: Leap high into the air and stab downward as you fall. You'll have a meteor effect while falling, and when you land you'll create a large pillar of fire in front of you.

extra_info.name: Pyra: Moving during Prominence Revolt
extra_info.description: Tilt the stick left or right during the jump to move a bit forward or backward.

extra_info.name: Mythra: Ray of Punishment
extra_info.description: Slash upward and jump into the air. After the jump, fire a shot of light diagonally downward. If this shot hits an opponent or the ground, it will explode.

extra_info.name: Mythra: Chroma Dust
extra_info.description: If you press the special-move button after the jump but before the single shot, you'll fire a scatter shot. It has a wide range and launches foes sideways—perfect for defending the sides of the stage.

extra_info.name: Mythra: Chroma Dust in the Original Game
extra_info.description: Originally in Xenoblade Chronicles 2: Torna - The Golden Country, this move was a powerful special ability you could use in a pinch.

tips.name: Pyra: Burning Sword
tips.description: Rex can hit multiple opponents with a huge pillar of fire, launching them away. This Final Smash deals less damage than Mythra's, but it has higher launch power and can more easily KO opponents.

extra_info.name: Mythra: Sacred Arrow
extra_info.description: Rain down arrows from above at the opponent Rex strikes. This Final Smash has less launch power than Pyra's, but it deals high continuous damage in a wide area.

tips.name: Pyra: Flare Smash
tips.description: Pyra's side smash deals serious damage and has high launch power. The power of this attack is unique to Pyra, so go on the offense and try to land an early KO with this attack!
If an opponent lands an attack right before you dodge, you'll take less damage and apply a slowing effect to them. Use your faster movement to launch a counterattack!

The third strike of the neutral attack will differ between Pyra and Mythra. Pyra will have greater launch power, while Mythra will launch opponents upward instead, setting them up for follow-up attacks.

Hit an opponent in front of you to launch them upward. The attack hits in a wide area above but leaves you vulnerable otherwise. Pyra can launch foes with one hit, while Mythra needs a few to send them flying.

Strike both forward and backward with your sword and your leg. The sword blow will deal more damage than the kick. This attack covers a wide area in the front and back but leaves you vulnerable above.
Pyra will launch opponents with impressive force, while Mythra will continuously attack and make it easy to follow up. Both attacks hit a wide area but will leave you vulnerable upon landing.
When Pyra strikes opponents below her, she'll cause a meteor effect. Mythra won't trigger a meteor effect, but she is also less vulnerable when using this move.
Kazuya made his debut in the 1994 arcade game Tekken. This was a fighting game that used 3D polygons, which was a rare thing back then. The game was known for its unique characters.

Kazuya is one of the main characters in the Tekken series. He's fought against his own son, Jin Kazama, and his own father, Heihachi Mishima. Thanks to the Devil Gene, he is able to transform into a devil.

tips.name: Devil Blaster
tips.description: When on the ground, unleash a powerful beam ahead of you. In the air, the beam will angle downward. This attack has long range and can hit up to three fighters, but it loses power with each hit.

extra_info.name: Aiming the Devil Blaster
extra_info.description: You can angle the beam up or down by inputting up or down with the control stick right before the attack starts. Shots angled downward may hit shorter opponents, but the power will be reduced.

tips.name: Devil Fist
tips.description: The leading fist of this attack will pass through the opponent it hits. If they're hit at the start of the attack, they'll be rendered immobile and be wide open for a follow-up attack!

extra_info.name: Devil Fist in the Air
extra_info.description: The charge of this attack travels a shorter distance in the air than it does on the ground. You'll stop at edges when on the ground, though, but not if you're airborne. Be careful not to throw yourself off the stage!

extra_info.name: Devil Fist Invincibility and Vulnerability
extra_info.description: When used on the ground, you'll have a brief moment of invincibility at the start of the move. You'll be vulnerable afterward though, so use this move when a hit is a sure thing!

tips.name: Devil Wings
tips.description: You can shift a little to the left or right by pressing left or right on the control stick as you rise. Also, you can attack opponents with the claws on the wings!

extra_info.name: After Using Devil Wings
extra_info.description: After a little time passes, you can use most other moves after using this one. The only exception is the side special—you'll have to land before you can use that.

tips.name: Heaven's Door
tips.description: Deal serious damage to your opponent by grabbing them and then slamming them to the ground. You can ignore shields, but if you miss, you'll be wide open!

extra_info.name: Heaven's Door's Super Armor
extra_info.description: This move will give you super armor right at the start. You can try and surprise an attacking opponent by starting this move at the same time, but watch out—the move triggers slowly.

extra_info.name: Accumulated Damage and Heaven's Door
extra_info.description: When you're in the air after grabbing someone with this move, you can shift them left or right. The more damage they've taken, the more you'll be able to shift around!

extra_info.name: Enemies Near Heaven's Door
extra_info.description: If there are opponents near where you slam someone down, you can also launch them with the impact.

tips.name: Final Blaster
tips.description: Unleash a far-reaching beam from your forehead. If that beam hits a foe, more beams from your wings and chest will fire together. These beams will cover a wide range in front of you, possibly hitting more opponents.
If your damage is at 100% or more, or if you've got 1/4 or less of your stamina remaining in Stamina mode, you will Rage, increasing your power.
While affected by Rage, a red aura will surround you. Additionally, the fighter name near the accumulated damage number will flash red.

While in a Rage and on the ground, press → ↓ ↘ + either hold down the attack or special-move buttons, perform a down special, or grab, and you'll unleash this move. Use command input for the strongest version.
This move is very powerful and has few downsides—in fact, you can disregard an opponent's shield! However, if you miss, you'll quickly drop out of Rage. Use it well!

Your Rage will end when you hit an opponent with Rage Drive. Rage may also end if you miss with Rage Drive or take damage too many times. Once it ends, you'll have to get KO'd and come back to Rage again.

tips.name: Glorious Demon God Fist
tips.description: A solid hit from this move can be the single most damaging ability Kazuya has. It leaves you exposed, but it can send foes flying far to the side. It's perfect for multiple-foe battles!

tips.name: Lion Slayer
tips.description: The hand thrown downward will have a meteor effect. If it hits someone on the ground, it'll bounce them upward, making it easy to continue a combo.
All of Kazuya's smash attacks have super armor at the beginning of the move. His side and down smash attacks are great at weakening an opponent's shields, too.

While on the ground, combine directional inputs and the attack button, for example ↓ + attack button or → → + attack button, in order to use command-input moves. These moves are stronger than the normal ones.

tips.name: 10 Hit Combo
tips.description: Repeatedly press the attack button quickly, or hold it down, to pull off a combo of up to 10 hits. The last attack will ignore an opponent's shield.

extra_info.name: Flash Punch Combo
extra_info.description: After using two neutral attacks, wait just a moment before the third neutral attack in order to pull off this move. The attack power for this move is high and useful in dealing some quick damage.

extra_info.name: Left Splits Kick
extra_info.description: When facing right, you can quickly input → → + attack button to use this move. It has great power and won't leave you vulnerable to attacks. It can also reflect projectiles!

extra_info.name: Twin Pistons
extra_info.description: Unleash an uppercut with ↑ + attack button, and then press the attack button again to use Twin Pistons. Your upper half will be invincible during the move, so it's a powerful counter for attacks from the air.

extra_info.name: Using Twin Pistons
extra_info.description: If you stop at the first move, you will launch opponents upward, making them easier to hit with a follow-up attack. If you continue into the second move, you can launch opponents even higher.

extra_info.name: Roundhouse to Triple Spin Kicks
extra_info.description: Input ↗ + attack button four times while facing right to use the move. If the first attack hits, you'll keep hitting for serious damage. You'll hop slightly at the start, avoiding low attacks and going for a counter.

extra_info.name: Oni Front Kick
extra_info.description: Use → + attack button for this move. Since this move launches foes sideways, try to use it near the edge. It's an easy KO against foes that have trouble getting back to the stage.

extra_info.name: Tsunami Kick
extra_info.description: While facing right, lift an opponent off the ground with ↘ + attack button, then launch them upward by pressing the attack button again. The first attack sets up a combo, the second weakens shields.

extra_info.name: Nejiri Uraken
extra_info.description: You can use the move with ↓ + attack button. Toward the end of the move, you will be invincible while using the Uraken. If you press the shield button before using the Uraken, you can cancel the move.

extra_info.name: Stature Smash
extra_info.description: When facing right, this move is triggered by pressing ↙ + attack button. The reach is short, but the move leaves you less vulnerable. If it hits, you'll have a perfect opportunity for a follow-up attack.

extra_info.name: Flash Tornado
extra_info.description: When facing right, this move is triggered by pressing ← + attack button. The top half of your body will be invincible, and you can attack diagonally upward. Use this against enemies coming from above.

extra_info.name: Jump Side Kick
extra_info.description: You can use this move by pressing ↖ + attack button while at a distance from the opponents. It's hard for opponents to grab you while jumping, so this move is great against opponents planning to intercept you.

extra_info.name: Jump Side Kick Invincibility
extra_info.description: The lower half of your body is invincible at the start of this move. The leg that extends to kick will briefly be invincible, making this a great attack against opponents when attacking from above.

extra_info.name: Tombstone Crusher
extra_info.description: When facing right, this move is triggered by crouching and pressing ↘ + attack button. You'll be invincible for a while, and the attack launches foes in a low direction, so try and intercept airborne opponents.

extra_info.name: Crouch Jab
extra_info.description: When facing right, this move is triggered by crouching and pressing ↓ + attack button. It's a quick move and is easy to follow with Oni Front Kick, Wind God Fist, or Spinning Demon to Left Hook.

extra_info.name: Crouch Spin Kick
extra_info.description: When facing right, this move is triggered by crouching and pressing ↙ + attack button. You will attack from a low position, making it easier to dodge opponents that are aiming high.

extra_info.name: Demon God Fist
extra_info.description: You can use this move while moving from a crouch to standing by pressing the attack button. A hit will stun the opponent, but after the attack, you'll be left vulnerable. Don't miss!

extra_info.name: Gates of Hell
extra_info.description: When facing right, this move is triggered by pressing ↘ ↓ ↘ + grab. The reach of this attack is quite long. It's a tricky input, but pulling it off will launch an opponent way off to the side—perfect for KOs.

extra_info.name: Crouch Dash
extra_info.description: When facing right, this special dash is triggered by pressing → ↓ ↘. You're invincible right when you start moving, and your upper half is invincible until you stand. Use it to avoid attacks and quickly get in close.

extra_info.name: Movement after Crouch Dash
extra_info.description: Use Crouch Dash and then Spinning Demon to Left Hook or Wind God Fist to keep opponents at bay. If the opponent likes to shield, use Heaven's Door or Gates of Hell to grab them.

extra_info.name: Wind God Fist
extra_info.description: When facing right, this move is triggered by pressing → ↓ ↘ + attack button. You can quickly get into range with Crouch Dash. It's very powerful and makes it easy to follow up and combo after the hit.

extra_info.name: Electric Wind God Fist
extra_info.description: When facing right, this move is triggered by pressing ↘ and the attack button at the same time while using Wind God Fist. The attack is stronger and has longer invincibility. It will stun opponents if it hits.

extra_info.name: Electric Wind God Fist and Shields
extra_info.description: Unlike the Wind God Fist, the Electric Wind God Fist will push away opponents that shield, giving you some distance.

extra_info.name: Dragon Uppercut
extra_info.description: When facing right, this move is triggered by pressing → ↓ ↘ + holding the attack button. Your whole body is invincible at the start, and your upper half will be invincible while you uppercut. This is a slow, powerful move.

extra_info.name: Spinning Demon to Left Hook
extra_info.description: When facing right, this move is triggered by pressing → ↓ ↘ + special- move button. When you start moving, you'll briefly be invincible. Use this to launch opponents, especially if you're saving your Rage Drive.

tips.description: You can trigger this move by pressing the attack button while dashing. You can move forward while avoiding low attacks, but your back is completely exposed.
In 1-on-1 fights, you will automatically face toward your opponent. Keep that in mind when you're looking to use command-input moves!
While you have low damage, the weak attacks of opponents will not cause you to flinch. This makes it much easier to unleash combos.
Follow up on vulnerable opponents using moves like Stature Smash (↙ + attack button), Lion Slayer (down smash attack), or Roundhouse to Triple Spin Kicks (↗ + attack button four times).
Sora made his debut in KINGDOM HEARTS, released in 2002. After his homeland was consumed by darkness, Sora set off on an adventure to find Riku and Kairi, his childhood friends.
An energetic boy from Destiny Islands. He was chosen by the Keyblade and travels through many worlds, going on adventures related to the heart.
The Keyblade swings slowly, but the attacks can deal damage over a longer stretch of time, so it's easy to outhit others. However, you are very vulnerable before and after attacks as a result.

tips.name: Magic
tips.description: Each time you use your neutral special, it will cycle between three types: Firaga, Thundaga, and Blizzaga. Firaga can be shot multiple times, Thundaga has high damage power, and Blizzaga can freeze opponents.

extra_info.name: Firaga
extra_info.description: Press the button repeatedly to fire multiple shots. Thanks to its long range, this move is the best of the magic attacks for keeping your opponents at a distance.

extra_info.name: Thundaga
extra_info.description: Call down lighting[sic] in a wide range in front of you, sending anyone who gets hit flying upward. This move leaves you vulnerable before and after you use it, but hitting three times will deal some serious damage.

extra_info.name: Thundaga in the Air
extra_info.description: Use this move while you're in the air and lightning will come down closer together and from higher up. This does change the range of the move, though, so keep that in mind.

extra_info.name: Blizzaga
extra_info.description: These chilly shots will scatter and fly through the air. Opponents who are hit several times will freeze, and those who have taken a lot of damage will freeze for longer. Get close to make it really easy to hit!

tips.name: Sonic Blade Additional Inputs
tips.description: Tilt the control stick while charging forward to charge in that direction. You can affect the direction you charge up to two times.

extra_info.name: Charge Opponents With Sonic Blade
extra_info.description: If you press the special-move button when opponents are nearby, you will automatically charge toward the nearest foe. The attack is slightly stronger when charging using this method instead of the control stick.

extra_info.name: Sonic Blade Charge Speed
extra_info.description: Each time you charge, you'll lose a bit of speed. You'll also lose speed if you hit an opponent or they shield against your attack, so don't go charging into danger without a plan.

extra_info.name: Sonic Blade and Edges
extra_info.description: If you are offstage and use the special-move button to perform your last charge, you'll head toward the edge to try and recover.

tips.name: Shifting During Aerial Sweep
tips.description: As you rise up, you can shift left or right by inputting left or right on the control stick. Also, you can use Sonic Blade at the end of the move to evade foes or recover.

tips.name: Counterattack
tips.description: Deflect an opponent's direct attack and briefly stagger them, as well as negate the damage of projectiles! The stronger an attack, the more powerful the counter!

extra_info.name: Countering Behind You
extra_info.description: You can counter attacks that come from behind, but the area that will trigger the move is smaller, so it's harder to do than against attacks from the front.

tips.name: Sealing the Keyhole
tips.description: A Keyhole will appear in front and damage up to three nearby opponents. Any opponent with more than 100% damage will be instantly KO'd at the end of the move.

extra_info.name: Caught in Sealing the Keyhole
extra_info.description: The first beam you unleash will launch opponents toward the Keyhole. There's no limit to how many foes can be hit by this beam, but only the first three will get caught up in the attack that follows.

tips.description: Some of Sora's attacks can lead into combos of up to three hits. The neutral attack, side tilt attack, neutral air attack, and forward air attack can all be combos by holding down or repeatedly pressing the button.
On its own, the side tilt attack will have increased launch power. When used as part of a combo, it can do increased damage. Judge how best to use it in different situations.
Starting a combo with the neutral attack won't deal much damage, but it is quick. Starting with a side tilt attack will deal more damage, but it takes longer.
If you hit an enemy with the down tilt attack, they'll be launched slightly upward. This sets them up nicely for an up special move, or you could use an up tilt attack to start a combo.

Combos with the neutral air attack are easier to do without any left or right input. Combos with the forward air attack are easier to land by continuously tilting the control stick toward the opponent.
It's easier to land the first hit of an airborne combo using the neutral air attack, but combos from forward air attacks are a great way to lock down an opponent on the left or right.
This dash attack has a lot of reach and stays out for quite a while. Use it to close the distance with an opponent and bring the fight to them!
This up smash attack has great launch power, making it easy to KO foes upward. The start of the move also hits in front, so opponents there might just get caught in the attack too!
You have a brief period of intangibility at the start of this move. It's particularly strong against opponents who are using ledge attacks, or against opponents who are shielding.
Attack in all directions as you fall directly downward, but be careful using this when you're offstage; you might be able to save yourself with an up special or side special, but it'll be tough!
If you reduce the last opponent's health to zero in a Stamina Battle, something special will happen, similar to what happened in the original game!
